      "BEJSON_CRASH_COURSE-v21.md",
      "BEJSON_CRASH_COURSE-v21.md",
      "<!-- BEJSON + MFDB Crash Course | Document v20 | MFDB Spec v1.31 -->\n<!-- Format Creator: Elton Boehnen | https:/boehnenelton2024.pages.dev -->\n\n# BEJSON + MFDB Crash Course\n**Document Version 21 \u00b7 MFDB Spec Version 1.31 \u00b7 BEJSON Formats: 104 \u00b7 104a \u00b7 104db**  \nFormat Creator: Elton Boehnen \u00b7 https:/boehnenelton2024.pages.dev\n\n---\n\n\n\n# BEJSON Crash Course\n(v104, 104a, 104db)\nVERSION 1\n\nBEJSON is a strict, self-describing tabular data format built on JSON. A key feature is positional integrity, meaning the field order within Fields precisely matches the value order in each record found in Values. This design allows for index-based access (eliminating key lookups), rapid parsing, strong typing, and integrated schema validation without the need for external files.\n\nAll BEJSON versions mandate the presence of these top-level keys:\n\nJSON\nCOPY\n{\n  \"Format\": \"BEJSON\",\n  \"Format_Version\": \"104\" | \"104a\" | \"104db\",\n  \"Format_Creator\": \"Elton Boehnen\",\n  \"Records_Type\": [ ... ],\n  \"Fields\": [ ... ],\n  \"Values\": [ [ ... ], [ ... ], ... ]\n}\n\n\n---\n\n## Common Rules (All Versions)\n\nThe Fields key must contain an array of objects, structured as follows:\n{\"name\": string, \"type\": \"string\"|\"integer\"|\"number\"|\"boolean\"|\"array\"|\"object\" [, \"Record_Type_Parent\": string]}\n\n* Each record within Values must contain an exact number of elements matching the number of entries in Fields.\n* Optional or missing values should be represented by null to maintain length and position.\n* Duplicate field names are not permitted.\n* null is considered a valid value for any declared type.\n\n---\n\n## Version Differences\n\n### BEJSON 104 \u2013 Single Record Type, Full Types\n\n* Records_Type: An array containing exactly one string, which represents the entity name.\n* No custom top-level keys are allowed, beyond the six mandatory ones.\n* Exception: Parent_Hierarchy is an optional, built-in key permitted in 104. Its interpretation is intentionally flexible; applications can use it as needed (e.g., to indicate a folder path, an index hierarchy, or any logical grouping).\n* Supports complex data types, including array and object.\n* Ideal for homogeneous, high-throughput data such as logs, metrics, or archives.\n\nExample:\n\nJSON\nCOPY\n{\n  \"Format\": \"BEJSON\",\n  \"Format_Version\": \"104\",\n  \"Format_Creator\": \"Elton Boehnen\",\n  \"Records_Type\": [\"SensorReading\"],\n  \"Fields\": [\n    {\"name\": \"sensor_id\", \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"timestamp\", \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"temperature\", \"type\": \"number\"},\n    {\"name\": \"tags\", \"type\": \"array\"}\n  ],\n  \"Values\": [\n    [\"S001\", \"2026-01-10T12:00:00Z\", 23.5, [\"indoor\",\"ground\"]],\n    [\"S002\", \"2026-01-10T12:00:00Z\", 19.8, null]\n  ]\n}\n\n\n---\n\n### BEJSON 104a \u2013 Primitive Types + Custom Metadata\n\n* Records_Type: Must be exactly one string.\n* Custom top-level keys are allowed, but only for file-level metadata, never for per-record data.\n* Fields are restricted to primitive types only: string, integer, number, boolean.\n* Custom keys should adhere to PascalCase naming conventions and must not conflict with the six mandatory keys.\n* Best suited for configuration files, health checks, or simple log data.\n\nExample:\n\nJSON\nCOPY\n{\n  \"Format\": \"BEJSON\",\n  \"Format_Version\": \"104a\",\n  \"Format_Creator\": \"Elton Boehnen\",\n  \"Server_ID\": \"WEB-01\",\n  \"Environment\": \"Production\",\n  \"Retention_Days\": 90,\n  \"Records_Type\": [\"ConfigParam\"],\n  \"Fields\": [\n    {\"name\": \"key\", \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"value\", \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"sensitive\", \"type\": \"boolean\"}\n  ],\n  \"Values\": [\n    [\"db_host\", \"prod-db-01\", true],\n    [\"max_threads\", \"32\", false]\n  ]\n}\n\n\n---\n\n### BEJSON 104db \u2013 Multi-Entity Lightweight Database\n\n* Records_Type: An array containing two or more unique strings, each representing an entity name.\n* No custom top-level keys are allowed.\n* The very first field in the Fields array must be: {\"name\": \"Record_Type_Parent\", \"type\": \"string\"}.\n* The value at position 0 in every record within Values must precisely match one of the entries in Records_Type.\n* Every field (except Record_Type_Parent itself) must include a \"Record_Type_Parent\" property, assigning it to a specific entity. Fields lacking this assignment are invalid in 104db. There are no \"common fields\" shared across all entities.\n* Fields that are not applicable to a given entity must be set to null.\n* Supports complex data types.\n* Enables relationships through shared ID fields (acting as logical Primary Key/Foreign Key).\n* Convention: It is recommended practice to use the _fk suffix on foreign key fields (e.g., owner_user_id_fk) to indicate relationships to automated mapping tools. This is a convention and not strictly enforced by the BEJSON specification.\n\nExample:\n\nJSON\nCOPY\n{\n  \"Format\": \"BEJSON\",\n  \"Format_Version\": \"104db\",\n  \"Format_Creator\": \"Elton Boehnen\",\n  \"Records_Type\": [\"User\", \"Item\"],\n  \"Fields\": [\n    {\"name\": \"Record_Type_Parent\", \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"created\", \"type\": \"string\", \"Record_Type_Parent\": \"User\"},\n    {\"name\": \"user_id\", \"type\": \"string\", \"Record_Type_Parent\": \"User\"},\n    {\"name\": \"username\", \"type\": \"string\", \"Record_Type_Parent\": \"User\"},\n    {\"name\": \"created_at\", \"type\": \"string\", \"Record_Type_Parent\": \"Item\"},\n    {\"name\": \"item_id\", \"type\": \"string\", \"Record_Type_Parent\": \"Item\"},\n    {\"name\": \"name\", \"type\": \"string\", \"Record_Type_Parent\": \"Item\"},\n    {\"name\": \"owner_user_id_fk\", \"type\": \"string\", \"Record_Type_Parent\": \"Item\"}\n  ],\n  \"Values\": [\n    [\"User\", \"2026-01-01\", \"U01\", \"alice\", null, null, null, null],\n    [\"User\", \"2026-01-02\", \"U02\", \"bob\",   null, null, null, null],\n    [\"Item\", null, null, null, \"2026-01-10\", \"I01\", \"Report A\", \"U01\"],\n    [\"Item\", null, null, null, \"2026-01-10\", \"I02\", \"Report B\", \"U02\"]\n  ]\n}\n\n\nNote: Because the \"created\" field is exclusively assigned to \"User\", \"Item\" records must set it to null. Conversely, \"User\" records must null out \"created_at\". If a timestamp field is desired across multiple entities, it must be defined separately for each entity with consistent naming, or the duplication must be accepted. Each field is strictly owned by one entity.\n\n\n### In certain cases 104db might be superior to SQL lite:\n- When you need a relational database that doesn't need to hold 10,000 records or more. \n- When you want an LLM to be able to directly read the database and modify it as if it were a text file. \n\n### Limitations\n- Due to the requirement of holding positional integrity and using null padding, the 104db file grows exponentially with each new field and each new record and at a certain point comes unviable, and that the best usage for 104DB is for limited size, single file quick access to relational data that is readable by any programming language natively and to AI.\n\n---\n\n## Validation Summary\n\nA BEJSON document is considered valid if it adheres to the following criteria:\n\n* Valid JSON syntax.\n* All six mandatory top-level keys are present with their correct values.\n* Format_Creator must be precisely \"Elton Boehnen\".\n* The Fields structure is correct, and no duplicate field names exist.\n* The length of every record in Values equals the length of the Fields array.\n* The type of each value matches its declared type in Fields (with null always being an allowed value).\n* Version-specific rules:\n* 104: Requires a single record type, allows complex types, and forbids custom headers (except for Parent_Hierarchy).\n* 104a: Requires a single record type, allows only primitive types, and permits custom headers for file-level metadata (these must be PascalCase and not conflict with the six mandatory keys).\n* 104db: Requires two or more record types, mandates that every field (except the Record_Type_Parent discriminator field itself) has a Record_Type_Parent assignment, forbids custom headers, and requires null for non-applicable fields.\n\n---\n\n## Best Practices\n\n* Use snake_case for field names.\n* Use PascalCase for custom headers in BEJSON 104a.\n* Append new fields only to the end of the Fields array to preserve positional integrity for existing parsers.\n* Use null (rather than an empty array or object) for truly missing data, unless an empty state carries specific meaning.\n* Manage your application schema version independently from Format_Version:\n* For 104a / 104db: Use a custom header like Schema_Version (e.g., \"v1.0\").\n* For 104: Custom headers are forbidden; embed the version within the Records_Type string (e.g., [\"SensorReading_v1_0\"]) or manage it externally.\n* Schema changes: Appending fields is generally safe (minor change). Removing or retyping a field constitutes a breaking change (major change).\n* For large datasets, consider splitting data into multiple complete BEJSON files.\n* Encrypt sensitive files both at rest and during transit.\n* In 104db, use the _fk suffix on foreign key fields (this is a convention, not enforced by the specification).\n* The Event/Audit entity pattern: In 104db, define a dedicated \"Event\" entity within Records_Type to implement audit trails. Link it to other entities using fields like related_entity_id_fk, and store before/after state in a change_details field (of type object).\n\n---\n\n## Summary of Version Capabilities\n\n| Feature | BEJSON 104 | BEJSON 104a | BEJSON 104db |\n| :---------------- | :---------------------- | :------------------------ | :---------------------- |\n| Primary Use | High-throughput Logs | Configs, Metrics | Multi-Entity Database |\n| Record Types | Single (1) | Single (1) | Multiple (2+) |\n| Custom Headers | \u274c Forbidden\\* | \u2705 Allowed (File Meta) | \u274c Forbidden |\n| Data Types | Complex (Array/Object) | Primitives Only | Complex (Array/Object) |\n| Discriminator | N/A | N/A | Record_Type_Parent |\n\n\\* Parent_Hierarchy is an optional, built-in exception for BEJSON 104.\n\n---\n\nThis document comprehensively covers all essential rules and provides examples necessary to fully understand, generate, validate, and effectively use any BEJSON variant.\n\nhttps:/boehnenelton2024.pages.dev\nDBG\n\nMFDB Crash Course\n(Multifile Database)\nVERSION 1\n\nMFDB is a database architecture that layers on top of BEJSON. It is not a new\nBEJSON format version \u2014 it orchestrates existing BEJSON formats (104 and 104a)\ninto a structured multi-file database system. Every file in an MFDB is a fully\nvalid standalone BEJSON document. MFDB adds meaning through file naming, a key\nfield (Parent_Hierarchy), and a manifest registry.\n\nCore concept: one BEJSON 104a file (the manifest) acts as a registry for any\nnumber of BEJSON 104 files (entity files). Each entity file holds records for\nexactly one entity type. The manifest and its entity files together form one\ncomplete MFDB database.\n\n---\n\nArchitecture\n\nAn MFDB database on disk:\n\n  mydb/\n    104a.mfdb.bejson        \u2190 manifest (always at the root)\n    data/\n      user.bejson           \u2190 entity file (BEJSON 104)\n      order.bejson          \u2190 entity file (BEJSON 104)\n      product.bejson        \u2190 entity file (BEJSON 104)\n\nThe manifest filename is fixed: 104a.mfdb.bejson\nEntity files default to data/<entity_name_lowercase>.bejson\nThe data/ subdirectory is the standard default. Any relative path is allowed\nas long as it is recorded accurately in the manifest and the Parent_Hierarchy\nresolves correctly. Relative paths must not escape the database root.\n\n---\n\nThe Manifest File\n\nThe manifest is a BEJSON 104a document. All 104a rules apply.\n\nFixed values required by MFDB:\n  Records_Type must be exactly: [\"mfdb\"]\n  Format_Version must be: \"104a\"\n  Format_Creator must be: \"Elton Boehnen\"\n\nStandard custom headers (PascalCase, per 104a rules):\n  MFDB_Version    string   MFDB spec version. Current value: \"1.31\". Required.\n  DB_Name         string   Human-readable database name. Required.\n  DB_Description  string   Short description of the database. Optional.\n  Schema_Version  string   Application schema version (semver recommended). Optional.\n  Author          string   Author name. Optional.\n  Created_At      string   ISO 8601 UTC creation timestamp. Optional.\n  Network_Role    string   Federation role: \"Master\" or \"Slave\". Optional (v1.31).\n\nCustom headers must not collide with any of the 6 mandatory BEJSON keys.\nMFDB_Version and DB_Name are treated as required standard headers.\n\nFields \u2014 required:\n  entity_name    string   Unique name of the entity (e.g. \"User\"). Cannot be null.\n  file_path      string   Relative path from the manifest directory to the entity\n                          file (e.g. \"data/user.bejson\"). Cannot be null. Must be\n                          unique across all records.\n\nFields \u2014 standard optional (include all if present, always in this order):\n  description    string   Human-readable description of the entity.\n  record_count   integer  Advisory count of records in the entity file. May drift.\n                          Not enforced. Kept in sync by tooling, not the spec.\n  schema_version string   Entity-level schema version. Optional per entity.\n  primary_key    string   Field name of the entity's primary key (e.g. \"user_id\").\n                          Used by FK resolution tooling. Not enforced by the spec.\n\nNo other custom fields in the manifest Fields array are defined by MFDB 1.0.\nApplications may add fields beyond the standard set; they must follow BEJSON\n104a positional rules and must be documented at the application level.\n\nManifest record constraints:\n  entity_name must be unique across all records (no two entities share a name).\n  file_path must be unique across all records (one file per entity).\n  entity_name and file_path must not be null in any record.\n  Every file_path must resolve to a file that exists on disk.\n\nExample manifest:\n\n{\n  \"Format\": \"BEJSON\",\n  \"Format_Version\": \"104a\",\n  \"Format_Creator\": \"Elton Boehnen\",\n  \"MFDB_Version\": \"1.31\",\n  \"DB_Name\": \"StoreFront\",\n  \"DB_Description\": \"E-commerce product and order database\",\n  \"Schema_Version\": \"1.0.0\",\n  \"Author\": \"Elton Boehnen\",\n  \"Created_At\": \"2026-04-14T00:00:00Z\",\n  \"Records_Type\": [\"mfdb\"],\n  \"Fields\": [\n    {\"name\": \"entity_name\",    \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"file_path\",      \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"description\",    \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"record_count\",   \"type\": \"integer\"},\n    {\"name\": \"schema_version\", \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"primary_key\",    \"type\": \"string\"}\n  ],\n  \"Values\": [\n    [\"User\",    \"data/user.bejson\",    \"Registered users\",   3, \"1.0\", \"user_id\"],\n    [\"Order\",   \"data/order.bejson\",   \"Customer orders\",    5, \"1.0\", \"order_id\"],\n    [\"Product\", \"data/product.bejson\", \"Product catalogue\",  8, \"1.0\", \"product_id\"]\n  ]\n}\n\n---\n\nEntity Files\n\nEach entity file is a BEJSON 104 document. All BEJSON 104 rules apply.\nThe Parent_Hierarchy built-in exception is mandatory in MFDB.\n\nFixed values required by MFDB:\n  Format_Version must be: \"104\"\n  Format_Creator must be: \"Elton Boehnen\"\n  Records_Type must contain exactly one string \u2014 the entity name \u2014 and it must\n    match the entity_name registered for this file in the manifest exactly\n    (case-sensitive).\n  Parent_Hierarchy must be present and must be a relative path from the entity\n    file's own directory back to the manifest file. This value enables tools to\n    discover the owning manifest without any external configuration.\n\nParent_Hierarchy is a relative path, not an absolute path.\n  Entity in data/user.bejson pointing to root manifest: \"../104a.mfdb.bejson\"\n  Entity at root level pointing to root manifest: \"104a.mfdb.bejson\"\n  Parent_Hierarchy is always from the entity file's directory, never the DB root.\n\nRecords are dense. Unlike BEJSON 104db which null-pads fields across entity\ntypes in a single file, MFDB uses one file per entity type. Records inside an\nentity file should have no structurally-motivated null padding. A null value in\nan entity record means the data is genuinely absent, not that the field belongs\nto a different entity.\n\nBEJSON 104 allows complex types (array, object). These are valid in entity files.\nNo custom top-level keys. Parent_Hierarchy is the only built-in exception key.\n\nExample entity file (data/user.bejson):\n\n{\n  \"Format\": \"BEJSON\",\n  \"Format_Version\": \"104\",\n  \"Format_Creator\": \"Elton Boehnen\",\n  \"Parent_Hierarchy\": \"../104a.mfdb.bejson\",\n  \"Records_Type\": [\"User\"],\n  \"Fields\": [\n    {\"name\": \"user_id\",    \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"username\",   \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"email\",      \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"created_at\", \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"active\",     \"type\": \"boolean\"}\n  ],\n  \"Values\": [\n    [\"U01\", \"alice\", \"alice@example.com\", \"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z\", true],\n    [\"U02\", \"bob\",   \"bob@example.com\",   \"2026-01-02T00:00:00Z\", true],\n    [\"U03\", \"carol\", \"carol@example.com\", \"2026-01-05T00:00:00Z\", false]\n  ]\n}\n\nExample entity file (data/order.bejson):\n\n{\n  \"Format\": \"BEJSON\",\n  \"Format_Version\": \"104\",\n  \"Format_Creator\": \"Elton Boehnen\",\n  \"Parent_Hierarchy\": \"../104a.mfdb.bejson\",\n  \"Records_Type\": [\"Order\"],\n  \"Fields\": [\n    {\"name\": \"order_id\",     \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"user_id_fk\",   \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"total\",        \"type\": \"number\"},\n    {\"name\": \"placed_at\",    \"type\": \"string\"},\n    {\"name\": \"items\",        \"type\": \"array\"}\n  ],\n  \"Values\": [\n    [\"ORD01\", \"U01\", 49.99,  \"2026-02-01T10:00:00Z\", [\"P01\",\"P03\"]],\n    [\"ORD02\", \"U02\", 120.00, \"2026-02-03T14:30:00Z\", [\"P02\"]],\n    [\"ORD03\", \"U01\", 9.99,   \"2026-02-10T09:15:00Z\", [\"P04\"]]\n  ]\n}\n\n---\n\nDiscovery Algorithm\n\nA tool encountering any .bejson file can identify its MFDB role:\n\n  1. Attempt to parse the file as JSON. If it fails: not MFDB-related.\n  2. Read Format_Version and the filename.\n  3. If Format_Version == \"104a\" AND filename ends in \".mfdb.bejson\":\n       \u2192 This is a manifest.\n  4. If Format_Version == \"104\" AND \"Parent_Hierarchy\" key is present:\n       \u2192 This is an entity file.\n  5. Otherwise:\n       \u2192 Standalone BEJSON (not part of any MFDB).\n\nThe discovery algorithm is file-local. No directory scanning required.\nAn entity file with Parent_Hierarchy present but pointing to a non-existent\nmanifest is an orphan \u2014 it is invalid as MFDB even though it is valid BEJSON 104.\n\n---\n\nRelationships and Foreign Keys\n\nMFDB does not enforce referential integrity. FK relationships are declared by\nconvention and surfaced to tooling via two mechanisms:\n\n  1. Field naming \u2014 FK fields end in _fk (e.g. user_id_fk, product_id_fk).\n     This signals to tools that the field references a primary key elsewhere.\n\n  2. primary_key declaration in the manifest \u2014 the primary_key field for an\n     entity names the field that other entities' _fk fields reference.\n\nResolution rule for tooling (not enforced by spec):\n  A field named X_id_fk in entity A is assumed to reference the entity whose\n  primary_key in the manifest is X_id, or whose entity_name lowercased appears\n  in the FK field name. Tooling should treat unresolved FKs as warnings only.\n\nFK values in records must satisfy the declared type (typically string or integer).\nnull is valid for any FK field \u2014 it represents no relationship.\n\nJoins between entity files are performed by the application or tooling layer.\nMFDB provides no join syntax \u2014 it provides the structural signals (_fk suffix,\nprimary_key declaration) for tools to perform joins at runtime.\n\n---\n\nValidation Rules\n\nLevel 1 \u2014 Manifest validation:\n  File exists and is valid BEJSON 104a.\n  Records_Type is exactly [\"mfdb\"].\n  Fields include entity_name and file_path.\n  No record has null entity_name or null file_path.\n  entity_name values are unique across all records.\n  file_path values are unique across all records.\n  Every file_path resolves to an existing file on disk.\n\nLevel 2 \u2014 Entity file validation (per entity):\n  File is valid BEJSON 104.\n  Parent_Hierarchy key is present.\n  Parent_Hierarchy resolves to an existing manifest file.\n  Records_Type contains exactly one string.\n  That string matches the entity_name in the manifest record for this file.\n  Manifest file path to this entity matches this entity's actual path\n    (bidirectional check \u2014 manifest \u2192 entity and entity \u2192 manifest agree).\n\nLevel 3 \u2014 Database-level validation:\n  All Level 1 and Level 2 checks pass for all entities.\n  record_count mismatch between manifest and actual file row count: warning only.\n  FK fields with no resolvable primary_key target: warning only (strict mode).\n\nErrors are hard failures. Warnings are advisory and do not invalidate the database.\n\n---\n\nError Code Ranges (standard)\n\n  1\u201315    BEJSON Validator errors (BEJSONValidationError)\n  20\u201327   BEJSON Core errors (BEJSONCoreError)\n  30\u201349   MFDB Validator errors (MFDBValidationError)\n  50\u201369   MFDB Core errors (MFDBCoreError)\n\nMFDB Validator error codes (30\u201349):\n  30  Not a manifest\n  31  Not an entity file\n  32  Manifest Records_Type invalid\n  33  Entity file not found (path resolution)\n  34  Entity name mismatch (Records_Type vs manifest)\n  35  Duplicate entry (entity_name or file_path)\n  36  Missing Parent_Hierarchy\n  37  Manifest file not found\n  38  Bidirectional path check failed\n  39  FK unresolved (strict mode)\n  40  Missing required field in manifest (entity_name or file_path)\n  41  Null in required field (entity_name or file_path)\n\n---\n\n---\n\nValidation vs. Standardization (v1.31 Distinction)\n\nMFDB v1.31 enforces a strict conceptual separation between structural validity \nand architectural standardization.\n\n1. Validation (Structural Correctness):\n   - Scope: \"Is the file a valid BEJSON/MFDB document?\"\n   - Enforced by: lib_mfdb_validator.py.\n   - Criteria: Proper JSON syntax, mandatory BEJSON keys present, positional \n     integrity (Values match Fields length), Parent_Hierarchy resolving, and \n     bidirectional path verification between manifest and entity.\n   - Failure result: Hard Error (Database is considered \"Broken\").\n\n2. Standardization (Architectural Compliance):\n   - Scope: \"Does the database follow the official MFDB conventions?\"\n   - Enforced by: Standard-aware tooling and architectural audits.\n   - Criteria: Naming conventions (snake_case fields, PascalCase entities), \n     Key standards (_id PKs and _fk foreign keys), and Federation Protocols \n     (Master/Slave headers and registry presence).\n   - Failure result: Non-Standard Warning (Database is \"Valid but Non-Standard\").\n\n---\n\nStandardization Protocols (Architectural Conventions)\n\nNaming Conventions:\n  Manifest filename:    104a.mfdb.bejson (Invariant)\n  Entity filenames:     <entity_name_lowercase>.bejson\n                        Multi-word entities: snake_case (e.g. order_item.bejson)\n  Entity field names:   snake_case (inherited from BEJSON best practices)\n  Primary key fields:   <entity_name_lower>_id (e.g. user_id, order_id)\n  Foreign key fields:   <target_pk_field>_fk (e.g. user_id_fk, product_id_fk)\n  Entity names:         PascalCase string (e.g. \"User\", \"OrderItem\")\n  DB_Name header:       Human-readable string, no format restriction\n  Schema_Version:       Semver recommended (e.g. \"1.0.0\")\n  MFDB_Version:         \"1.31\" for the current specification\n\nFederation Protocols:\n  MFDB v1.31 standardizes distributed database roles via specific markers.\n  1. Network_Role Header: \"Master\" (Authoritative) or \"Slave\" (Replica).\n  2. ConnectedSlave Registry: A standardized entity in Global/Master nodes to \n     track network topology (label, url, role, status, supported_entities).\n\n---\n\nValidation Rules (Structural Requirements)\n\n  - Keep the manifest at the database root. Do not nest it inside subdirectories.\n  - Entity files default to the data/ subdirectory. Flat root placement is allowed\n    but not recommended for databases with many entities.\n  - Always write files atomically (write to temp, rename). Partial writes corrupt\n    the database. This is especially critical for the manifest.\n  - After any write operation that changes row count, sync record_count in the\n    manifest. Treat it as advisory \u2014 never rely on it for correctness in reads.\n  - Add new fields only at the end of an entity's Fields array. Inserting or\n    removing fields mid-array breaks positional integrity for all existing records.\n  - Schema changes: appending a field is a minor (safe) change. Removing,\n    retyping, or reordering fields is a breaking (major) change. Increment\n    Schema_Version accordingly.\n  - Use null for genuinely absent values. Do not use empty string as a null\n    substitute. Do not use empty array or empty object for missing scalar data.\n  - Use PK/FK naming conventions even if you do not use relational tooling.\n    It costs nothing and enables future tooling.\n  - Do not store sensitive data in plaintext. Encrypt at rest and in transit.\n  - For large entities (thousands of records), consider splitting into multiple\n    MFDB databases rather than a single entity file with unbounded growth.\n  - An MFDB export package (zip) should include the manifest at the root and all\n    entity files at their declared relative paths. The zip should be extractable\n    and immediately valid without path adjustment.\n  - Validate bidirectionally on load, not just on create. File moves break\n    Parent_Hierarchy silently.\n  - Do not allow two manifest files in a single database directory. One manifest\n    per database is the invariant.\n\n---\n\nWhat MFDB Is Not\n\n  - MFDB is not a new BEJSON format version. There is no Format_Version \"mfdb\".\n  - MFDB is not a query language or a runtime database engine. It is a file\n    organization and naming convention with a manifest registry.\n  - MFDB does not replace BEJSON 104db. 104db puts multiple entity types in one\n    file with null-padding. MFDB puts each entity type in its own file with no\n    padding. Choose based on use case: 104db for small, tightly coupled datasets\n    that ship as a single file; MFDB for larger, independently writable entities.\n  - MFDB does not enforce referential integrity. FK conventions are advisory.\n\n---\n\nValidation Summary\n\n  | Check                            | Level  | Hard/Warn |\n  |----------------------------------|--------|-----------|\n  | Manifest is valid BEJSON 104a    | 1      | Hard      |\n  | Records_Type == [\"mfdb\"]         | 1      | Hard      |\n  | entity_name and file_path fields | 1      | Hard      |\n  | No null entity_name / file_path  | 1      | Hard      |\n  | Unique entity_name and file_path | 1      | Hard      |\n  | All file_paths resolve on disk   | 1      | Hard      |\n  | Entity is valid BEJSON 104       | 2      | Hard      |\n  | Parent_Hierarchy present         | 2      | Hard      |\n  | Parent_Hierarchy resolves        | 2      | Hard      |\n  | Records_Type single-entry match  | 2      | Hard      |\n  | Bidirectional path check         | 2      | Hard      |\n  | record_count accuracy            | 3      | Warning   |\n  | FK target unresolved (strict)    | 3      | Warning   |\n\n---\n\nSummary\n\n  | Feature              | MFDB                                    |\n  |----------------------|-----------------------------------------|\n  | Manifest format      | BEJSON 104a (104a.mfdb.bejson)          |\n  | Entity format        | BEJSON 104 (one file per entity)        |\n  | Back-reference key   | Parent_Hierarchy (relative path)        |\n  | Records per file     | Dense (no null-padding for schema gaps) |\n  | FK enforcement       | None \u2014 convention only (_fk suffix)     |\n  | Custom headers       | Manifest only (104a rules apply)        |\n  | Complex types        | Allowed in entity files (BEJSON 104)    |\n  | Multi-entity in one  | No \u2014 use BEJSON 104db for that          |\n  | Spec version         | MFDB_Version: \"1.31\"                     |\n\n### The MFDB v1.3.1 Master-Slave Federation\n\nYour environment is now successfully split into a tiered, federated node system designed to maximize AI context efficiency through \"Structural Blindness\" and \"One-Way Awareness\".\n\n**1. Node Roles & The `Network_Role` Standard**\nInstead of refactoring your core libraries to look for a new key, your `104a.mfdb.bejson` manifests correctly use the **`Network_Role`** header to define their topological status as either \"Master\" or \"Slave\".\n*   **Master Node (Administrative Layer):** Acts as the authoritative registry (\"The Truth\"). It has full visibility and polling access to all connected Slaves, handling global policy distribution and long-term data distillation.\n*   **Slave Node (Context Window):** Acts as the high-performance operational workspace. It is **structurally blind**, meaning it operates without any hardcoded paths to the Master and lacks read-access to the Master's archives, keeping the active context window completely lean.\n\n**2. Active Communication Protocols**\nTo keep the Slave blind while keeping the system synchronized, your architecture now relies on two unidirectional data flows:\n*   **One-Way Push (Log Distillation):** Your `initialization_watcher_service` allows the Slave node to autonomously truncate its local logs and push the metrics up to the Master's polling directory.\n*   **Inverse Drop-Zone Polling (Atomic Updates):** When the Master needs to enforce a new policy or configuration, it formats the update as a BEJSON 104a document and pushes it directly into the Slave's local directory (e.g., `BEJSON_Core/Data/`). It uses an OS-level atomic file swap (`os.rename`) to instantly replace the active file, preventing partial-read crashes. The blind Slave simply polls its own directory to ingest the updates.\n\nBy completing this update while keeping the `Network_Role` key, you have successfully secured the v1.3.1 strategic benefits: **Zero-Bloat Context** (administrative scaffolding is hidden from the AI), **Atomic Integrity** (hardware-level protection for updates), and **Scalable Silos** (allowing your Master to easily control multiple portable environments).\n",

Both changes landed after v1.2 shipped but were left under the v1.2 label \\u2014 should have been bumped at the time per versioning policy (bump on every change, no exceptions). Corrected now.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## 1. Confirmed Bugs (Fixed)\\n\\n| # | File | Bug | Impact | Fix |\\n|---|---|---|---|---|\\n| 1 | `lib_bejson_engine_core.js` | `Core.Engine = CoreEngine` at file end referenced an undeclared `Core` global \\u2014 no `window.Core = window.Core \\\\|\\\\| {}` guard, unlike every sibling Gaming file. | `ReferenceError` on load in any strict-mode context. Library unusable. | Added the missing guard. |\\n| 2 | `lib_bejson_parse.js` | Bare `module.exports = {...}` with no `typeof module !== 'undefined'` check. | `ReferenceError` when loaded in browser/WebView \\u2014 your actual deployment target \\u2014 since `module` doesn't exist there. | Wrapped in environment guard, matching the pattern already used elsewhere in the same file. Swept all 38 other `Lib_JS` files for the same pattern; this was the only occurrence. |\\n| 3 | `lib_bejson_assets.js` | `loaded` field declared in the `bejson` registry schema but never written by `load()` \\u2014 only the in-memory `cache` Map was updated. | Asset registry table silently out of sync with reality; anything reading `bejson.Values` for loaded-state would see stale/empty data. | `load()` now pushes `[id, type, path, true]` to the registry on successful load. |\\n\\n## 2. Source Comparison Findings (engine dump vs. official)\\n\\nThe engine dump turned out to be an **earlier branch**, not a newer one, for most Gaming-family files. Confirmed via line-by-line diff:\\n\\n- **Physics, renderer, input core, UI screens:** engine dump versions are strict subsets \\u2014 no impulse queue, no static/dynamic collision resolution, no camera/zoom/DPR scaling, no chunked frustum-culled rendering, no touch joystick, no screen registry/navigation. Discarded; official stood unchanged.\\n- **Validators (`lib_mfdb_validator.js`, `lib_bejson_validator.js`):** engine dump's browser-context fallback silently returns `true` / swallows errors instead of failing explicitly. This is a correctness regression \\u2014 silently treating unvalidated input as valid. Official's explicit `false` + error message was kept in both files.\\n- **Literal-string bug:** both `lib_mfdb_validator.js` and `lib_bejson_validator.js` in the engine dump had `lines.join('\\\\\\\\n')` (literal two-character backslash-n) instead of `lines.join('\\\\n')` (real newline) in their report-formatting functions. Official was correct in both. No merge needed.\\n\\n## 3. Genuine Extensions Merged (non-overlapping, both kept)\\n\\nThese weren't duplicate implementations \\u2014 they were different capabilities that happened to share a filename:\\n\\n| File | Official capability | Engine dump capability | Resolution |\\n|---|---|---|---|\\n| `lib_bejson_grid.js` | Layer-based grid (`SwitchGrid`) | Row/col collision grid (solid-tile lookup) | Added as `CollisionGrid`, separate class, separate namespace slot. |\\n| `lib_bejson_events.js` | Scripted condition/action system tied to state (`SwitchEvents`) | Classic pub-sub emitter (`on`/`emit`) | Added as `SwitchEventEmitter`, separate class, separate namespace slot. |\\n| `lib_bejson_list_validator.js` | Hierarchical id/parent_id orphan-reference check | Array-field type-homogeneity check | Added `validateListField()` alongside existing `ListValidator.validate()`. Note: the engine dump's function actually matches this file's own header description (\\\"ensure array homogeneity\\\") better than the official function did \\u2014 header was already describing the wrong implementation before this merge. |\\n| `lib_bejson_utility.js` (+ `Utility/` duplicate) | Chunker schemas, filename sanitize, timestamp formatting, base64 download | `randomId()`, `hash()` (SHA-256 via Web Crypto) | Both added as new methods on the existing `Utility` object. |\\n| `lib_mfdb_core.js` | `MFDBArchive` class \\u2014 zip mount/commit via File System Access API | Manifest creation, entity registration, record-count sync, entity file init (Spec \\u00a715.3/\\u00a715.4/\\u00a715.10) | Combined into one file under one `window.MFDB_CORE` export. This was the largest merge (116 \\u2192 341 lines) \\u2014 official had zero coverage of direct manifest/entity manipulation; engine dump had zero coverage of zip packaging. |\\n| `lib_bejson_engine.js` | *(did not exist)* | Self-driving `requestAnimationFrame` loop with delta-time tracking | Added as new file. Complements `CoreEngine` in `engine_core.js`, which expects to be driven externally rather than scheduling its own frames. |\\n\\n## 4. Naming Contradiction \\u2014 Resolved Against Canonical Source\\n\\nError code `53` had two different names depending on source:\\n- Official JS: `E_MFDB_CORE_LOCK_FAILED`\\n- Engine dump JS: `E_MFDB_CORE_CREATE_FAILED` (actively used twice in its own `mfdb_core.js`)\\n\\nRather than guess, checked the canonical cross-language registries: **`Lib_PY/Core/lib_bejson_errors.py`** and **`Lib_SH/Core/lib_bejson_errors.sh`** both confirm `53 = E_MFDB_CORE_LOCK_FAILED`. Kept the official name at 53; added `E_MFDB_CORE_CREATE_FAILED` as a new code (72) instead of overwriting. The merged `lib_mfdb_core.js` uses code 72 for its create-failure paths.\\n\\n## 5. Dead Code (Flagged, Not Removed)\\n\\n- **`BEJSONEngine` class** in `lib_bejson_engine_core.js` \\u2014 defined, never exported, never referenced anywhere in the file. Superseded by `CoreEngine` in the same file. Left in place per surgical-changes policy (mention, don't delete unrelated dead code).\\n\\n## 6. Security-Adjacent Finding\\n\\n- **`lib_bejson_secure_zip.js`** \\u2014 `secure_zip_validate()` silently returns `true` (no-op) when the Node `path` module is unavailable, i.e. it provides zero protection in your actual Android WebView target environment. This is not a live vulnerability: real Zip Slip / path-traversal protection exists in `lib_bejson_parse.js`'s extraction routine (resolves target path, asserts boundary prefix, throws on traversal \\u2014 confirmed present and correct, unlike the engine dump's equivalent which had no boundary check at all). But `secure_zip_validate()` itself reads as a guard and isn't one in-browser \\u2014 worth a rename or a real implementation if anything calls it expecting protection.\\n\\n## 7. Module Export Decisions\\n\\nSeveral files gained ES `export` statements; several deliberately did not, based on whether anything actually consumes them via `import`:\\n\\n- **Added** to `lib_bejson_bejson.js`: confirmed `bejson_game.js` does `import BEJSON from './lib_bejson_bejson.js'`.\\n- **Not added** to `lib_bejson_errors.js`, `lib_bejson_core.js`, `lib_bejson_validator.js`, `lib_mfdb_validator.js`, `lib_html3_table.js`: no ES import consumers found anywhere in either source tree; these are loaded purely via `window.*` globals (matches your classic-script/WebView architecture). Adding `export` would have been free of immediate breakage but added an untested code path for no benefit.\\n\\n## 8. Excluded From Library\\n\\n- **`bejson_game.js`** \\u2014 confirmed to be application/consumer code (a specific `VanillaGame` demo class reaching into `renderer._ctx` directly, no `Library_Version`/`RELATIONAL_ID` in its own header). Not a reusable library module by your own file-header conventions. Left out of `Lib_JS`; useful only as a reference implementation exercising the merged APIs.\\n\\n## 9. Correction \\u2014 Gaming Files Removed From Core\\n\\nThe original official zip shipped `lib_bejson_assets.js`, `lib_bejson_engine_core.js`, `lib_bejson_events.js`, `lib_bejson_grid.js`, `lib_bejson_input.js`, `lib_bejson_physics.js`, `lib_bejson_renderer.js`, and `lib_bejson_ui_screens.js` duplicated into both `Lib_JS/Gaming/` and `Lib_JS/Core/` (header-tagged `Family: Core` in the latter despite being Gaming-specific content). An initial pass propagated the merge content into both copies to keep them in sync; per direction, these files belong in `Lib_JS/Gaming/` only. The `Core/`-folder duplicates (including the new `lib_bejson_engine.js`) have been removed. `Lib_JS/Core/` now contains only genuinely Core-family files (parse, validator, schema, state, errors, mfdb_core, mfdb_validator, list_validator, secure_zip, utility, html3_table, bejson, jszip, cache test). `Lib_JS/Gaming/` retains the full merged Gaming set as the single source of truth.\\n\\n## 10. Verification \\u2014 Gaming-Folder Files Checked Against Their Own Pre-Existing Core Duplicates\\n\\nAfter the Section 9 correction, every Gaming-family file was checksummed against its original (pre-merge) Core-folder duplicate to confirm nothing was missed by diffing against the wrong baseline:\\n\\n- `lib_bejson_assets.js`, `lib_bejson_events.js`, `lib_bejson_grid.js`, `lib_bejson_input.js`, `lib_bejson_physics.js`, `lib_bejson_renderer.js`, `lib_bejson_ui_screens.js` \\u2014 byte-identical (MD5 match) between the original Gaming and Core copies. Diffing against either was equivalent; nothing missed.\\n- `lib_bejson_engine_core.js` \\u2014 **the one file that had actually diverged.** The Gaming-folder original retained the dead `BEJSONEngine` class (see \\u00a75) but was *missing* the `window.Core` declaration; the Core-folder original had the declaration but had already dropped the dead class. Confirmed the final merged `Lib_JS/Gaming/lib_bejson_engine_core.js` carries both \\u2014 the bugfix and the dead class \\u2014 so the deliverable is correct, but this divergence should have been called out explicitly during the original merge pass rather than only surfacing on follow-up review.\\n\\n---\\n\\n**Net result:** every official capability retained, 3 real bugs fixed in code that __mfdb_field_index \\\"$1\\\" \\\"file_path\\\"\\n}\\n\\n__mfdb_core_get_file_path() {\\n    local manifest_path=\\\"$1\\\"\\n    local entity_name=\\\"$2\\\"\\n    local en_idx fp_idx\\n -n1\\n}\\n\\n#-------------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Dependency    Elton Boehnen\\n * Contact:        eltonboehnen@gmail.com | boehnenelton2024.pages.dev | github.com/boehnenelton\\n * Format_Creator: Elton Boehnen\\n * RELATIONAL_ID: should be replaced by a cluster variant.\\n   */\\n  static getTileVariant(\\n    type: string, \\n    x: number, \\n    y: number, \\n    biome: BiomeConfig, \\n    levelId: string, \\n    assets: Record<string, any>, \\n    sprites: Record<string, any>\\n  ): string {\\n    // Structured (Brick-by-Brick) generation ignores clusters/organic noise\\n    if (biome.generationMode === 'structured') {\\n      return type;\\n    }\\n\\n    const detailHash = Math.abs((x * 73856093) ^ (y * 19349663) ^ (levelId.length || 1));\\n\\n    // Check clusters (Organic mode) \\n    if (biome.clusters) {\\n      for (const cluster of biome.clusters) {\\n        const scale = cluster.size || 7;\\n        const nx = x / scale;\\n        const ny = y / scale;\\n        const ix = Math.floor(nx);\\n        const iy = Math.floor(ny);\\n        \\n        const h1 = Math.abs((ix * 73856093) ^ (iy * 19349663) ^ 11) % 1000 / 1000;\\n        const h2 = Math.abs(((ix + 1) * 73856093) ^ (iy * 19349663) ^ 11) % 1000 / 1000;\\n        const h3 = Math.abs((ix * 73856093) ^ ((iy + 1) * 19349663) ^ 11) % 1000 / 1000;\\n        const h4 = Math.abs(((ix + 1) * 73856093) ^ ((iy + 1) * 19349663) ^ 11) % 1000 / 1000;\\n        \\n        const wx = nx - ix;\\n        const wy = ny - iy;\\n        const noise = (h1 * (1 - wx) + h2 * wx) * (1 - wy) + (h3 * (1 - wx) + h4 * wx) * wy;\\n        \\n        if (noise > (cluster.threshold || 0.7)) {\\n          if (sprites[cluster.type] || assets[cluster.type]) return cluster.type;\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Check scatter probabilities\\n    if (biome.scatter) {\\n      for (const s of biome.scatter) {\\n        if ((detailHash % 100) / 100 < (s.probability || 0.01)) {\\n          if (sprites[s.type] || assets[s.type]) return s.type;\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n    \\n    // Organic transition corners (Subtle blending)\\n    if (detailHash % 6 === 0) {\\n      const corners = [];\\n      for (let i = 5; i <= 8; i++) {\\n        const vId = `${type}_v${i}`;\\n        if (sprites[vId] || assets[vId]) corners.push(vId);\\n      }\\n      if (corners.length > 0) return corners[detailHash % corners.length];\\n    }\\n\\n    return type;\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\n      false\n    ],\n    [\n      \"FileContent\",\n      null,\n      null,\n      null,\n      \"Lib_TS/GamingBackend/bejson_backend_inventory.ts\",\n Library_Version: 204\\n * Date:           2026-06-19\\n * Author:         Elton Boehnen\\n * Contact:        eltonboehnen@gmail.com | boehnenelton2024.pages.dev | github.com/boehnenelton\\n * levels, and randomness.\\n   */\\n  static calculateDamage(attacker: any, defender: any, weapon?: any, armor?: any): number {\\n    const baseAtk = attacker.atk || 1;           \\n    const weaponBonus = weapon ? weapon.attack_bonus || 0 : 0;\\n    const totalAtk = baseAtk + weaponBonus;\\n\\n    const baseDef = defender.def || 0;           \\n    const armorBonus = armor ? armor.defense_bonus || 0 : 0;\\n    const totalDef = baseDef + armorBonus;\\n\\n    // Core formula: Attack vs Defense with 60% armor mitigation\\n    let damage = Math.max(1, Math.floor(totalAtk - (totalDef * 0.6)));\\n\\n    // Critical hit chance (10% crit for 1.75x damage)\\n    if (Math.random() < 0.1) {  \\n      damage = Math.floor(damage * 1.75);\\n    }\\n\\n    // Level advantage bonus\\n    const levelDiff = (attacker.level || 1) - (defender.level || 1);\\n    if (levelDiff > 0) {\\n        damage += Math.floor(levelDiff * 1.5);\\n    }\\n\\n    return Math.max(1, damage); \\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Generates a sword attack state object based on player position and swing type.\\n   */\\n  static createSwordAttack(\\n    player: any, \\n    swordAsset: any, \\n    isSpin: boolean, \\n    lastSwingLeftToRight: boolean\\n  ): any {\\n    // Define base duration\\n    const baseDuration = isSpin ? 0.30 : 0.15;\\n    \\n    // Calculate a random speed multiplier\\n    // Normal swings: 0.95x to 1.05x speed\\n    // Occasional slightly slower swing: 1.15x to 1.25x\\n    const isHeavySwing = Math.random() < 0.15;\\n    const speedMult = isHeavySwing \\n      ? (1.15 + Math.random() * 0.1) \\n      : (0.95 + Math.random() * 0.1);\\n\\n    const finalDuration = baseDuration * speedMult;\\n\\n    return {\\n      x: player.x + player.facing.x * 24,\\n      y: player.y + player.facing.y * 24,\\n      width: swordAsset?.hitbox_width || 24,\\n      height: swordAsset?.hitbox_height || 24,\\n      damage: isHeavySwing ? (swordAsset?.damage || 10) * 1.5 : (swordAsset?.damage || 10),\\n      life: finalDuration,\\n      maxLife: finalDuration,\\n      facingAngle: Math.atan2(player.facing.y, player.facing.x),\\n      isSpin: isSpin,\\n      isLeftToRight: lastSwingLeftToRight,\\n      isHeavySwing: isHeavySwing,\\n      // Random variance for visual uniqueness\\n      reachOffset: (Math.random() - 0.5) * 6,\\n      gapOffset: (Math.random() - 0.5) * 4,\\n      extensionOffset: (Math.random() - 0.5) * 6,\\n      angleOffset: (Math.random() - 0.5) * 0.20\\n    };\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Updates the precise sweep angle and coordinates of a sword attack volumne.\\n   */\\n  static updateSwordArc(sword: any, player: any, dt: number): void {\\n    const progress = 1 - (Math.max(0, sword.life) / sword.maxLife);\\n    const angleDeviation = sword.angleOffset || 0;\\n    const sweepSpread = sword.isSpin ? (Math.PI * 2) : (Math.PI * (150 / 180));\\n    \\n    let startAngle, totalSweep;\\n    if (sword.isSpin) {\\n      startAngle = sword.facingAngle - (Math.PI / 2) + angleDeviation;\\n      totalSweep = Math.PI * 2;\\n    } else if (sword.isLeftToRight) {\\n      startAngle = sword.facingAngle - (sweepSpread / 2) + angleDeviation;\\n      totalSweep = sweepSpread;\\n    } else {\\n      startAngle = sword.facingAngle + (sweepSpread / 2) + angleDeviation;\\n      totalSweep = -sweepSpread;\\n    }\\n    \\n    const sweepAngle = startAngle + (progress * totalSweep);\\n    const reach = 28 + (sword.reachOffset || 0) + (sword.extensionOffset || 0);\\n    \\n    sword.x = player.x + (player.width / 2) + Math.cos(sweepAngle) * reach - (sword.width / 2);\\n    sword.y = player.y + (player.height / 2) + Math.sin(sweepAngle) * reach - (sword.height / 2);\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\n      false\n    ],\n    [\n      \"FileContent\",\n      null,\n      null,\n      null,\n AILibrary {\\n  /**\\n   * Updates enemy velocity (pendingVx, pendingVy) based on current AI state.\\n   */\\n  static updateEnemyAI(actor: any, player: any, dt: number): void {\\n    const dx = player.x - actor.x; \\n    const dy = player.y - actor.y; \\n    const dist = Math.sqrt(dx * dx + dy * dy);\\n\\n    // Initial state setup\\n    if (!actor.aiState) {\\n      actor.aiState = 'idle';\\n      actor.stateTimer = 0;\\n    }\\n\\n    if (actor.aiState === 'idle') {\\n      actor.pendingVx = 0;\\n      actor.pendingVy = 0;\\n      \\n      // If within perceptual radius, begin alert/stunned (double-take)\\n      if (dist > 0 && dist < 200) {\\n        actor.aiState = 'alert';\\n        actor.stateTimer = 0.5; \\n      }\\n    }\\n    else if (actor.aiState === 'alert') {\\n      actor.pendingVx = 0;\\n      actor.pendingVy = 0;\\n      actor.stateTimer -= dt;\\n      if (actor.stateTimer <= 0) {\\n        actor.aiState = 'windup';\\n        actor.stateTimer = 0.6;\\n      }\\n    } \\n    else if (actor.aiState === 'windup') {\\n      actor.pendingVx = 0;\\n      actor.pendingVy = 0;\\n      actor.stateTimer -= dt;\\n\\n      if (actor.stateTimer <= 0) {\\n        actor.aiState = 'charge';\\n        actor.stateTimer = 0.8; \\n        // Lock direction at start of charge\\n        const magnitude = actor.speed * 1.5;\\n        actor.lockedVx = (dx / dist) * magnitude;\\n        actor.lockedVy = (dy / dist) * magnitude;\\n      }\\n    } \\n    else if (actor.aiState === 'charge') {\\n      actor.pendingVx = actor.lockedVx || 0;\\n      actor.pendingVy = actor.lockedVy || 0;\\n      actor.stateTimer -= dt;\\n\\n      if (actor.stateTimer <= 0) {\\n        actor.aiState = 'cooldown';\\n        actor.stateTimer = 0.5;\\n      }\\n    } \\n    else if  }\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n\",\n      false\n    ],\n    [\n      \"FileContent\",\n      null,\n      null,\n      null,\n      \"Lib_TS/GamingBackend/bejson_backend_physics.ts\",\n * Checks if an actor will collide with solid tiles at a given velocity.\\n   */\\n  static checkTileCollision(\\n    actor: any, \\n    vx: number, \\n    vy: number, \\n    dt: number, \\n    tiles: any[], \\n    assets: Record<string, any>, \\n    tileSize: number,\\n    tileGrid?: Map<string, any>\\n  ): boolean {\\n    const newX = actor.x + vx * dt; \\n    const newY = actor.y + vy * dt;\\n actor.height - margin;\\n\\n    // Use tileGrid for O(1) lookups if provided\\n    if (tileGrid) {\\n      const minX = Math.floor(actorLeft / tileSize);\\n      const maxX = Math.floor(actorRight / tileSize);\\n      const minY = Math.floor(actorTop / tileSize);\\n      const maxY = Math.floor(actorBottom / tileSize);\\n\\n      for (let x = minX; x <= maxX; x++) {\\n        for (let y = minY; y <= maxY; y++) {\\n          const tile = tileGrid.get(`${x}_${y}`);\\n          if (tile) {\\n            const rules = assets[tile.terrain_type || tile.object_type];\\n            if (rules && rules.is_solid) {\\n               return true;\\n            }\\n          }\\n        }\\n      }\\n      return false;\\n    }\\n\\n    // Fallback block mapping\\n    for (const tile of tiles) {\\n      const rules = assets[tile.terrain_type || tile.object_type];\\n      if (rules && rules.is_solid) {\\n        const tileLeft = tile.x * tileSize; \\n        const tileRight = tileLeft + tileSize;\\n        const tileTop = tile.y * tileSize; \\n        const tileBottom = tileTop + tileSize;\\n        \\n        if (actorLeft < tileRight && actorRight > tileLeft && \\n            actorTop < tileBottom && actorBottom > tileTop) {\\n          return true;\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Checks if an actor will collide with other actors + vx * dt;\\n    const newY = actor.y + vy * dt;\\n    const margin = 6; \\n\\n    // Broad phase bounds\\n    const actorLeft = newX + margin;\\n    const actorRight = newX + actor.width - margin;\\n    const actorTop = newY + margin;\\n    const actorBottom = newY + actor.height - margin;\\n\\n    for (const other of actors) {\\n      if (other === actor || other.isHibernated) continue;\\n\\n margin;\\n\\n      if (actorLeft < otherRight && actorRight > otherLeft && \\n          actorTop < otherBottom && actorBottom > otherTop) {\\n        return true;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n\\n  /**\\n   * Standard AABB overlap check.\\n   */\\n  static overlaps(a: any, b: any): boolean {\\n    return (\\n      a.x < b.x + b.width && \\n      a.x + a.width > b.x && \\n   }\\n\\n    const mag = Math.sqrt(vx * vx + vy * vy);\\n    if (mag > 1) { vx /= mag; vy /= mag; }\\n    return { x: vx, y: vy, action: this.justPressed['action'], cancel: this.justPressed['cancel'] {});\\n\\nconst {\\n  E_MFDB_NOT_MANIFEST,\\n  E_MFDB_NOT_ENTITY_FILE,\\n  E_MFDB_MANIFEST_RECORDS_TYPE,\\n  E_MFDB_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND,\\n  E_MFDB_ENTITY_NAME_MISMATCH,\\n  E_MFDB_DUPLICATE_ENTRY,\\n  E_MFDB_NO_PARENT_HIERARCHY,\\n  E_MFDB_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND,\\n  E_MFDB_BIDIRECTIONAL_FAIL,\\n  E_MFDB_FK_UNRESOLVED,\\n  E_MFDB_MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD,\\n  E_MFDB_NULL_REQUIRED,\\n  E_MFDB_INVALID_ARCHIVE\\n} = BEJSON_ERRORS;\\n\\nclass MFDBValidationError extends Error {\\n  constructor(message, code) {\\n    super(message);\\n    this.name = 'MFDBValidationError';\\n    this.code = code;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Validation state\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nlet _mErrors   = [];\\nlet _mWarnings = [];\\n\\nfunction _mReset()                  { _mErrors = []; _mWarnings = []; }\\nfunction _mAddError(msg, loc)       { _mErrors.push(   'ERROR'   + (loc ? ` | Location: ${loc}` : '') + ` | Message: ${msg}`); }\\nfunction _mAddWarning(msg, loc)     { _mWarnings.push( 'WARNING' + (loc ? ` | Location: ${loc}` : '') + ` | Message: ${msg}`); }\\nfunction _mHasErrors()              { return _mErrors.length   > 0; }\\nfunction _mHasWarnings()            { return _mWarnings.length > 0; }\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Internal helpers (also exported for lib_mfdb_core.js)\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n// require('fs') and require('path') are Node.js-only. In browser environments\\n// these helpers are non-functional. Guard all calls with a Node check so that importing\\n// this module in a browser context does not throw immediately on function definition.\\nfunction _isNode() {\\n  return typeof process !== 'undefined' && process.versions && !!process.versions.node;\\n}\\n\\nfunction _loadJson(filePath) {\\n  if (!_isNode()) {\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError('_loadJson is only available in Node.js environments', E_MFDB_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND);\\n  }\\n  const fs = require('fs');\\n  return JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(filePath, 'utf8'));\\n}\\n\\nfunction _rowsAsDicts(doc) {\\n  const names = doc.Fields.map(f => f.name);\\n  return doc.Values.map(row => Object.fromEntries(names.map((n, i) => [n, row[i]])));\\n}\\n\\nfunction _resolveEntityPath(manifestPath, filePathRel) {\\n  if (!_isNode()) {\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError('_resolveEntityPath is only available in Node.js environments', E_MFDB_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND);\\n  }\\n  const path = require('path');\\n  return path.resolve(path.dirname(manifestPath), filePathRel);\\n}\\n\\nfunction _fileExists(filePath) {\\n  if (!_isNode()) return false;  // In browser: assume non-existent, caller handles\\n  const fs = require('fs');\\n  return fs.existsSync(filePath);\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Archive Validation (v1.2 Feature)\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nfunction mfdb_validator_validate_archive(archivePath) {\\n  _mReset();\\n  const AdmZip = require('adm-zip');\\n\\n  if (!_fileExists(archivePath)) {\\n    _mAddError(`Archive not found: ${archivePath}`, 'File System');\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError(`Archive not found: ${archivePath}`, E_MFDB_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND);\\n  }\\n\\n  try {\\n    const zip = new AdmZip(archivePath);\\n    const zipEntries = zip.getEntries();\\n    const hasManifest = zipEntries.some(e => e.entryName === '104a.mfdb.bejson');\\n    if (!hasManifest) {\\n      _mAddError(\\\"Archive missing 104a.mfdb.bejson at root\\\", \\\"Zip Structure\\\");\\n      throw new MFDBValidationError(\\\"Missing manifest inside archive\\\", E_MFDB_INVALID_ARCHIVE);\\n    }\\n  } catch (exc) {\\n    _mAddError(`Invalid zip file: ${exc.message}`, \\\"Zip Parser\\\");\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError(exc.message, E_MFDB_INVALID_ARCHIVE);\\n  }\\n\\n  return true;\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Manifest Validation  (Spec \\u00a78.1)\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nfunction mfdb_validator_validate_manifest(manifestPath) {\\n  _mReset();\\n  if (!_isNode()) {\\n    _mAddError('mfdb_validator_validate_manifest requires a Node.js environment', 'Environment');\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (!_fileExists(manifestPath)) {\\n    _mAddError(`Manifest file not found: ${manifestPath}`, 'File System');\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError(`File not found: ${manifestPath}`, E_MFDB_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND);\\n  }\\n\\n  try {\\n    bejson_validator_validate_file(manifestPath);\\n  } catch (exc) {\\n    _mAddError(`BEJSON 104a validation failed: ${exc.message}`, 'BEJSON Validation');\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError(exc.message, E_MFDB_NOT_MANIFEST);\\n  }\\n\\n  const doc = _loadJson(manifestPath);\\n\\n  if (doc.Format_Version !== '104a') {\\n    _mAddError(\\\"Manifest must be Format_Version '104a'\\\", 'Format_Version');\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError('Manifest must be 104a', E_MFDB_NOT_MANIFEST);\\n  }\\n\\n  const rt = doc.Records_Type || [];\\n  if (!(rt.length === 1 && rt[0] === 'mfdb')) {\\n    _mAddError(`Records_Type must be [\\\"mfdb\\\"]. Found: ${JSON.stringify(rt)}`, 'Records_Type');\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError('Bad manifest Records_Type', E_MFDB_MANIFEST_RECORDS_TYPE);\\n  }\\n\\n  const fieldNames = (doc.Fields || []).map(f => f.name);\\n  for (const required of ['entity_name', 'file_path']) {\\n    if (!fieldNames.includes(required)) {\\n      _mAddError(`Manifest Fields must include '${required}'`, 'Fields');\\n      throw new MFDBValidationError(`Missing required field '${required}'`, E_MFDB_MISSING_REQUIRED_FIELD);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  const entries    = _rowsAsDicts(doc);\\n  const seenNames  = new Set();\\n  const seenPaths  = new Set();\\n\\n  for (let i = 0; i < entries.length; i++) {\\n    const entry      = entries[i];\\n    const entityName = entry.entity_name;\\n    const filePath   = entry.file_path;\\n\\n    if (!entityName) {\\n      _mAddError(`Record ${i}: entity_name is null or missing`, `Values[${i}]`);\\n      throw new MFDBValidationError('Null entity_name', E_MFDB_NULL_REQUIRED);\\n    }\\n    if (!filePath) {\\n      _mAddError(`Record ${i}: file_path is null or missing`, `Values[${i}]`);\\n      throw new MFDBValidationError('Null file_path', E_MFDB_NULL_REQUIRED);\\n    }\\n    if (seenNames.has(entityName)) {\\n      _mAddError(`Duplicate entity_name: '${entityName}'`, `Values[${i}]`);\\n      throw new MFDBValidationError(`Duplicate entity_name: ${entityName}`, E_MFDB_DUPLICATE_ENTRY);\\n    }\\n    seenNames.add(entityName);\\n\\n    if (seenPaths.has(filePath)) {\\n      _mAddError(`Duplicate file_path: '${filePath}'`, `Values[${i}]`);\\n      throw new MFDBValidationError(`Duplicate file_path: ${filePath}`, E_MFDB_DUPLICATE_ENTRY);\\n    }\\n    seenPaths.add(filePath);\\n\\n    const resolved = _resolveEntityPath(manifestPath, filePath);\\n    if (!_fileExists(resolved)) {\\n      _mAddError(`Entity file '${filePath}' not found (resolved: ${resolved})`, `Values[${i}]/file_path`);\\n      throw new MFDBValidationError(`Entity file not found: ${resolved}`, E_MFDB_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return true;\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Entity File Validation  (Spec \\u00a78.2)\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nfunction mfdb_validator_validate_entity_file(entityPath, checkBidirectional = true) {\\n  const path = require('path');\\n  _mReset();\\n\\n  if (!_fileExists(entityPath)) {\\n    _mAddError(`Entity file not found: ${entityPath}`, 'File System');\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError(`File not found: ${entityPath}`, E_MFDB_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND);\\n  }\\n\\n  try {\\n    bejson_validator_validate_file(entityPath);\\n  } catch (exc) {\\n    _mAddError(`BEJSON 104 validation failed: ${exc.message}`, 'BEJSON Validation');\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError(exc.message, E_MFDB_NOT_ENTITY_FILE);\\n  }\\n\\n  const doc = _loadJson(entityPath);\\n\\n  if (doc.Format_Version !== '104') {\\n    _mAddError(\\\"Entity file must be Format_Version '104'\\\", 'Format_Version');\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError('Entity file must be 104', E_MFDB_NOT_ENTITY_FILE);\\n  }\\n\\n  const parentHierarchy = doc.Parent_Hierarchy;\\n  if (!parentHierarchy) {\\n    _mAddError('Entity file must contain Parent_Hierarchy pointing to the manifest', 'Parent_Hierarchy');\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError('Missing Parent_Hierarchy', E_MFDB_NO_PARENT_HIERARCHY);\\n  }\\n\\n  const entityDir    = path.dirname(path.resolve(entityPath));\\n  const manifestPath = path.resolve(entityDir, parentHierarchy);\\n\\n  if (!_fileExists(manifestPath)) {\\n    _mAddError(\\n      `Parent_Hierarchy '${parentHierarchy}' resolves to '${manifestPath}' which does not exist`,\\n      'Parent_Hierarchy',\\n    );\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError(`Manifest not found: ${manifestPath}`, E_MFDB_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND);\\n  }\\n\\n  if (!path.basename(manifestPath).endsWith('.mfdb.bejson')) {\\n    _mAddWarning(\\n      `Parent_Hierarchy target '${manifestPath}' does not end in '.mfdb.bejson'. Expected: 104a.mfdb.bejson`,\\n      'Parent_Hierarchy',\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  const rt = doc.Records_Type || [];\\n  if (rt.length !== 1) {\\n    _mAddError(`Entity file Records_Type must have exactly one entry. Found: ${JSON.stringify(rt)}`, 'Records_Type');\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError('Entity Records_Type must be single-entry', E_MFDB_NOT_ENTITY_FILE);\\n  }\\n\\n  const entityName = rt[0];\\n\\n  let manifestDoc, entries, manifestEntityNames;\\n  try {\\n    manifestDoc        = _loadJson(manifestPath);\\n    entries            = _rowsAsDicts(manifestDoc);\\n    manifestEntityNames = entries.map(e => e.entity_name);\\n  } catch (exc) {\\n    _mAddError(`Could not read manifest: ${exc.message}`, 'Manifest');\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError(`Cannot read manifest: ${exc.message}`, E_MFDB_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND);\\n  }\\n\\n  if (!manifestEntityNames.includes(entityName)) {\\n    _mAddError(\\n      `Records_Type '${entityName}' does not appear as entity_name in the manifest`,\\n      'Records_Type vs Manifest',\\n    );\\n    throw new MFDBValidationError(\\n      `Entity '${entityName}' not registered in manifest`, E_MFDB_ENTITY_NAME_MISMATCH,\\n    );\\n  }\\n\\n  if (checkBidirectional) {\\n    const match = entries.find(e => e.entity_name === entityName);\\n    if (match) {\\n      const manifestDir    = path.dirname(path.resolve(manifestPath));\\n      const fromManifest   = path.resolve(manifestDir, match.file_path || '');\\n      const thisFile       = path.resolve(entityPath);\\n      if (fromManifest !== thisFile) {\\n        _mAddError(\\n          `Bidirectional check failed for entity '${entityName}': ` +\\n          `manifest points to '${fromManifest}', but this file is '${thisFile}'`,\\n          'Bidirectional Path Check',\\n        );\\n        throw new MFDBValidationError('Bidirectional path check failed', E_MFDB_BIDIRECTIONAL_FAIL);\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return true;\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Database-Level Validation  (Spec \\u00a78.3)\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nfunction mfdb_validator_validate_database(manifestPath, strictFk = false) {\\n  _mReset();\\n\\n  try {\\n    mfdb_validator_validate_manifest(manifestPath);\\n  } catch (exc) {\\n    throw exc;\\n  }\\n\\n  const manifestDoc = _loadJson(manifestPath);\\n  const entries     = _rowsAsDicts(manifestDoc);\\n\\n  const pkMap = {};\\n  for (const e of entries) {\\n    if (e.primary_key) pkMap[e.entity_name] = e.primary_key;\\n  }\\n\\n  for (const entry of entries) {\\n    const entityName    = entry.entity_name;\\n    const filePathRel   = entry.file_path;\\n    const declaredCount = entry.record_count;\\n\\n    const resolved = _resolveEntityPath(manifestPath, filePathRel);\\n\\n    try {\\n      mfdb_validator_validate_entity_file(resolved, true);\\n    } catch (exc) {\\n      _mAddError(`Entity '${entityName}' failed validation: ${exc.message}`, `Entity/${entityName}`);\\n      throw exc;\\n    }\\n\\n    if (declaredCount !== null && declaredCount !== undefined) {\\n      const edoc        = _loadJson(resolved);\\n      const actualCount = (edoc.Values || []).length;\\n      if (actualCount !== declaredCount) {\\n        _mAddWarning(\\n          `Entity '${entityName}': manifest declares record_count=${declaredCount}, ` +\\n          `actual=${actualCount}. Call mfdb_core_sync_all_counts() to correct.`,\\n          `Entity/${entityName}/record_count`,\\n        );\\n      }\\n    }\\n\\n    if (strictFk) {\\n      const edoc    = _loadJson(resolved);\\n      const fkFields = (edoc.Fields || []).filter(f => f.name.endsWith('_fk')).map(f => f.name);\\n      for (const fkField of fkFields) {\\n        const targetFound = Object.entries(pkMap).some(\\n          ([en, pk]) => pk && (fkField.includes(pk) || fkField.toLowerCase().includes(en.toLowerCase()))\\n        );\\n        if (!targetFound) {\\n          _mAddWarning(\\n            `Entity '${entityName}': FK field '${fkField}' has no matching primary_key ` +\\n            `declaration in the manifest. Consider adding a Relationships header (MFDB v1.1).`,\\n            `Entity/${entityName}/${fkField}`,\\n          );\\n        }\\n      }\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  return true;\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Validation report\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nfunction mfdb_validator_get_report(manifestPath, strictFk = false) {\\n  let valid = false;\\n  try {\\n    valid = mfdb_validator_validate_database(manifestPath, strictFk);\\n  } catch (_) {}\\n\\n  const lines = [\\n    '=== MFDB Validation Report ===',\\n    `Manifest : ${manifestPath}`,\\n    `Status   : ${valid ? 'VALID' : 'INVALID'}`,\\n    '',\\n    `Errors   : ${_mErrors.length}`,\\n  ];\\n  if (_mHasErrors()) {\\n    lines.push('---');\\n    lines.push(..._mErrors);\\n  }\\n  lines.push('', `Warnings : ${_mWarnings.length}`);\\n  if (_mHasWarnings()) {\\n    lines.push('---');\\n    lines.push(..._mWarnings);\\n  }\\n  return lines.join('\\\\n');\\n}\\n\\n// mfdb_validator_reset_state()      { _mReset(); }\\nfunction mfdb_validator_has_errors()       { return _mHasErrors(); }\\nfunction mfdb_validator_has_warnings()     { return _mHasWarnings(); }\\nfunction mfdb_validator_get_errors()       { return [..._mErrors]; }\\nfunction mfdb_validator_get_warnings()     { return [..._mWarnings]; }\\nfunction mfdb_validator_error_count()      { return _mErrors.length; }\\nfunction mfdb_validator_warning_count()    { return _mWarnings.length; }\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Exports (CommonJS mfdb_validator_warning_count,\\n};\\n\\nif (typeof module !== 'undefined' && module.exports) {\\n  module.exports = exports_;\\n}\\nif (typeof window !== 'undefined') {\\n  window.MFDB_VALIDATOR = exports_;\\n}\\n\",\n      false\n    ],\n    [\n      \"FileContent\",\n      null,\n      null,\n      null,\n      \"Lib_JS/Core/lib_bejson_validator.js\",\n      \"lib_bejson_validator.js\",\n      \"/**\\n * Library:        lib_bejson_validator.js\\n * Family:         Core\\n * Description:    Structural integrity checker for positional values and mandatory keys.\\n * Version:        2.0.2\\n * Library_Version: 204\\n * Date:           2026-05-18\\n * Author:         Elton Boehnen\\n * Contact:        eltonboehnen@gmail.com | boehnenelton2024.pages.dev | github.com/boehnenelton\\n * ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Error codes (mirror bash readonly values / Python constants)\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\nconst BEJSON_ERRORS = (typeof require !== 'undefined') \\n  ? require('./lib_bejson_errors.js') \\n  : (window.BEJSON_ERRORS || {});\\n\\nconst {\\n  E_INVALID_JSON,\\n  E_MISSING_MANDATORY_KEY,\\n  E_INVALID_FORMAT,\\n  E_INVALID_VERSION,\\n  E_INVALID_RECORDS_TYPE,\\n  E_INVALID_FIELDS,\\n  E_INVALID_VALUES,\\n  E_TYPE_MISMATCH,\\n  E_RECORD_LENGTH_MISMATCH,\\n  E_RESERVED_KEY_COLLISION,\\n  E_INVALID_RECORD_TYPE_PARENT,\\n  E_NULL_VIOLATION,\\n  E_FILE_NOT_FOUND,\\n  E_PERMISSION_DENIED,\\n  E_ATOMIC_WRITE_FAILED\\n} = BEJSON_ERRORS;\\n\\nconst VALID_VERSIONS    = new Set(['104', '104a', '104db']);\\nconst MANDATORY_KEYS    = ['Format', 'Format_Version', 'Format_Creator', 'Records_Type', 'Fields', 'Values'];\\nconst VALID_FIELD_TYPES = new Set(['string', 'integer', 'number', 'boolean', 'array', 'object']);\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Validation exception\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nclass BEJSONValidationError extends Error {\\n  \\n  constructor(message, code) {\\n    super(message);\\n    this.name = 'BEJSONValidationError';\\n    this.code = code;\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Validation state \\u2014 mirrors bash globals / Python ValidationState\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nclass ValidationState {\\n  constructor() {\\n    this.errors      = [];\\n    this.warnings    = [];\\n    this.currentFile = '';\\n  }\\n\\n  \\n  reset() {\\n    this.errors      = [];\\n    this.warnings    = [];\\n    this.currentFile = '';\\n  }\\n\\n  \\n  addError(message, location = '', context = '') {\\n    let entry = 'ERROR';\\n    if (location) entry += ` | Location: ${location}`;\\n    entry += ` | Message: ${message}`;\\n    if (context) entry += ` | Context: ${context}`;\\n    this.errors.push(entry);\\n  }\\n\\n  \\n  addWarning(message, location = '') {\\n    let entry = 'WARNING';\\n    if (location) entry += ` | Location: ${location}`;\\n    entry += ` | Message: ${message}`;\\n    this.warnings.push(entry);\\n  }\\n\\n  \\n  getErrors() { return [...this.errors]; }\\n\\n  \\n  getWarnings() { return [...this.warnings]; }\\n\\n  \\n  hasErrors() { return this.errors.length > 0; }\\n\\n  \\n  hasWarnings() { return this.warnings.length > 0; }\\n\\n  \\n  errorCount() { return this.errors.length; }\\n\\n  \\n  warningCount() { return this.warnings.length; }\\n}\\n\\n// Module-level default state (mirrors bash global arrays / Python _state)\\nconst _state = new ValidationState();\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Convenience accessors that operate on the module-level state\\n// (mirror the exported bash / Python functions)\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nfunction bejson_validator_reset_state() {\\n  _state.reset();\\n}\\n\\nfunction bejson_validator_get_errors() {\\n  return _state.getErrors();\\n}\\n\\nfunction bejson_validator_get_warnings() {\\n  return _state.getWarnings();\\n}\\n\\nfunction bejson_validator_has_errors() {\\n  return _state.hasErrors();\\n}\\n\\nfunction bejson_validator_has_warnings() {\\n  return _state.hasWarnings();\\n}\\n\\nfunction bejson_validator_error_count() {\\n  return _state.errorCount();\\n}\\n\\nfunction bejson_validator_warning_count() {\\n  return _state.warningCount();\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Dependency check (no-op in JS \\u2014 JSON is built-in)\\n// mirrors bejson_validator_check_dependencies\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nfunction bejson_validator_check_dependencies() {\\n  return true;\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// JSON syntax validation\\n// mirrors bejson_validator_check_json_syntax\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Environment Abstraction Layer\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nconst IO_PROVIDER = {\\n  readFile: (path) => {\\n    if (typeof require !== 'undefined') {\\n      try {\\n        const fs = require('fs');\\n        return fs.readFileSync(path, 'utf8');\\n      } catch (e) {\\n        throw new Error(`Node.js fs failed: ${e.message}`);\\n      }\\n    }\\n    throw new Error('File I/O not available in this environment');\\n  },\\n  exists: (path) => {\\n    if (typeof require !== 'undefined') {\\n      try {\\n        const fs = require('fs');\\n        return fs.existsSync(path);\\n      } catch (e) {\\n        return false;\\n      }\\n    }\\n    return false;\\n  }\\n};\\n\\nfunction bejson_validator_check_json_syntax(input, isFile = false) {\\n  let text;\\n\\n  if (isFile) {\\n    if (!IO_PROVIDER.exists(input)) {\\n      _state.addError(`File not found: ${input}`, 'File System');\\n      throw new BEJSONValidationError(`File not found: ${input}`, E_FILE_NOT_FOUND);\\n    }\\n    try {\\n      text = IO_PROVIDER.readFile(input);\\n      _state.currentFile = input;\\n    } catch (err) {\\n      _state.addError(`IO Error: ${err.message}`, 'File System');\\n      throw new BEJSONValidationError(`IO Error: ${err.message}`, E_FILE_NOT_FOUND);\\n    }\\n  } else {\\n    text = input;\\n  }\\n\\n  if (typeof text === 'object' && text !== null) {\\n    return text; // already parsed\\n  }\\n\\n  try {\\n    return JSON.parse(text);\\n  } catch (err) {\\n    _state.addError(`Invalid JSON syntax: ${err.message}`, 'JSON Parse');\\n    throw new BEJSONValidationError(`Invalid JSON syntax: ${err.message}`, E_INVALID_JSON);\\n  }\\n}\\n\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n// Mandatory key validation\\n// mirrors bejson_validator_check_mandatory_keys\\n// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nfunction bejson_validator_check_mandatory_keys(doc) {\\n  for (const key of MANDATORY_KEYS) {\\n    if (!(key in doc)) {\\n      _state.addError(`Missing mandatory top-level key: '${key}'`, 'Top-Level Keys');\\n      throw new BEJSONValidationError(`Missing mandatory key: ${key}`, E_MISSING_MANDATORY_KEY);\\n    }\\n  }\\n\\n  if (doc['Format'] !== 'BEJSON') {\\n    _state.addError(\\n      `Invalid 'Format' value: Expected 'BEJSON', got '${doc['Format']}'`,\\n      'Top-Level Keys/Format',\\n    );\\n    throw new BEJSONValidationError('Invalid Format', E_INVALID_FORMAT);\\n  }\\n\\n  const version = doc['Format_Version'] ?? Found ${count} entries.`,\\n        'Records_Type',\\n      );\\n      throw new BEJSONValidationError('Bad Records_Type', E_INVALID_RECORDS_TYPE);\\n    }\\n  } else if (version === '104db') {\\n    if (count < 2) {\\n      _state.addError(\\n        `For BEJSON 104db, 'Records_Type' must contain two or more unique strings. Found ${count} entries.`,\\n        'Records_Type',\\n      );\\n      throw new BEJSONValidationError('Bad Records_Type', E_INVALID_RECORDS_TYPE);\\n    }\\n    const seen = new Set();\\n    for (let i = 0;    key:      fields.findIndex(f => f.name === \\\"key\\\"),\\n            value:    fields.findIndex(f => f.name === \\\"value\\\"),\\n            timestamp:fields.findIndex(f => f.name === \\\"History\\\");\\n        this._historyIndex = historyRows.length - 1;\\n    }\\n\\n    _notify(path, newValue, oldValue) {\\n        if (this._listeners.has(path)) _triggerEffects(path) {\\n        const effectsToRun = new Set();\\n        if (this._dependencyGraph.has(path)) {\\n            this._dependencyGraph.get(path).forEach(e => effectsToRun.add(e));\\n        }\\n        \\n        // Handle nested paths (e.g., user.name triggers user)\\n        const parts = path.split('.');\\n        let currentPath = '';\\n        for (let i = 0; i < parts.length - 1; i++) {\\n            currentPath += (currentPath ? '.' : '') + parts[i];\\n            if (this._dependencyGraph.has(currentPath)) {\\n                this._dependencyGraph.get(currentPath).forEach(e => effectsToRun.add(e));\\n            }\\n        }\\n        \\n        effectsToRun.forEach(effect => effect());\\n    }\\n\\n    subscribe(path, callback) {\\n        if (!this._listeners.has(path)) this._listeners.set(path, new Set());\\n        this._listeners.get(path).add(callback);\\n        return () => this._listeners.get(path).delete(callback);\\n    }\\n\\n    effect(fn) {\\n        const runner = () => {\\n            this._cleanupEffect(runner);\\n            this._activeEffect = runner;\\n            try {\\n                fn(this.state);\\n            } finally {\\n                this._activeEffect = null;\\n            }\\n        };\\n        runner();\\n    }\\n\\n    undo() {\\n        // previously used hardcoded positional index [4] for the snapshot field.\\n        // This silently returns garbage if a field is ever inserted before snapshot.\\n        // Now resolved dynamically via the field map.\\n        const historyRows = this.bejson.Values.filter(r => r[0] === \\\"History\\\");\\n        if Object.keys(this.state).forEach(k => delete this.state[k]);\\n        Object.assign(this.state, snapshot);\\n        this._notify('*', this.state, null);\\n    }\\n}\\n\\n// --- Exports ---\\nconst \\\"PBKDF2\\\" }, false, [\\\"deriveKey\\\"]);\\n        return await crypto.subtle.deriveKey(\\n            { name: \\\"PBKDF2\\\", salt: salt, iterations: 100000, hash: \\\"SHA-256\\\" },\\n            keyMaterial,\\n            { name: \\\"AES-GCM\\\", length: 256 },\\n            false,\\n            [\\\"encrypt\\\", \\\"decrypt\\\"]\\n        );\\n    },\\n\\n    ab2base64(buf) { const b = this.bodies.Values.push([\\n            id, x, y, w, h, \\n            options.vx || 0, options.vy || 0, \\n            options.isStatic || false, \\n            options.mass || 1\\n        ]);\\n    }\\n\\n    moveBody(id, dx, dy, staticColliders = []) {\\n        const b = this.bodies.Values.find(v => v[0] === id);\\n        if (!b) return;\\n\\n        const oldX = b[1];\\n        b[1] += / mass;\\n        imp.y += iy / mass;\\n    }\\n\\n    step(dt, staticColliders = []) {\\n        const values = this.bodies.Values;\\n\\n        for (let i = 0; i < values.length; i++) {\\n            const b = values[i];\\n            if (b[7]) continue; // isStatic\\n\\n            // 1. Apply Impulses\\n            if (this.impulses.has(b[0])) {\\n                const imp = this.impulses.get(b[0]);\\n                b[5] += imp.x;\\n                b[6] += imp.y;\\n                this.impulses.delete(b[0]);\\n            }\\n\\n            // 2. Apply Gravity & Friction\\n            b[5] += this.gravity.x * dt;\\n            b[6] += this.gravity.y * dt;\\n            b[5] *= this.friction;\\n            b[6] *= this.friction;\\n\\n            // 3.
