      const bB = values[j];\\n                if (this._checkAABB(bA, bB)) {\\n                    this._resolveCollision(bA, bB);\\n                }\\n            }\\n        }\\n    }\\n\\n    _checkStaticCollisions(b, colliders) {\\n        for (const c of colliders) {\\n            const cx = Array.isArray(c) ? c[0] : c.x;\\n            const cy = Array.isArray(c) ? c[1] : c.y;\\n            const cw = Array.isArray(c) ? c[2] : (c.w || c.width);\\n            const ch = Array.isArray(c) ? c[3] : (c.h || c.height);\\n\\n            if (b[1] < cx + cw && b[1] + b[3] > cx && b[2] < cy + ch && b[2] + b[4] > cy) {\\n                return true;\\n            }\\n        }\\n        return false;\\n    }\\n\\n    _checkAABB(a, b) {\\n        return (a[1] < b[1] + b[3] && a[1] + a[3] > b[1] && a[2] < b[2] + b[4] && a[2] + a[4] > b[2]);\\n    }\\n\\n    _resolveCollision(a, b) {\\n        if (a[7] && b[7]) return;\\n        \\n        const m1 = a[8] || 1;\\n        const m2 = b[8] || 1;\\n        const totalMass = m1 + m2;\\n\\n        const v1x = a[5];\\n        const v1y = a[6];\\n        const v2x = b[5];\\n        const v2y = b[6];\\n\\n        if (a[7]) { // a is static\\n            b[5] = -v2x; b[6] = -v2y;\\n            return;\\n        }\\n        if (b[7]) { // b is static\\n            a[5] = -v1x; a[6] = -v1y;\\n            return;\\n        }\\n\\n        // Elastic collision formula for 1D applied to each axis\\n        a[5] = ((v1x * (m1 - m2)) + (2 * m2 * v2x)) / totalMass;\\n        b[5] = ((v2x * (m2 - m1)) + (2 * m1 * v1x)) / totalMass;\\n\\n        a[6] = ((v1y * (m1 - m2)) + (2 * m2 * v2y)) / totalMass;\\n        b[6] = ((v2y * (m2 - m1)) + (2 * m1 * v1y)) / totalMass;\\n    }\\n}\\n\\nBEJSON_Switch.Physics = SwitchPhysics;\\nexport default Use 'C' or 'Heal' if your health drops.\\\"; }\\n                else {\\n                    this.sword = {\\n                        x: this.player.x + this.player.facing.x * 24, y: this.player.y + this.player.facing.y * 24,\\n                        width: 48, height: 48, damage: (this.assets['sword'] || {damage: 10}).damage, life: 0.2\\n                    };\\n                }\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        if (this.input._isBoundJustPressed('menu')) {\\n            if (this.state === 'PLAYING') { this.stateStatus = 'MENU'; \\n        const levelHeight = this.level.height * this.tileSize;\\n\\n        const checkTileCollision = (actor, vx, vy) => {\\n            const newX = actor.x + vx * dt; const newY = actor.y + vy * dt;\\n            for (const tile of this.tiles) {\\n                const rules = this.assets[tile.terrain_type || tile.object_type];\\n                if (rules && rules.is_solid && newX < tile.x * this.tileSize + this.tileSize && newX + actor.width > tile.x * this.tileSize && newY < tile.y * this.tileSize + this.tileSize && newY + actor.height > tile.y * this.tileSize) return true;\\n            }\\n            return false;\\n        };\\n\\n        let pKnockX = 0, pKnockY = 0;\\n        \\n        for (let i = this.actors.length - 1; \\n                    actor.pendingVy += this.player.facing.y * 400;\\n                    if (actor.health <= 0) {\\n                        this.actors.splice(i, 1); this.scoreValue += actor.xpReward; \\n                         pKnockY += ((this.player.y - actor.y)/len) * 500;\\n                         if (this.player.health <= 0) this.stateStatus = 'GAMEOVER';\\n                    }\\n                }\\n            }\\n        }\\n\\n        let speedMult = 1;\\n        const currentTile = this.tiles.find(t => t.x === Math.floor((this.player.x + this.player.width/2) / this.tileSize) && t.y === Math.floor((this.player.y + this.player.height/2) / this.tileSize));\\n        if (currentTile && (currentTile.terrain_type || currentTile.object_type) === 'bush') speedMult = 0.5;\\n        const currentSpeed = this.player.speed * speedMult;\\n        \\n        let targetVx = inp.x;\\n        let targetVy = inp.y;\\n        \\n        let finalVx = targetVx * currentSpeed + pKnockX;\\n        let finalVy = targetVy * currentSpeed + pKnockY;\\n        \\n        let canMoveX = !checkTileCollision(this.player, finalVx, 0);\\n        let canMoveY = !checkTileCollision(this.player, 0, finalVy);\\n\\n        if (!canMoveX) { targetVx = 0; \\n            finalVx = (targetVx / length) * currentSpeed + pKnockX; \\n            finalVy = (targetVy / length) * currentSpeed + pKnockY; \\n            if (checkTileCollision(this.player, finalVx, 0)) finalVx = 0;\\n            if (checkTileCollision(this.player, 0, finalVy)) finalVy = 0;\\n        }\\n\\n        this.player.vx = finalVx;\\n        this.player.vy = finalVy;\\n\\n        if (targetVx !== 0 || targetVy !== 0) this.player.facing = { x: targetVx === 0 ? 0 : Math.sign(targetVx), y: targetVy === 0 ? 0 : Math.sign(targetVy) };\\n        \\n        this.player.x = Math.max(0, Math.min(this.player.x + this.player.vx * dt, levelWidth - this.player.width));\\n        this.player.y = Math.max(0, Math.min(this.player.y + this.player.vy * dt, levelHeight - this.player.height));\\n        \\n        if (this.sword) {\\n            this.sword.life -= dt;\\n            this.sword.x = this.player.x + this.player.facing.x * 24; \\n        const scale = this.renderer.canvas.width / this.camera.width; \\n        ctx.scale(scale, scale); \\n        ctx.translate(-this.camera.x, -this.camera.y);\\n        \\n        // Draw tiles\\n        this.tiles.forEach(tile => {\\n            const tx = tile.x * this.tileSize; MdFileNotFoundError(str(p))\\n\\n    fid        = _file_id(str(p))\\n    index_path = str(Path(db_root) / \\\"data\\\" / f\\\"{fid}.chunk_index.bejson\\\")\\n\\n    # Build the chunk index for this file\\n    index_doc = md_indexer_build_index_doc(str(p))\\n    bejson_core_atomic_write(index_path, index_doc)\\n\\n    line_count  = sum(1 for _ in p.open(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\"))\\n    chunk_count = len(index_doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []))\\n\\n    # Update MarkdownFile entity\\n    file_doc = _load_file_entity(db_root)\\n    fi       = bejson_core_get_field_map(file_doc)\\n\\n    fid_idx   = fi.get(\\\"file_id\\\",   _FILE_LEGACY[\\\"file_id\\\"])\\n    fpath_idx = fi.get(\\\"file_path\\\", _FILE_LEGACY[\\\"file_path\\\"])\\n\\n    # Check if already registered\\n    existing_idx = None\\n    for i, row in enumerate(file_doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", [])):\\n        if row[fpath_idx] == str(p):\\n            existing_idx = i\\n            break\\n\\n    new_row = [None] * len(FILE_FIELDS)\\n    new_row[_FILE_LEGACY[\\\"file_id\\\"]]      = fid\\n    new_row[_FILE_LEGACY[\\\"file_path\\\"]]    = str(p)\\n    new_row[_FILE_LEGACY[\\\"index_path\\\"]]   = index_path\\n    new_row[_FILE_LEGACY[\\\"last_indexed\\\"]] = _now()\\n    new_row[_FILE_LEGACY[\\\"line_count\\\"]]   = line_count\\n    new_row[_FILE_LEGACY[\\\"chunk_count\\\"]]  = chunk_count\\n    new_row[_FILE_LEGACY[\\\"description\\\"]]  = description or None\\n    new_row[_FILE_LEGACY[\\\"tags\\\"]]         = list(tags) if tags else []\\n\\n    if existing_idx is not None:\\n        file_doc[\\\"Values\\\"][existing_idx] = new_row\\n    else:\\n        file_doc[\\\"Values\\\"].append(new_row)\\n\\n    _save_file_entity(db_root, file_doc)\\n\\n    # Sync the flat chunk entity\\n    _sync_chunk_entity(db_root, str(p), fid, index_doc)\\n\\n    return fid\\n\\ndef md_db_list_files(db_root: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Return all registered files as a list of dicts.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    file_doc = _load_file_entity(db_root)\\n    fields   = [f[\\\"name\\\"] for f in file_doc.get(\\\"Fields\\\", [])]\\n    result   = []\\n    for row in file_doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []):\\n        item = {fields[i]: row[i] for i in range(min(len(fields), len(row)))}\\n        result.append(item)\\n    return result\\n\\ndef md_db_get_file(db_root: str, file_id: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Return a file record dict by file_id, or None if not found.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    for f in md_db_list_files(db_root):\\n        if f.get(\\\"file_id\\\") == file_id:\\n            return f\\n    return None\\n\\ndef md_db_get_index_path(db_root: str, file_id: str) -> Optional[str]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the index_path for a registered file, or None.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    rec = md_db_get_file(db_root, file_id)\\n    return rec.get(\\\"index_path\\\") if rec else None\\n\\ndef md_db_reindex_file(db_root: str, file_id: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Reindex a registered file, refreshing its chunk index and syncing\\n    the flat chunk entity.\\n\\n    Returns the new index document.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n fi.get(\\\"file_path\\\", _FILE_LEGACY[\\\"file_path\\\"])\\n    ipath_idx = fi.get(\\\"index_path\\\", _FILE_LEGACY[\\\"index_path\\\"])\\n    ts_idx    = fi.get(\\\"last_indexed\\\", _FILE_LEGACY[\\\"last_indexed\\\"])\\n    lc_idx    = fi.get(\\\"line_count\\\", _FILE_LEGACY[\\\"line_count\\\"])\\n    cc_idx    = fi.get(\\\"chunk_count\\\", _FILE_LEGACY[\\\"chunk_count\\\"])\\n\\n    for row in file_doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []):\\n        if row[fid_idx] == file_id:\\n            file_path  = row[fpath_idx]\\n            index_path = row[ipath_idx]\\n\\n            new_doc = md_indexer_reindex_file(file_path, index_path, preserve_metadata=True)\\n\\n            # Update file registry metadata\\n            row[ts_idx] = _now()\\n            row[lc_idx] = sum(1 for _ in Path(file_path).open(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\"))\\n            row[cc_idx] = len(new_doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []))\\n\\n            _save_file_entity(db_root, file_doc)\\n            _sync_chunk_entity(db_root, file_path, file_id, new_doc)\\n\\n            return new_doc\\n\\n    raise MFDBWrapperError(f\\\"file_id '{file_id}' not found in MarkdownFile registry\\\")\\n\\ndef md_db_reindex_all(db_root: str) -> Dict[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Reindex all registered files. results\\n\\ndef md_db_unregister_file(db_root: str, file_id: str) -> bool:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Remove a file from the registry and clean up its index file.\\n    Does NOT delete the actual _FILE_LEGACY[\\\"file_path\\\"])\\n\\n    new_values = []\\n    removed    = None\\n\\n    for row in file_doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []):\\n        if row[fid_idx] == file_id:\\n            removed = bool]] = None,\\n) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Assemble content from all registered files matching the filters.\\n    Uses the flat MarkdownChunk entity for cross-file queries.\\n\\n    Args:\\n        db_root:     Root directory of the Markdown MFDB.\\n        tags:        If provided, include only chunks with ANY of these tags.\\n        active_only: Include only is_active=True chunks.\\n        separator:   String between assembled chunks.\\n        predicate:   Optional callable(chunk_dict) -> bool.\\n\\n    Returns:\\n        Assembled string content.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    chunk_doc = _load_chunk_entity(db_root)\\n    all_chunks = md_indexer_list_chunks(chunk_doc)\\n\\n    if active_only:\\n        all_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if c.get(\\\"is_active\\\") is True]\\n\\n    if tags:\\n        tag_set    = set(tags)\\n        all_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if set(c.get(\\\"tags\\\") or []) & tag_set]\\n\\n    if predicate:\\n        all_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if predicate(c)]\\n\\n    if not all_chunks:\\n        raise AssembleEmptyError(f\\\"tags={tags}, active_only={active_only}\\\")\\n\\n    all_chunks.sort(key=lambda c: (\\n        c.get(\\\"sort_order\\\") if c.get(\\\"sort_order\\\") is not None else 9999,\\n        c.get(\\\"file_path\\\") or \\\"\\\",\\n        c.get(\\\"start_line\\\") or 0,\\n    ))\\n\\n    parts = []\\n chunk[\\\"end_line\\\"]\\n\\n            p = Path(file_path)\\n            if not p.exists():\\n                logging.warning(f\\\"[lib_md_db] Skipping chunk '{chunk['chunk_id']}' \\u2014 file missing\\\")\\n                continue\\n\\n            lines   = p.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\").splitlines(keepends=True)\\n            segment = \\\"\\\".join(lines[start_line:end_line])\\n            parts.append(segment)\\n        except Exception as e:\\n            logging.warning(f\\\"[lib_md_db] Skipping chunk '{chunk.get('chunk_id')}': {e}\\\")\\n\\n    if not parts:\\n        raise prompts and policy documents.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    return md_db_assemble(db_root, tags=[tag], active_only=True, separator=separator)\\n\\n# registry\\\")\\n    return md_ops_pull(index_path, chunk_id, verify_checksum=verify_checksum)\\n\\ndef md_db_inject(db_root: str, file_id: str, chunk_id: str, new_content: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n '{file_id}' not found in registry\\\")\\n    new_doc = md_ops_inject(index_path, chunk_id, new_content)\\n    # Re-sync flat entity after inject (index was rewritten by md_ops_inject)\\n    file_rec = \"README.md\",\n      \"# lib_bejson_markdown \\u2014 Lib_MD v1.0.0\\n\\n**Author:** Elton Boehnen  \\n**Contact:** eltonboehnen@gmail.com \\u00b7 boehnenelton2024.pages.dev \\u00b7 [github.com/boehnenelton](https://github.com/boehnenelton)  \\n**RELATIONAL_ID:** md-lib-readme-20260615-001  \\n**MFDB Version:** 1.31  \\n**Date:** 2026-06-15\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Overview\\n\\n`lib_bejson_markdown` is a standalone Python library that treats markdown files as **addressable content stores**. It scans markdown structure, maps every block (headings, code fences, prose, frontmatter) to a stable line-range address, and backs all metadata in BEJSON 104 / MFDB v1.31 format.\\n\\nThe primary use case is **policy and instruction set management**: define chunks once, tag them, toggle them active or inactive, and assemble them on demand into system prompts, policy documents, or any other output format.\\n\\n**This library does not modify any existing Lib_PY/Lib_JS/Lib_TS libraries.**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Architecture\\n\\n```\\nLib_MD_v1/\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 lib_md_errors.py        \\u2014 Custom exception classes (error codes 70\\u201389)\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 lib_md_indexer.py       \\u2014 Markdown scanner and BEJSON 104 index factory\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 lib_md_ops.py           \\u2014 pull / inject / toggle / assemble operations\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 lib_md_db.py            \\u2014 MFDB v1.31 wrapper for multi-file management\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 config.json             \\u2014 BEJSON 104a library configuration\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 demo.py                 \\u2014 Runnable demonstration script\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 README.md               \\u2014 This document\\n\\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 lib/\\n    \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 (copy Core/*.py from Lib_PY here, or add Core to sys.path)\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Dependencies\\n\\nThis library requires `lib_bejson_core.py` and `lib_mfdb_core.py` from your **Lib_PY v2 Core** family. There are two ways to satisfy this:\\n\\n**Option A \\u2014 local lib/ folder (portable, recommended for standalone scripts):**\\n```\\ncp /path/to/Lib_PY/Core/lib_bejson_core.py   Lib_MD_v1/lib/\\ncp /path/to/Lib_PY/Core/lib_mfdb_core.py     Lib_MD_v1/lib/\\ncp /path/to/Lib_PY/Core/lib_bejson_env.py    Lib_MD_v1/lib/\\ncp /path/to/Lib_PY/Core/lib_bejson_errors.py Lib_MD_v1/lib/\\ncp /path/to/Lib_PY/Core/lib_mfdb_validator.py Lib_MD_v1/lib/\\ncp /path/to/Lib_PY/Core/lib_bejson_path_guard.py Lib_MD_v1/lib/\\n```\\n\\n**Option B \\u2014 sys.path (when embedded in a larger project):**\\n```python\\nimport sys\\nsys.path.insert(0, \\\"/path/to/Lib_PY/Core\\\")\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n## BEJSON Document Format\\n\\n### MarkdownChunk Index (BEJSON 104)\\n\\nEach markdown file gets its own `*.chunk_index.bejson` document with `Records_Type: [\\\"MarkdownChunk\\\"]`.\\n\\n| Field        | Type    | Description |\\n|:-------------|:--------|:------------|\\n| `chunk_id`   | string  | Stable identifier derived from file stem + type + line + seq |\\n| `file_path`  | string  | Absolute path to the markdown file |\\n| `start_line` | integer | 0-based inclusive line start |\\n| `end_line`   | integer | 0-based exclusive line end (Python slice convention) |\\n| `chunk_type` | string  | `heading`, `code_block`, `frontmatter`, `policy`, `raw` |\\n| `label`      | string  | Human-readable chunk name |\\n| `is_active`  | boolean | Participates in assemble operations when True |\\n| `tags`       | array   | String tags for filtering |\\n| `sort_order` | integer | Assembly sequence, independent of line position |\\n| `injected_at`| string  | ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of last injection |\\n| `checksum`   | string  | SHA-256[:16] of raw chunk content |\\n\\n### MarkdownFile Registry (MFDB entity \\u2014 BEJSON 104)\\n\\n| Field          | Type    | Description |\\n|:---------------|:--------|:------------|\\n| `file_id`      | string  | Stable slug from file stem |\\n| `file_path`    | string  | Absolute path to markdown file |\\n| `index_path`   | string  | Path to the per-file chunk index document |\\n| `last_indexed` | string  | ISO 8601 UTC timestamp |\\n| `line_count`   | integer | Total lines at last index |\\n| `chunk_count`  | integer | Number of chunks at last index |\\n| `description`  | string  | Optional human label |\\n| `tags`         | array   | File-level tags |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Module Reference\\n\\n### `lib_md_indexer` \\u2014 Scanner\\n\\n#### `md_indexer_scan_file(file_path) -> List[Dict]`\\nScan a markdown file and return raw chunk dicts. Pure read \\u2014 no writes.\\n\\n#### `md_indexer_build_index_doc(file_path, default_active=True) -> Dict`\\nScan file and return a complete BEJSON 104 MarkdownChunk document. Does not write.\\n\\n#### `md_indexer_save_index(index_doc, index_path) -> bool`\\nAtomically write an index document to disk.\\n\\n#### `md_indexer_reindex_file(file_path, index_path, preserve_metadata=True) -> Dict`\\nFull reindex. Preserves tags, is_active, sort_order from the previous index via best-effort matching (chunk_id first, then proximity \\u00b15 lines). Always recomputes checksums.\\n\\n#### `md_indexer_list_chunks(index_doc) -> List[Dict]`\\nReturn all chunks as a list of named dicts. Convenience for inspection.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### `lib_md_ops` \\u2014 Operations\\n\\n#### `md_ops_pull(index_path, chunk_id, verify_checksum=True) -> str`\\nPull raw text content of a chunk. Raises `ChunkDriftError` if checksum mismatches and `verify_checksum=True`.\\n\\n#### `md_ops_inject(index_path, chunk_id, new_content) -> Dict`\\nReplace a chunk's content. Writes file atomically then performs a full reindex. Returns the new index document.\\n\\n#### `md_ops_toggle(index_path, chunk_id, active=None) -> bool`\\nFlip or explicitly set `is_active`. Returns the new value.\\n\\n#### `md_ops_toggle_by_tag(index_path, tag, active) -> List[str]`\\nSet `is_active` for every chunk with a matching tag. Returns list of modified chunk_ids.\\n\\n#### `md_ops_set_tags(index_path, chunk_id, tags)`\\nSet the `tags` array for a specific chunk.\\n\\n#### `md_ops_set_sort_order(index_path, chunk_id, sort_order)`\\nSet the `sort_order` for a specific chunk.\\n\\n#### `md_ops_assemble(index_path, tags=None, active_only=True, separator=\\\"\\\\n\\\", predicate=None) -> str`\\nAssemble chunks matching filters into a single string. Sorted by sort_order.\\n\\n#### `md_ops_assemble_by_tag(index_path, tag, separator=\\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\") -> str`\\nShorthand: assemble all active chunks with a specific tag.\\n\\n#### `md_ops_list_chunks(index_path, active_only=False, tag=None) -> List[Dict]`\\nReturn filtered, sorted chunk dicts from an index.\\n\\n#### `md_ops_reindex(file_path, index_path) -> Dict`\\nPublic reindex wrapper. Safe to call anytime \\u2014 idempotent.\\n\\n---\\n\\n### `lib_md_db` \\u2014 MFDB Multi-File Manager\\n\\n#### `md_db_create(db_root, db_name, description) -> str`\\nCreate a new Markdown MFDB database. Returns manifest path.\\n\\n#### `md_db_exists(db_root) -> bool`\\nCheck if a valid Markdown MFDB exists at db_root.\\n\\n#### `md_db_register_file(db_root, file_path, description, tags) -> str`\\nRegister and index a markdown file. Returns file_id. Updates in place if already registered.\\n\\n#### `md_db_list_files(db_root) -> List[Dict]`\\nReturn all registered files.\\n\\n#### `md_db_get_file(db_root, file_id) -> Optional[Dict]`\\nReturn a file record by file_id.\\n\\n#### `md_db_reindex_file(db_root, file_id) -> Dict`\\nReindex a registered file, syncing the flat chunk entity.\\n\\n#### `md_db_reindex_all(db_root) -> Dict[str, Dict]`\\nReindex all registered files. Returns {file_id: new_doc}.\\n\\n#### `md_db_unregister_file(db_root, file_id) -> bool`\\nRemove file from registry and clean up its index. Does NOT delete the markdown file.\\n\\n#### `md_db_assemble(db_root, tags, active_only, separator, predicate) -> str`\\nAssemble across all registered files using the flat chunk entity.\\n\\n#### `md_db_assemble_by_tag(db_root, tag, separator) -> str`\\nShorthand cross-file assembly by tag.\\n\\n#### `md_db_pull(db_root, file_id, chunk_id) -> str`\\nPull a chunk using file_id + chunk_id.\\n\\n#### `md_db_inject(db_root, file_id, chunk_id, new_content) -> Dict`\\nInject into a chunk and sync flat entity.\\n\\n#### `md_db_toggle(db_root, file_id, chunk_id, active) -> bool`\\nToggle is_active and sync flat entity.\\n\\n#### `md_db_set_tags(db_root, file_id, chunk_id, tags)`\\nSet tags and sync flat entity.\\n\\n#### `md_db_list_chunks(db_root, file_id, active_only, tag) -> List[Dict]`\\nList chunks for a specific registered file.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## Error Reference\\n\\nAll errors inherit from `MarkdownLibError(Exception)`.\\n\\n| Code | Class | Trigger |\\n|:-----|:------|:--------|\\n| 70 | `ChunkNotFoundError` | chunk_id not in index |\\n| 71 | `FileNotFoundError` (MD) | markdown file missing from disk |\\n| 72 | `IndexNotFoundError` | index document missing or unreadable |\\n| 73 | `ChunkDriftError` | checksum mismatch \\u2014 file edited out-of-band |\\n| 74 | `IndexStaleError` | index known stale after failed write |\\n| 75 | `WriteFailedError` | atomic write failed |\\n| 76 | `InjectRangeInvalidError` | start/end line out of bounds |\\n| 77 | `AssembleEmptyError` | no chunks matched filters |\\n| 78 | `MFDBWrapperError` | MFDB layer error |\\n| 79 | `InvalidDocumentError` | BEJSON doc fails structural check |\\n\\n---\\n\\n## The Offset Drift Problem\\n\\nLine numbers are a fragile address space. Every external edit to a markdown file can shift every subsequent chunk's address. This library handles drift via:\\n\\n1. **Checksum detection** \\u2014 every `pull` verifies the chunk's stored SHA-256[:16] against the actual file content. A mismatch raises `ChunkDriftError`.\\n2. **Full reindex after write** \\u2014 every `inject` call performs a complete reindex immediately after the file write, within the same call. There is no incremental offset tracking.\\n3. **Metadata preservation** \\u2014 reindex preserves `tags`, `is_active`, and `sort_order` from the previous index via chunk_id matching with proximity fallback (\\u00b15 lines).\\n\\n**The index is ephemeral metadata derived from the file. The file is always the source of truth.**\\n\\n---\\n\\n## System Prompt / Policy Use Case\\n\\nThis is the primary motivating use case. Example flow:\\n\\n```python\\nfrom lib_md_ops import (\\n    md_indexer_reindex_file,\\n    md_ops_set_tags,\\n    md_ops_toggle_by_tag,\\n    md_ops_assemble_by_tag,\\n    md_ops_list_chunks,\\n)\\n\\nINDEX = \\\"my_prompt.chunk_index.bejson\\\"\\n\\n# 1. Index your system prompt file\\nmd_indexer_reindex_file(\\\"GEMINI.md\\\", INDEX)\\n\\n# 2. Inspect what was found\\nfor chunk in md_ops_list_chunks(INDEX):\\n    print(chunk[\\\"chunk_id\\\"], chunk[\\\"chunk_type\\\"], chunk[\\\"label\\\"])\\n\\n# 3. Tag the chunks you want in your active system prompt\\nmd_ops_set_tags(INDEX, \\\"gemini_coding_persona_0_0\\\",  [\\\"gemini_context\\\"])\\nmd_ops_set_tags(INDEX, \\\"gemini_rules_heading_5_1\\\",   [\\\"gemini_context\\\"])\\nmd_ops_set_tags(INDEX, \\\"gemini_python_rules_raw_8_2\\\", [\\\"gemini_context\\\", \\\"python\\\"])\\n\\n# 4. Toggle a chunk off (e.g., suppress a section)\\nmd_ops_toggle_by_tag(INDEX, \\\"python\\\", active=False)\\n\\n# 5. Assemble the active system prompt\\nsystem_prompt = md_ops_assemble_by_tag(INDEX, \\\"gemini_context\\\")\\nprint(system_prompt)\\n```\\n\\nThe assembled string can be passed directly to any AI API as the system instruction. Switching context (e.g., from coding to writing) is a `toggle_by_tag` call \\u2014 not a file edit.\\n\\n---\\n\\n## MFDB Database Layout\\n\\nWhen using `lib_md_db` for multi-file management:\\n\\n```\\nmarkdown_db/\\n\\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 104a.mfdb.bejson          \\u2190 manifest\\n\\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 data/\\n    \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 markdown_file.bejson  \\u2190 file registry entity (BEJSON 104)\\n    \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 markdown_chunk.bejson \\u2190 flat cross-file chunk index (BEJSON 104)\\n    \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 gemini.chunk_index.bejson\\n    \\u251c\\u2500\\u2500 policy.chunk_index.bejson\\n    \\u2514\\u2500\\u2500 ...                   \\u2190 per-file chunk indexes\\n```\\n\\n---\\n\\n*lib_bejson_markdown v1.0.0 \\u2014 Elton Boehnen \\u2014 eltonboehnen@gmail.com \\u2014 boehnenelton2024.pages.dev \\u2014 github.com/boehnenelton*\\n\",\n      false\n    ],\n    [\n      \"FileContent\",\n      null,\n      null,\n      null,\n      \"Lib_PY/MD/lib_md_ops.py\",\n      \"lib_md_ops.py\",\n      \"\\\"\\\"\\\"\\nLibrary:        lib_md_ops.py\\nFamily:         MD\\nDescription:    The four core operations for the lib_bejson_markdown library: pull, inject, toggle, assemble. Offset drift is handled via full-reindex after every write \\u2014 never via incremental offset tracking. RELATIONAL_ID: md-lib-ops-20260615-001\\nVersion:        1.0.0\\nLibrary_Version: 204\\nDate:           2026-06-15\\nAuthor:         Elton Boehnen\\nContact:        eltonboehnen@gmail.com | boehnenelton2024.pages.dev | github.com/boehnenelton\\nFormat_Creator: Elton Boehnen\\nRELATIONAL_ID:  7292f83c-0f0f-4f92-8a86-99db3a32cc58\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nimport os\\nimport sys\\nimport tempfile\\nimport logging\\nfrom datetime import datetime, timezone\\nfrom pathlib import Path\\nfrom typing import Any, Callable, Dict, List, Optional\\n\\n# bejson_core_load_file,\\n        bejson_core_atomic_write,\\n        bejson_core_get_field_map,\\n    )\\nexcept ImportError as _e:\\n    raise ImportError(\\n        \\\"[lib_md_ops] Cannot import lib_bejson_core. \\\"\\n        \\\"Copy your Lib_PY/Core files into Lib_MD/lib/ or add Core to sys.path. \\\"\\n        f\\\"Original error: {_e}\\\"\\n    )\\n\\nfrom lib_md_errors import (\\n    ChunkNotFoundError,\\n    FileNotFoundError    as MdFileNotFoundError,\\n    IndexNotFoundError,\\n    ChunkDriftError,\\n    WriteFailedError,\\n    InjectRangeInvalidError,\\n    AssembleEmptyError,\\n    InvalidDocumentError,\\n)\\nfrom lib_md_indexer import (\\n    _checksum,\\n    _now_iso,\\n    _CHUNK_LEGACY,\\n    md_indexer_reindex_file,\\n    md_indexer_get_chunk_row,\\n    md_indexer_list_chunks,\\n    md_indexer_save_index,\\n    CHUNK_FIELDS,\\n)\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Version\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nVERSION = \\\"1.0.0\\\"\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Internal helpers\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef _load_index(index_path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Load and minimally validate a MarkdownChunk index document.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    p = Path(index_path)\\n    if not p.exists():\\n        raise IndexNotFoundError(index_path)\\n    doc = bejson_core_load_file(index_path)\\n    if not doc or not isinstance(doc, dict):\\n        raise InvalidDocumentError(index_path, \\\"bejson_core_load_file returned None or non-dict\\\")\\n    rt = doc.get(\\\"Records_Type\\\", [])\\n    if not rt or rt[0] != \\\"MarkdownChunk\\\":\\n        raise InvalidDocumentError(index_path, f\\\"Records_Type must be ['MarkdownChunk'], got {rt}\\\")\\n    return doc\\n\\ndef _read_file_lines(file_path: str) -> List[str]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Read a file as a list of lines (with line endings preserved).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    p = Path(file_path)\\n    if not p.exists():\\n        raise MdFileNotFoundError(file_path)\\n    return p.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\").splitlines(keepends=True)\\n\\ndef _write_file_atomic(file_path: str, lines: List[str]) -> None:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Write a list of lines to a file atomically (temp \\u2192 rename).\\n    Raises WriteFailedError on any failure.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    target = Path(file_path).resolve()\\n    target_dir = target.parent\\n\\n    try:\\n        fd, tmp_path = tempfile.mkstemp(dir=str(target_dir), suffix=\\\".md.tmp\\\")\\n        with os.fdopen(fd, \\\"w\\\", encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\") as f:\\n            f.writelines(lines)\\n            f.flush()\\n            os.fsync(f.fileno())\\n        os.replace(tmp_path, str(target))\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        if \\\"tmp_path\\\" in dir() and os.path.exists(tmp_path):\\n            try:\\n                os.unlink(tmp_path)\\n            except OSError:\\n                pass\\n        raise WriteFailedError(file_path, str(e))\\n\\ndef _get_chunk_fields(doc: Dict[str, Any]):\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Return field indices for a MarkdownChunk index document.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    fi = bejson_core_get_field_map(doc)\\n    return {\\n        \\\"chunk_id\\\":    fi.get(\\\"chunk_id\\\",    _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"chunk_id\\\"]),\\n        \\\"file_path\\\":   fi.get(\\\"file_path\\\",   _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"file_path\\\"]),\\n        \\\"start_line\\\":  fi.get(\\\"start_line\\\",  _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"start_line\\\"]),\\n        \\\"end_line\\\":    fi.get(\\\"end_line\\\",    _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"end_line\\\"]),\\n        \\\"chunk_type\\\":  fi.get(\\\"chunk_type\\\",  _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"chunk_type\\\"]),\\n        \\\"label\\\":       fi.get(\\\"label\\\",       _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"label\\\"]),\\n        \\\"is_active\\\":   fi.get(\\\"is_active\\\",   _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"is_active\\\"]),\\n        \\\"tags\\\":        fi.get(\\\"tags\\\",        _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"tags\\\"]),\\n        \\\"sort_order\\\":  fi.get(\\\"sort_order\\\",  _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"sort_order\\\"]),\\n        \\\"injected_at\\\": fi.get(\\\"injected_at\\\", _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"injected_at\\\"]),\\n        \\\"checksum\\\":    fi.get(\\\"checksum\\\",    _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"checksum\\\"]),\\n    }\\n\\ndef _row_to_dict(row: List, fi: Dict[str, int]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Convert a raw BEJSON row to a named dict using a field-index map.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    return {name: (row[idx] if idx < len(row) else None) for name, idx in fi.items()}\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Operation 1 \\u2014 PULL\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef md_ops_pull(\\n    index_path: str,\\n    chunk_id: str,\\n    verify_checksum: bool = True,\\n) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Pull the raw text content of a chunk from its markdown file.\\n\\n    Args:\\n        index_path:       Path to the MarkdownChunk BEJSON 104 index file.\\n        chunk_id:         The chunk_id to retrieve.\\n        verify_checksum:  If True, raises ChunkDriftError when checksum mismatches.\\n                          Set False only when you know the file has changed and\\n                          are about to call reindex anyway.\\n\\n    Returns:\\n        The raw text content of the chunk (including trailing newline if present).\\n\\n    Raises:\\n        IndexNotFoundError   \\u2014 index file missing\\n        ChunkNotFoundError   \\u2014 chunk_id not in index\\n        MdFileNotFoundError  \\u2014 markdown file missing\\n        ChunkDriftError      \\u2014 checksum mismatch (only when verify_checksum=True)\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    doc = _load_index(index_path)\\n    fi  = _get_chunk_fields(doc)\\n\\n    row = md_indexer_get_chunk_row(doc, chunk_id)\\n    if row is None:\\n        raise ChunkNotFoundError(chunk_id)\\n\\n    chunk = _row_to_dict(row, fi)\\n    file_path  = chunk[\\\"file_path\\\"]\\n    start_line = chunk[\\\"start_line\\\"]\\n    end_line   = chunk[\\\"end_line\\\"]\\n    stored_cs  = chunk[\\\"checksum\\\"]\\n\\n    lines   = _read_file_lines(file_path)\\n    segment = \\\"\\\".join(lines[start_line:end_line])\\n\\n    if verify_checksum:\\n        actual_cs = _checksum(segment)\\n        if actual_cs != stored_cs:\\n            raise ChunkDriftError(chunk_id, file_path, stored_cs, actual_cs)\\n\\n    return segment\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Operation 2 \\u2014 INJECT\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef md_ops_inject(\\n    index_path: str,\\n    chunk_id: str,\\n    new_content: str,\\n) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Replace a chunk's content in the markdown file and reindex.\\n\\n    Sequence (atomic-safe):\\n        1. Load index \\u2192 resolve file_path, start_line, end_line\\n        2. Read file lines\\n        3. Splice new_content into [start_line:end_line]\\n        4. Write new file atomically (temp \\u2192 rename)\\n        5. Full reindex of that file, preserving other chunk metadata\\n        6. Write new index atomically\\n\\n    Args:\\n        index_path:   Path to the MarkdownChunk index file.\\n        chunk_id:     The chunk to replace.\\n        new_content:  New raw text for the chunk. Must end with a newline\\n                      if you want to preserve normal markdown line structure.\\n\\n    Returns:\\n        The new index document (post-reindex).\\n\\n    Raises:\\n        IndexNotFoundError, ChunkNotFoundError, MdFileNotFoundError,\\n        InjectRangeInvalidError, WriteFailedError\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    doc = _load_index(index_path)\\n    fi  = _get_chunk_fields(doc)\\n\\n    row = md_indexer_get_chunk_row(doc, chunk_id)\\n    if row is None:\\n        raise ChunkNotFoundError(chunk_id)\\n\\n    chunk      = _row_to_dict(row, fi)\\n    file_path  = chunk[\\\"file_path\\\"]\\n    start_line = chunk[\\\"start_line\\\"]\\n    end_line   = chunk[\\\"end_line\\\"]\\n\\n    lines = _read_file_lines(file_path)\\n\\n    if start_line < 0 or end_line > len(lines) or start_line > end_line:\\n        raise InjectRangeInvalidError(chunk_id, start_line, end_line, len(lines))\\n\\n    # Splice new content\\n    new_lines = new_content.splitlines(keepends=True)\\n    # Ensure trailing newline on last injected line\\n    if new_lines and not new_lines[-1].endswith(\\\"\\\\n\\\"):\\n        new_lines[-1] += \\\"\\\\n\\\"\\n\\n    updated_lines = lines[:start_line] + new_lines + lines[end_line:]\\n\\n    # Step 1: write file\\n    _write_file_atomic(file_path, updated_lines)\\n\\n    # Step 2: full reindex (always after write, always in same call)\\n    new_doc = md_indexer_reindex_file(file_path, index_path, preserve_metadata=True)\\n\\n    return new_doc\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Operation 3 \\u2014 TOGGLE\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef md_ops_toggle(\\n    index_path: str,\\n    chunk_id: str,\\n    active: Optional[bool] = None,\\n) -> bool:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Toggle or explicitly set the is_active flag for a chunk.\\n\\n    Args:\\n        index_path:   Path to the MarkdownChunk index file.\\n        chunk_id:     The chunk to toggle.\\n        active:       If None, flips the current value.\\n                      If True/False, sets it explicitly.\\n\\n    Returns:\\n        The new is_active value.\\n\\n    Raises:\\n        IndexNotFoundError, ChunkNotFoundError, WriteFailedError\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    doc = _load_index(index_path)\\n    fi  = _get_chunk_fields(doc)\\n    id_idx     = fi[\\\"chunk_id\\\"]\\n    active_idx = fi[\\\"is_active\\\"]\\n\\n    found = False\\n    new_val = None\\n\\n    for row in doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []):\\n        if row[id_idx] == chunk_id:\\n            current = row[active_idx]\\n            new_val = (not current) if active is None else bool(active)\\n            row[active_idx] = new_val\\n            found = True\\n            break\\n\\n    if not found:\\n        raise ChunkNotFoundError(chunk_id)\\n\\n    if not bejson_core_atomic_write(index_path, doc):\\n        raise WriteFailedError(index_path, \\\"atomic write failed during toggle\\\")\\n\\n    return new_val\\n\\ndef md_ops_toggle_by_tag(\\n    index_path: str,\\n    tag: str,\\n    active: bool,\\n) -> List[str]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Set is_active for every chunk with a matching tag.\\n\\n    Args:\\n        index_path:   Path to the MarkdownChunk index file.\\n        tag:          Tag string to match against each chunk's tags array.\\n        active:       True to activate, False to deactivate.\\n\\n    Returns:\\n        List of chunk_ids that were modified.\\n\\n    Raises:\\n        IndexNotFoundError, WriteFailedError\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    doc = _load_index(index_path)\\n    fi  = _get_chunk_fields(doc)\\n    id_idx     = fi[\\\"chunk_id\\\"]\\n    active_idx = fi[\\\"is_active\\\"]\\n    tags_idx   = fi[\\\"tags\\\"]\\n\\n    modified = []\\n\\n    for row in doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []):\\n        chunk_tags = row[tags_idx] or []\\n        if tag in chunk_tags:\\n            row[active_idx] = active\\n            modified.append(row[id_idx])\\n\\n    if not bejson_core_atomic_write(index_path, doc):\\n        raise WriteFailedError(index_path, \\\"atomic write failed during tag toggle\\\")\\n\\n    return modified\\n\\ndef md_ops_set_tags(\\n    index_path: str,\\n    chunk_id: str,\\n    tags: List[str],\\n) -> None:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Set the tags array for a specific chunk.\\n\\n    Raises:\\n        IndexNotFoundError, ChunkNotFoundError, WriteFailedError\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    doc = _load_index(index_path)\\n    fi  = _get_chunk_fields(doc)\\n    id_idx   = fi[\\\"chunk_id\\\"]\\n    tags_idx = fi[\\\"tags\\\"]\\n\\n    found = False\\n    for row in doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []):\\n        if row[id_idx] == chunk_id:\\n            row[tags_idx] = list(tags)\\n            found = True\\n            break\\n\\n    if not found:\\n        raise ChunkNotFoundError(chunk_id)\\n\\n    if not bejson_core_atomic_write(index_path, doc):\\n        raise WriteFailedError(index_path, \\\"atomic write failed during set_tags\\\")\\n\\ndef md_ops_set_sort_order(\\n    index_path: str,\\n    chunk_id: str,\\n    sort_order: int,\\n) -> None:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Set the sort_order for a specific chunk.\\n\\n    Raises:\\n        IndexNotFoundError, ChunkNotFoundError, WriteFailedError\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    doc = _load_index(index_path)\\n    fi  = _get_chunk_fields(doc)\\n    id_idx    = fi[\\\"chunk_id\\\"]\\n    order_idx = fi[\\\"sort_order\\\"]\\n\\n    found = False\\n    for row in doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []):\\n        if row[id_idx] == chunk_id:\\n            row[order_idx] = int(sort_order)\\n            found = True\\n            break\\n\\n    if not found:\\n        raise ChunkNotFoundError(chunk_id)\\n\\n    if not bejson_core_atomic_write(index_path, doc):\\n        raise WriteFailedError(index_path, \\\"atomic write failed during set_sort_order\\\")\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Operation 4 \\u2014 ASSEMBLE\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef md_ops_assemble(\\n    index_path: str,\\n    tags: Optional[List[str]] = None,\\n    active_only: bool = True,\\n    separator: str = \\\"\\\\n\\\",\\n    predicate: Optional[Callable[[Dict[str, Any]], bool]] = None,\\n) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Assemble chunks from one or more markdown files into a single string.\\n\\n    Chunks are sorted by sort_order then by file_path + start_line as tiebreaker.\\n    Content is verified via checksum before inclusion; drifted chunks log a warning\\n    but are still included (use predicate to filter them if needed).\\n\\n    Args:\\n        index_path:   Path to the MarkdownChunk index file.\\n        tags:         If provided, only chunks where ANY of these tags is present\\n                      in the chunk's tags array are included.\\n        active_only:  If True (default), only is_active=True chunks are included.\\n        separator:    String inserted between assembled chunks. Default: \\\"\\\\\\\\n\\\".\\n        predicate:    Optional callable(chunk_dict) -> bool for custom filtering.\\n                      Called after is_active and tag filters.\\n\\n    Returns:\\n        Assembled string content.\\n\\n    Raises:\\n        IndexNotFoundError   \\u2014 index file missing\\n        AssembleEmptyError   \\u2014 no chunks matched the filters\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    doc = _load_index(index_path)\\n    fi  = _get_chunk_fields(doc)\\n\\n    all_chunks = md_indexer_list_chunks(doc)\\n\\n    # --- Filter: active ---\\n    if active_only:\\n        all_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if c.get(\\\"is_active\\\") is True]\\n\\n    # --- Filter: tags ---\\n    if tags:\\n        tag_set = set(tags)\\n        filtered = []\\n        for c in all_chunks:\\n            chunk_tags = set(c.get(\\\"tags\\\") or [])\\n            if chunk_tags & tag_set:\\n                filtered.append(c)\\n        all_chunks = filtered\\n\\n    # --- Filter: predicate ---\\n    if predicate:\\n        all_chunks = [c for c in all_chunks if predicate(c)]\\n\\n    if not all_chunks:\\n        reason = []\\n        if active_only:\\n            reason.append(\\\"active_only=True\\\")\\n        if tags:\\n            reason.append(f\\\"tags={tags}\\\")\\n        if predicate:\\n            reason.append(\\\"predicate filter\\\")\\n        raise AssembleEmptyError(\\\", \\\".join(reason) if reason else \\\"no chunks in index\\\")\\n\\n    # --- Sort: sort_order primary, file_path + start_line secondary ---\\n    all_chunks.sort(key=lambda c: (\\n        c.get(\\\"sort_order\\\") if c.get(\\\"sort_order\\\") is not None else 9999,\\n        c.get(\\\"file_path\\\") or \\\"\\\",\\n        c.get(\\\"start_line\\\") or 0,\\n    ))\\n\\n    # --- Pull content for each chunk ---\\n    parts = []\\n    for chunk in all_chunks:\\n        cid        = chunk[\\\"chunk_id\\\"]\\n        file_path  = chunk[\\\"file_path\\\"]\\n        start_line = chunk[\\\"start_line\\\"]\\n        end_line   = chunk[\\\"end_line\\\"]\\n        stored_cs  = chunk[\\\"checksum\\\"]\\n\\n        try:\\n            lines   = _read_file_lines(file_path)\\n            segment = \\\"\\\".join(lines[start_line:end_line])\\n        except MdFileNotFoundError as e:\\n            logging.warning(f\\\"[lib_md_ops] Assemble skipping chunk '{cid}' \\u2014 file missing: {e}\\\")\\n            continue\\n\\n        actual_cs = _checksum(segment)\\n        if actual_cs != stored_cs:\\n            logging.warning(\\n                f\\\"[lib_md_ops] Chunk drift for '{cid}' in '{file_path}' \\\"\\n                f\\\"(stored={stored_cs}, actual={actual_cs}). \\\"\\n                f\\\"Including stale content. Run reindex to fix.\\\"\\n            )\\n\\n        parts.append(segment)\\n\\n    if not parts:\\n        raise AssembleEmptyError(\\\"all matching chunks had missing files\\\")\\n\\n    return separator.join(parts)\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Convenience: assemble system prompt from a tag\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef md_ops_assemble_by_tag(\\n    index_path: str,\\n    tag: str,\\n    separator: str = \\\"\\\\n\\\\n\\\",\\n) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Shorthand to assemble all active chunks with a specific tag.\\n    Most common use case: building system prompts, policy documents, etc.\\n\\n    Example:\\n        assembled = md_ops_assemble_by_tag(\\\"chunks.bejson\\\", \\\"gemini_context\\\")\\n\\n    Returns:\\n        Assembled string of all matching active chunks.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    return md_ops_assemble(index_path, tags=[tag], active_only=True, separator=separator)\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Convenience: reindex\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nVERSION = \\\"1.0.0\\\"\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# BEJSON 104 schema for MarkdownChunk index documents\\n#\\n# Layout of Fields array \\u2014 positional order matters, append-only.\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nCHUNK_FIELDS = [\\n    {\\\"name\\\": \\\"chunk_id\\\",    \\\"type\\\": \\\"string\\\"},   # 0 \\u2014 stable UUID slug\\n    {\\\"name\\\": \\\"file_path\\\",   \\\"type\\\": \\\"string\\\"},   # 1 \\u2014 absolute path to markdown file\\n    {\\\"name\\\": \\\"start_line\\\",  \\\"type\\\": \\\"integer\\\"},  # 2 \\u2014 0-based inclusive\\n    {\\\"name\\\": \\\"end_line\\\",    \\\"type\\\": \\\"integer\\\"},  # 3 \\u2014 0-based exclusive (Python slice)\\n    {\\\"name\\\": \\\"chunk_type\\\",  \\\"type\\\": \\\"string\\\"},   # 4 \\u2014 heading|code_block|policy|raw|frontmatter\\n    {\\\"name\\\": \\\"label\\\",       \\\"type\\\": \\\"string\\\"},   # 5 \\u2014 human-readable display name\\n    {\\\"name\\\": \\\"is_active\\\",   \\\"type\\\": \\\"boolean\\\"},  # 6 \\u2014 participates in assemble operations\\n    {\\\"name\\\": \\\"tags\\\",        \\\"type\\\": \\\"array\\\"},    # 7 \\u2014 list of string tags for filtering\\n    {\\\"name\\\": \\\"sort_order\\\",  \\\"type\\\": \\\"integer\\\"},  # 8 \\u2014 assembly sequence, independent of line pos\\n    {\\\"name\\\": \\\"injected_at\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"string\\\"},   # 9 \\u2014 ISO 8601 UTC timestamp of last injection\\n    {\\\"name\\\": \\\"checksum\\\",    \\\"type\\\": \\\"string\\\"},   # 10 \\u2014 sha256[:16] of raw chunk content\\n]\\n\\n# Legacy fallback constants \\u2014 in case a consumer builds its own field map\\n_CHUNK_LEGACY = {f[\\\"name\\\"]: i for i, f in enumerate(CHUNK_FIELDS)}\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Chunk types\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nCHUNK_TYPE_HEADING     = \\\"heading\\\"\\nCHUNK_TYPE_CODE_BLOCK  = \\\"code_block\\\"\\nCHUNK_TYPE_FRONTMATTER = \\\"frontmatter\\\"\\nCHUNK_TYPE_POLICY      = \\\"policy\\\"\\nCHUNK_TYPE_RAW         = \\\"raw\\\"\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Internal helpers\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef _checksum(content: str) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Short SHA-256 fingerprint of chunk text content.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    return hashlib.sha256(content.encode(\\\"utf-8\\\")).hexdigest()[:16]\\n\\ndef _chunk_id(file_path: str, start_line: int, chunk_type: str, seq: int) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Deterministic, stable chunk ID derived from file path + position + type.\\n    Format: <file_stem>_<type>_<start>_<seq>\\n    Pure string construction \\u2014 no slugify library needed.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    stem = Path(file_path).stem\\n    safe = \\\"\\\"\\n    for ch in stem.lower():\\n        if ch.isalnum() or ch in \\\"-_\\\":\\n            safe += ch\\n        elif ch in \\\" .\\\":\\n            safe += \\\"_\\\"\\n    return f\\\"{safe}_{chunk_type}_{start_line}_{seq}\\\"\\n\\ndef _now_iso() -> str:\\n    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime(\\\"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ\\\")\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# State machine \\u2014 structural scanner\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef _scan_lines(lines: List[str]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Walk markdown lines using a two-flag state machine (no regex).\\n    Returns a list of raw chunk dicts:\\n        { start, end, chunk_type, label }\\n\\n    State flags:\\n        in_frontmatter  \\u2014 True while inside YAML front matter block\\n        in_code_block   \\u2014 True while inside a fenced code block\\n        fence_char      \\u2014 '`' or '~' \\u2014 which fence style opened the block\\n        fence_width     \\u2014 how many fence chars opened the block (3+)\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    chunks: List[Dict[str, Any]] = []\\n\\n    in_frontmatter  = False\\n    in_code_block   = False\\n    fence_char      = \\\"\\\"\\n    fence_width     = 0\\n\\n    # Track the current \\\"raw\\\" prose section between structural elements\\n    raw_start: Optional[int] = None\\n\\n    def close_raw(end_line: int):\\n        nonlocal raw_start\\n        if raw_start is not None and end_line > raw_start:\\n            # Only emit if there's non-blank content\\n            segment = lines[raw_start:end_line]\\n            if any(l.strip() for l in segment):\\n                chunks.append({\\n                    \\\"start\\\":      raw_start,\\n                    \\\"end\\\":        end_line,\\n                    \\\"chunk_type\\\": CHUNK_TYPE_RAW,\\n                    \\\"label\\\":      f\\\"Raw block (lines {raw_start}\\u2013{end_line - 1})\\\",\\n                })\\n        raw_start = None\\n\\n    i = 0\\n    total = len(lines)\\n\\n    # --- Handle YAML frontmatter (must be first line = \\\"---\\\") ---\\n    if total > 0 and lines[0].rstrip() == \\\"---\\\":\\n        in_frontmatter = True\\n        fm_start = 0\\n        i = 1\\n        while i < total:\\n            stripped = lines[i].rstrip()\\n            if stripped == \\\"---\\\" or stripped == \\\"...\\\":\\n                chunks.append({\\n                    \\\"start\\\":      fm_start,\\n                    \\\"end\\\":        i + 1,\\n                    \\\"chunk_type\\\": CHUNK_TYPE_FRONTMATTER,\\n                    \\\"label\\\":      \\\"Frontmatter\\\",\\n                })\\n                in_frontmatter = False\\n                i += 1\\n                break\\n            i += 1\\n        if in_frontmatter:\\n            # Unclosed frontmatter \\u2014 treat rest of file as raw\\n            chunks.append({\\n                \\\"start\\\":      0,\\n                \\\"end\\\":        total,\\n                \\\"chunk_type\\\": CHUNK_TYPE_RAW,\\n                \\\"label\\\":      \\\"Unclosed frontmatter (treated as raw)\\\",\\n            })\\n            return chunks\\n\\n    # --- Main scan loop ---\\n    raw_start = i  # begin tracking raw content from after frontmatter\\n\\n    while i < total:\\n        line = lines[i]\\n        stripped = line.rstrip()\\n\\n        # ---- Inside a fenced code block ----\\n        if in_code_block:\\n            # Detect closing fence: same char, same or greater width, optional trailing spaces\\n            s = stripped.lstrip()\\n            if s.startswith(fence_char * fence_width) and s.replace(fence_char, \\\"\\\").strip() == \\\"\\\":\\n                # Close the code block \\u2014 end is exclusive so +1\\n                close_raw(code_block_start)\\n                chunks.append({\\n                    \\\"start\\\":      code_block_start,\\n                    \\\"end\\\":        i + 1,\\n                    \\\"chunk_type\\\": CHUNK_TYPE_CODE_BLOCK,\\n                    \\\"label\\\":      f\\\"Code block (lines {code_block_start}\\u2013{i})\\\",\\n                })\\n                in_code_block = False\\n                fence_char    = \\\"\\\"\\n                fence_width   = 0\\n                raw_start     = i + 1\\n            i += 1\\n            continue\\n\\n        # ---- Detect opening fence ----\\n        # A fenced code block starts with 3+ backticks or 3+ tildes at line start\\n        lstripped = stripped.lstrip()\\n        for fc in (\\\"`\\\", \\\"~\\\"):\\n            if lstripped.startswith(fc * 3):\\n                # Count consecutive fence chars\\n                width = 0\\n                for ch in lstripped:\\n                    if ch == fc:\\n                        width += 1\\n                    else:\\n                        break\\n                if width >= 3:\\n                    close_raw(i)\\n                    in_code_block    = True\\n                    fence_char       = fc\\n                    fence_width      = width\\n                    code_block_start = i\\n                    i += 1\\n                    break\\n        else:\\n            # ---- Detect ATX heading (# / ## / ### etc.) ----\\n            if stripped.startswith(\\\"#\\\"):\\n                # Count leading #\\n                level = 0\\n                for ch in stripped:\\n                    if ch == \\\"#\\\":\\n                        level += 1\\n                    else:\\n                        break\\n                # Must be followed by space or end-of-line to be a real heading\\n                rest = stripped[level:]\\n                if not rest or rest.startswith(\\\" \\\"):\\n                    close_raw(i)\\n                    heading_text = rest.strip() if rest.strip() else f\\\"Heading (level {level})\\\"\\n                    # A heading chunk spans only that single line\\n                    chunks.append({\\n                        \\\"start\\\":      i,\\n                        \\\"end\\\":        i + 1,\\n                        \\\"chunk_type\\\": CHUNK_TYPE_HEADING,\\n                        \\\"label\\\":      heading_text[:80],\\n                    })\\n                    raw_start = i + 1\\n                    i += 1\\n                    continue\\n\\n            i += 1\\n            continue\\n\\n    # Close any trailing raw block\\n    close_raw(total)\\n\\n    # If an unclosed code block exists, emit as raw\\n    if in_code_block:\\n        chunks.append({\\n            \\\"start\\\":      code_block_start,\\n            \\\"end\\\":        total,\\n            \\\"chunk_type\\\": CHUNK_TYPE_RAW,\\n            \\\"label\\\":      f\\\"Unclosed code block (lines {code_block_start}\\u2013{total - 1})\\\",\\n        })\\n\\n    return chunks\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Public API\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef md_indexer_scan_file(file_path: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Scan a markdown file and return a list of raw chunk dicts.\\n    Each dict contains: start, end, chunk_type, label, checksum.\\n\\n    Does NOT write anything \\u2014 pure read operation.\\n    Raises MdFileNotFoundError if the file does not exist.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    p = Path(file_path).resolve()\\n    if not p.exists():\\n        raise MdFileNotFoundError(str(p))\\n\\n    raw_text = p.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n    lines    = raw_text.splitlines(keepends=True)\\n\\n    raw_chunks = _scan_lines(lines)\\n\\n    # Attach checksum to each chunk\\n    for chunk in raw_chunks:\\n        segment = \\\"\\\".join(lines[chunk[\\\"start\\\"]:chunk[\\\"end\\\"]])\\n        chunk[\\\"checksum\\\"] = _checksum(segment)\\n\\n    return raw_chunks\\n\\ndef md_indexer_build_index_doc(file_path: str, default_active: bool = True) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Scan a markdown file and produce a BEJSON 104 MarkdownChunk index document.\\n\\n    Args:\\n        file_path:      Absolute or relative path to the markdown file.\\n        default_active: Whether newly scanned chunks default to is_active=True.\\n\\n    Returns:\\n        A valid BEJSON 104 dict ready for atomic write or further manipulation.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    p = Path(file_path).resolve()\\n    raw_chunks = md_indexer_scan_file(str(p))\\n\\n    values = []\\n    now    = _now_iso()\\n\\n    for seq, chunk in enumerate(raw_chunks):\\n        cid   = _chunk_id(str(p), chunk[\\\"start\\\"], chunk[\\\"chunk_type\\\"], seq)\\n        label = chunk.get(\\\"label\\\") or f\\\"{chunk['chunk_type']} (line {chunk['start']})\\\"\\n\\n        # Build row in CHUNK_FIELDS positional order\\n        row = [\\n            cid,                        # 0  chunk_id\\n            str(p),                     # 1  file_path\\n            chunk[\\\"start\\\"],             # 2  start_line\\n            chunk[\\\"end\\\"],               # 3  end_line\\n            chunk[\\\"chunk_type\\\"],        # 4  chunk_type\\n            label,                      # 5  label\\n            default_active,             # 6  is_active\\n            [],                         # 7  tags\\n            seq,                        # 8  sort_order\\n            now,                        # 9  injected_at\\n            chunk[\\\"checksum\\\"],          # 10 checksum\\n        ]\\n        values.append(row)\\n\\n    doc = bejson_core_create_104(\\\"MarkdownChunk\\\", CHUNK_FIELDS, values)\\n    return doc\\n\\ndef md_indexer_save_index(index_doc: Dict[str, Any], index_path: str) -> bool:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Atomically write a MarkdownChunk index document to disk.\\n    Returns True on success, raises WriteFailedError on failure.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    result = bejson_core_atomic_write(index_path, index_doc)\\n    if not result:\\n        raise WriteFailedError(index_path, \\\"bejson_core_atomic_write returned False\\\")\\n    return True\\n\\ndef md_indexer_reindex_file(\\n    file_path: str,\\n    index_path: str,\\n    preserve_metadata: bool = True,\\n) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Full reindex of a markdown file.\\n\\n    If preserve_metadata=True and an existing index exists at index_path,\\n    it merges per-chunk metadata (tags, is_active, sort_order) from the old\\n    index into the new one by matching on chunk_type + start_line proximity.\\n    This is a best-effort match \\u2014 if the file changed substantially, metadata\\n    may not carry over cleanly. Checksums are always recomputed from the file.\\n\\n    Sequence: scan file \\u2192 build new index \\u2192 merge metadata \\u2192 write atomically.\\n    Returns the new index document.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    p = Path(file_path).resolve()\\n    if not p.exists():\\n        raise MdFileNotFoundError(str(p))\\n\\n    new_doc = md_indexer_build_index_doc(str(p))\\n\\n    if preserve_metadata and Path(index_path).exists():\\n        old_doc = bejson_core_load_file(index_path)\\n        if old_doc:\\n            new_doc = _merge_metadata(old_doc, new_doc)\\n\\n    md_indexer_save_index(new_doc, index_path)\\n    return new_doc\\n\\ndef _merge_metadata(old_doc: Dict[str, Any], new_doc: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Best-effort merge of per-chunk metadata from old_doc into new_doc.\\n    Matches by chunk_id first, falls back to (chunk_type, start_line) proximity.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    old_fi = bejson_core_get_field_map(old_doc)\\n    new_fi = bejson_core_get_field_map(new_doc)\\n\\n    old_id_idx    = old_fi.get(\\\"chunk_id\\\",   _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"chunk_id\\\"])\\n    old_type_idx  = old_fi.get(\\\"chunk_type\\\", _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"chunk_type\\\"])\\n    old_start_idx = old_fi.get(\\\"start_line\\\", _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"start_line\\\"])\\n    old_tags_idx  = old_fi.get(\\\"tags\\\",       _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"tags\\\"])\\n    old_act_idx   = old_fi.get(\\\"is_active\\\",  _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"is_active\\\"])\\n    old_sort_idx  = old_fi.get(\\\"sort_order\\\", _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"sort_order\\\"])\\n\\n    new_id_idx    = new_fi.get(\\\"chunk_id\\\",   _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"chunk_id\\\"])\\n    new_type_idx  = new_fi.get(\\\"chunk_type\\\", _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"chunk_type\\\"])\\n    new_start_idx = new_fi.get(\\\"start_line\\\", _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"start_line\\\"])\\n    new_tags_idx  = new_fi.get(\\\"tags\\\",       _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"tags\\\"])\\n    new_act_idx   = new_fi.get(\\\"is_active\\\",  _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"is_active\\\"])\\n    new_sort_idx  = new_fi.get(\\\"sort_order\\\", _CHUNK_LEGACY[\\\"sort_order\\\"])\\n\\n    # Build lookup: chunk_id -> old row\\n    old_by_id = {row[old_id_idx]: row for row in old_doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", [])}\\n\\n    # Build lookup: (chunk_type, approx_start) -> old row for proximity matching\\n    old_by_type_start = {}\\n    for row in old_doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []):\\n        key = (row[old_type_idx], row[old_start_idx])\\n        old_by_type_start[key] = row\\n\\n    for new_row in new_doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []):\\n        cid          = new_row[new_id_idx]\\n        new_type     = new_row[new_type_idx]\\n        new_start    = new_row[new_start_idx]\\n\\n        old_row = old_by_id.get(cid)\\n\\n        if old_row is None:\\n            # Proximity fallback: same type, closest start line within 5 lines\\n            best_match = None\\n            best_dist  = 999\\n            for (otype, ostart), orow in old_by_type_start.items():\\n                if otype == new_type:\\n                    dist = abs(ostart - new_start)\\n                    if dist < best_dist and dist <= 5:\\n                        best_dist  = dist\\n                        best_match = orow\\n            old_row = best_match\\n\\n        if old_row is not None:\\n            # Merge: tags, is_active, sort_order\\n            if old_row[old_tags_idx] is not None:\\n                new_row[new_tags_idx] = old_row[old_tags_idx]\\n            new_row[new_act_idx]  = old_row[old_act_idx]\\n            new_row[new_sort_idx] = old_row[old_sort_idx]\\n\\n    return new_doc\\n\\ndef md_indexer_list_chunks(index_doc: Dict[str, Any]) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Return all chunks from an index document as a list of dicts.\\n    Convenience for inspection and debugging.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    fi     = bejson_core_get_field_map(index_doc)\\n    fields = [f[\\\"name\\\"] for f in index_doc.get(\\\"Fields\\\", [])]\\n    result = []\\n    for row in index_doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []):\\n        item = {}\\n        for i, name in enumerate(fields):\\n            item[name] = row[i] if i < len(row) else None\\n        result.append(item)\\n ChunkerV6\\n\\nclass CMS_Chunker_Wrapper:\\n    def __init__(self, cms_manager):\\n        self.cms = cms_manager\\n        self.www_root = Path(cms_manager.www_root)\\n        self.backup_dir = CMS Backup\\\", tags: str = \\\"backup,cms\\\"):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Chunk the www_root and export as a zip.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        try:\\n            # 1. Perform Chunking\\n            # Note: ChunkerV6 creates .mfdb/ folder inside target_dir\\n            result = ChunkerV6.do_chunk(str(self.www_root), changelog=changelog, tags=tags)\\n            if not result.get(\\\"ok\\\"):\\n                return {\\\"success\\\": False, \\\"error\\\": result.get(\\\"message\\\")}\\n\\n            detail = result.get(\\\"detail\\\", {})\\n            version = detail.get(\\\"version\\\")\\n            manifest_path = detail.get(\\\"manifest\\\")\\n            \\n            # 2. Export as Zip to backups folder\\n            timestamp = ChunkerV6.get_timestamp()\\n            zip_filename = f\\\"site_backup_{version}_{timestamp}.zip\\\"\\n            zip_path = self.backup_dir / zip_filename\\n            \\n            export_result = ChunkerV6.do_export(str(manifest_path), version, str(zip_path))\\n            if export_result.get(\\\"ok\\\"):\\n                # 3. Automatic Bump for next backup\\n                ChunkerV6.do_bump(str(self.www_root), part=\\\"patch\\\")\\n                return {\\\"success\\\": True, \\\"zip_path\\\": str(zip_path), \\\"version\\\": version, \\\"message\\\": \\\"Site chunked and exported successfully.\\\"}\\n            else:\\n                return {\\\"success\\\": False, \\\"error\\\": export_result.get(\\\"message\\\")}\\n                \\n        except Exception as e:\\n            return {\\\"success\\\": False, \\\"error\\\": str(e)}\\n\\n    def restore_site(self, zip_filename: str):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Import a zip and unchunk to www_root.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        try:\\n            zip_path = self.backup_dir / zip_filename\\n            if not zip_path.exists():\\n                return {\\\"success\\\": False, \\\"error\\\": f\\\"Backup file not found: {zip_filename}\\\"}\\n\\n            # Create a clean temp workspace\\n            restore_workspace = self.www_root.parent / \\\".restore_workspace\\\"\\n            if restore_workspace.exists(): shutil.rmtree(restore_workspace)\\n            restore_workspace.mkdir(parents=True)\\n            (restore_workspace / \\\"data\\\").mkdir()\\n            \\n            temp_manifest = restore_workspace / \\\"104a.mfdb.bejson\\\"\\n            \\n            # 1. Import Zip\\n            import_result = ChunkerV6.do_import(str(temp_manifest), str(zip_path), overwrite=True)\\n            if not import_result.get(\\\"ok\\\"):\\n                return {\\\"success\\\": False, \\\"error\\\": import_result.get(\\\"message\\\")}\\n            \\n            # 2. Get version from imported manifest\\n            versions = ChunkerV6.list_versions(str(temp_manifest))\\n            if not versions:\\n                return {\\\"success\\\": False, \\\"error\\\": \\\"No versions found in backup zip.\\\"}\\n            \\n            target_version = versions[0][\\\"version\\\"] # Take latest/first\\n            \\n            # 3. Unchunk\\n            unchunk_result = ChunkerV6.do_unchunk(str(temp_manifest), target_version)\\n            if not unchunk_result.get(\\\"ok\\\"):\\n                return {\\\"success\\\": False, \\\"error\\\": unchunk_result.get(\\\"message\\\")}\\n            \\n            restored_files_dir = Path(unchunk_result.get(\\\"out_dir\\\"))\\n            \\n            # 4. Swap www_root content\\n            for item in self.www_root.iterdir():\\n                if item.name == \\\".restore_workspace\\\" or item.name == \\\".mfdb\\\": continue\\n                if item.is_dir(): shutil.rmtree(item)\\n                else: item.unlink()\\n            \\n            for item in restored_files_dir.iterdir():\\n                shutil.move(str(item), str(self.www_root / item.name))\\n            \\n            #      tags: str = \\\"\\\") -> dict:\\n    target_path = Path(target_dir).resolve()\\n    if not target_path.is_dir():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Not a directory: file_count = 0\\n    skipped    = []\\n\\n    for root, dirs, files in os.walk(target_path):\\n        dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if d not in excludes]\\n        for file in sorted(files):\\n                   raw     = f_path.read_bytes()\\n                    content = base64.b64encode(raw).decode(\\\"ascii\\\")\\n                    is_b64  = True\\n                else:\\n                    content = \\\"\\\" if binary else f_path.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n                new_rows.append([entity_name, rel_path, file, content, binary, is_b64])\\n                file_count \\\"entity_path\\\": entity_path,\\n            \\\"manifest\\\":    manifest_path,\\n        },\\n    }\\n\\ndef do_bump(target_dir: str, part: str = \\\"patch\\\") -> dict:\\n    target_path =     row = next((r for r in manifest_doc[\\\"Values\\\"]\\n                    if r[en_idx] == entity_name), None)\\n        if row is None:\\n            return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": str(e)}\\n\\ndef do_prune(manifest_path_arg: str, version: str) -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Remove a version from the manifest and its rows from the entity file.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    manifest_path = Path(manifest_path_arg).resolve()\\n    if not manifest_path.exists():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": \\\"Manifest not found.\\\"}\\n\\n    entity_name = version_to_entity_name(version)\\n    try:\\n        manifest_doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(manifest_path))\\n\\n        # Find the manifest row\\n        target_row = next((r for r in manifest_doc[\\\"Values\\\"]\\n                           if r[M_ENTITY_NAME] == entity_name), None)\\n        if target_row is None:\\n            return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Version '{version}' not found in manifest.\\\"}\\n\\n        entity_rel  = target_row[M_FILE_PATH]\\n        entity_abs  = manifest_path.parent / entity_rel\\n\\n        # Remove from entity file (atomic)\\n        rows_removed = 0\\n        if entity_abs.exists():\\n            entity_doc   = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(entity_abs))\\n            rows_removed = _remove_version_rows(entity_doc, entity_name)\\n            _write_entity(str(entity_abs), entity_doc)\\n\\n        # Remove from manifest (atomic)\\n        manifest_doc[\\\"Values\\\"] = [r for r in manifest_doc[\\\"Values\\\"]\\n                                   if r[M_ENTITY_NAME] != entity_name]\\n        _write_manifest(str(manifest_path), manifest_doc)\\n\\n        return {\\n            \\\"ok\\\":          True,\\n            \\\"message\\\":     f\\\"VERSION {version} PRUNED\\\",\\n            \\\"rows_removed\\\": rows_removed,\\n        }\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": str(e)}\\n\\ndef do_export(manifest_path_arg: str, version: str, out_path: str) -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Export a version as a self-contained zip.\\n    Zip layout: 104a.mfdb.bejson + data/<entity>.bejson (version rows only).\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    manifest_path = Path(manifest_path_arg).resolve()\\n    if not manifest_path.exists():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": \\\"Manifest not found.\\\", \\\"zip_path\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\n    entity_name = version_to_entity_name(version)\\n    try:\\n        manifest_doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(manifest_path))\\n\\n        target_row = next((r for r in manifest_doc[\\\"Values\\\"]\\n                           if r[M_ENTITY_NAME] == entity_name), None)\\n        if target_row is None:\\n            return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Version '{version}' not found.\\\", \\\"zip_path\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\n        entity_rel = target_row[M_FILE_PATH]\\n        entity_abs = manifest_path.parent / entity_rel\\n        if not entity_abs.exists():\\n            return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Entity file missing: {entity_abs}\\\", \\\"zip_path\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\n        entity_doc   = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(entity_abs))\\n        version_rows = _get_version_rows(entity_doc, entity_name)\\n\\n        # Build export manifest with only this version's row\\n        export_manifest = dict(manifest_doc)\\n        export_manifest[\\\"Values\\\"] = [target_row]\\n\\n        # Build export entity with only this version's rows\\n        export_entity = dict(entity_doc)\\n        export_entity[\\\"Values\\\"] = version_rows\\n\\n        # Write to temp dir then zip\\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:\\n            tmp_path = Path(tmp)\\n            (tmp_path / \\\"data\\\").mkdir()\\n\\n            manifest_tmp = tmp_path / \\\"104a.mfdb.bejson\\\"\\n            entity_tmp   = tmp_path / entity_rel\\n\\n            manifest_tmp.write_text(json.dumps(export_manifest, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False),\\n                                    encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n            entity_tmp.write_text(json.dumps(export_entity, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False),\\n                                  encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n\\n            zip_path = Path(out_path)\\n            zip_path.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n            with zipfile.ZipFile(str(zip_path), \\\"w\\\", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zf:\\n                zf.write(str(manifest_tmp), \\\"104a.mfdb.bejson\\\")\\n                zf.write(str(entity_tmp), entity_rel)\\n\\n        db_name = manifest_doc.get(\\\"DB_Name\\\", \\\"project\\\")\\n        return {\\n            \\\"ok\\\":       True,\\n            \\\"message\\\":  f\\\"VERSION {version} EXPORTED\\\",\\n            \\\"zip_path\\\": str(zip_path),\\n            \\\"files\\\":    len(version_rows),\\n            \\\"db_name\\\":  db_name,\\n        }\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": str(e), \\\"zip_path\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\ndef do_import(manifest_path_arg: str, zip_path_arg: str,\\n              on_conflict: str = \\\"reject\\\") -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Import versions from an export zip into an existing MFDB.\\n    on_conflict: \\\"reject\\\" (skip existing) | \\\"prefix\\\" (rename with _imp suffix)\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    manifest_path = Path(manifest_path_arg).resolve()\\n    zip_path      = Path(zip_path_arg).resolve()\\n\\n    if not manifest_path.exists():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": \\\"Target manifest not found.\\\", \\\"imported\\\": [], \\\"skipped\\\": []}\\n    if not zip_path.exists():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Zip not found: {zip_path}\\\", \\\"imported\\\": [], \\\"skipped\\\": []}\\n\\n    imported = []\\n    skipped  = []\\n\\n    try:\\n        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:\\n            tmp_path = Path(tmp)\\n            with zipfile.ZipFile(str(zip_path), \\\"r\\\") as zf:\\n                zf.extractall(str(tmp_path))\\n\\n            imp_manifest_path = tmp_path / \\\"104a.mfdb.bejson\\\"\\n            if not imp_manifest_path.exists():\\n                return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": \\\"Zip missing 104a.mfdb.bejson\\\",\\n                        \\\"imported\\\": [], \\\"skipped\\\": []}\\n\\n            imp_manifest = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(imp_manifest_path))\\n            tgt_manifest = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(manifest_path))\\n\\n            existing_entity_names = {r[M_ENTITY_NAME] for r in tgt_manifest[\\\"Values\\\"]}\\n\\n            for imp_row in imp_manifest[\\\"Values\\\"]:\\n                # Extend row to full width if needed\\n                while len(imp_row) < len(MANIFEST_FIELDS):\\n                    imp_row.append(\\\"\\\")\\n\\n                imp_entity_name = imp_row[M_ENTITY_NAME]\\n                imp_entity_rel  = imp_row[M_FILE_PATH]\\n                imp_entity_abs  = tmp_path / imp_entity_rel\\n\\n                if not imp_entity_abs.exists():\\n                    skipped.append(f\\\"{imp_entity_name}: entity file missing in zip\\\")\\n                    continue\\n\\n                # Conflict handling\\n                final_entity_name = imp_entity_name\\n                if imp_entity_name in existing_entity_names:\\n                    if on_conflict == \\\"reject\\\":\\n                        skipped.append(f\\\"{imp_entity_name}: already exists (rejected)\\\")\\n                        continue\\n                    else:  # prefix\\n                        final_entity_name = imp_entity_name + \\\"_imp\\\"\\n                        if final_entity_name in existing_entity_names:\\n                            skipped.append(f\\\"{imp_entity_name}: conflict and prefix taken\\\")\\n                            continue\\n                        imp_row[M_ENTITY_NAME] = final_entity_name\\n\\n                # Load import entity and rename its version keys if prefixed\\n                imp_entity = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(imp_entity_abs))\\n                new_rows   = []\\n                for r in imp_entity[\\\"Values\\\"]:\\n                    r = list(r)\\n                    if r[E_VERSION] == imp_entity_name and final_entity_name != imp_entity_name:\\n                        r[E_VERSION] = final_entity_name\\n                    new_rows.append(r)\\n\\n                # Target entity path (same entity file as other versions of this DB)\\n                tgt_entity_rel  = imp_entity_rel\\n                tgt_entity_abs  = manifest_path.parent / tgt_entity_rel\\n                tgt_entity_abs.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n\\n                if tgt_entity_abs.exists():\\n                    tgt_entity = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(tgt_entity_abs))\\n                else:\\n                    tgt_entity = dict(imp_entity)\\n                    tgt_entity[\\\"Values\\\"] = []\\n                    ph = os.path.relpath(str(manifest_path),\\n                                         str(tgt_entity_abs.parent))\\n                    tgt_entity[\\\"Parent_Hierarchy\\\"] = ph\\n\\n                tgt_entity[\\\"Values\\\"].extend(new_rows)\\n                _write_entity(str(tgt_entity_abs), tgt_entity)\\n\\n                # Append manifest row\\n                imp_row[M_FILE_PATH] = tgt_entity_rel\\n                tgt_manifest[\\\"Values\\\"].append(imp_row)\\n                existing_entity_names.add(final_entity_name)\\n                imported.append(final_entity_name)\\n\\n            _write_manifest(str(manifest_path), tgt_manifest)\\n\\n        return {\\n            \\\"ok\\\":      True,\\n            \\\"message\\\": f\\\"IMPORT COMPLETE: {len(imported)} imported, {len(skipped)} skipped\\\",\\n            \\\"imported\\\": imported,\\n            \\\"skipped\\\":  skipped,\\n        }\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": str(e), \\\"imported\\\": [], \\\"skipped\\\": []}\\n\\n# parser = argparse.ArgumentParser(\\n        description=f\\\"BEJSON MFDB Chunker v2 \\u2014 MFDB Spec {MFDB_SPEC_VERSION}\\\",\\n        formatter_class=argparse.RawDescriptionHelpFormatter,\\n : {', '.join(r['skipped'])}\\\")\\n\\n    elif args.list:\\n        versions = list_versions(args.list)\\n        meta     = get_manifest_meta(args.list)\\n        print(f\\\"DB: {meta.get('DB_Name',
