---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Helpers\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef get_timestamp() -> str:\\n    return time.strftime(\\\"%Y%m%d_%H%M%S\\\")\\n\\ndef now_iso() -> str:\\n    return datetime.now(timezone.utc).strftime(\\\"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ\\\")\\n\\ndef sanitize_name(name: str) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Lowercase, spaces to underscores, keep alnum and underscores.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    return \\\"\\\".join(c if c.isalnum() or c == \\\"_\\\" else \\\"_\\\"\\n                   for c in name.lower().replace(\\\" \\\", \\\"_\\\"))\\n\\ndef load_or_create_config(target_path: Path) -> dict:\\n    config_path = target_path / \\\"chunker_config.json\\\"\\n    if config_path.exists():\\n        try:\\n            with open(config_path) as f:\\n                config = json.load(f)\\n            for k, v in DEFAULT_CONFIG.items():\\n                if k not in config:\\n                    config[k] = v\\n            return config\\n        except Exception as e:\\n            print(f\\\"Warning: Failed to read config. Error: {e}\\\")\\n    new_config = dict(DEFAULT_CONFIG)\\n    new_config[\\\"project_name\\\"] = target_path.name\\n    try:\\n        with open(config_path, \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n            json.dump(new_config, f, indent=2)\\n        print(f\\\"[*] Created config at {config_path}  (project_name: {new_config['project_name']})\\\")\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        print(f\\\"Warning: Could not create config. Error: {e}\\\")\\n    return new_config\\n\\ndef save_config(target_path: Path, config: dict) -> None:\\n    config_path = target_path / \\\"chunker_config.json\\\"\\n    with open(config_path, \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n        json.dump(config, f, indent=2)\\n\\ndef is_binary(file_path: Path) -> bool:\\n    try:\\n        with open(file_path, \\\"tr\\\") as f:\\n            f.read(512)\\n        return False\\n    except UnicodeDecodeError:\\n        return True\\n\\ndef version_to_entity_name(version: str) -> str:\\n    return \\\"v\\\" + version.replace(\\\".\\\", \\\"_\\\")\\n\\ndef bump_version(version: str, part: str = \\\"patch\\\") -> str:\\n    try:\\n        parts = [int(x) for x in version.split(\\\".\\\")]\\n        while len(parts) < 3:\\n            parts.append(0)\\n        if part == \\\"major\\\":\\n            parts = [parts[0] + 1, 0, 0]\\n        elif part == \\\"minor\\\":\\n            parts = [parts[0], parts[1] + 1, 0]\\n        else:\\n            parts = [parts[0], parts[1], parts[2] + 1]\\n        return \\\".\\\".join(str(p) for p in parts)\\n    except Exception:\\n        return version\\n\\ndef parse_tags(tags_str: str) -> list[str]:\\n    return [t.strip() for t in tags_str.split(\\\",\\\") if t.strip()] if tags_str else []\\n\\ndef format_tags(tags: list[str]) -> str:\\n    return \\\",\\\".join(t.strip() for t in tags if t.strip())\\n\\ndef get_mfdb_dir(config: dict) -> Path:\\n    return Path(config[\\\"output_base\\\"]) / f\\\"{config['project_name']}_MFDB\\\"\\n\\ndef get_manifest_path(config: dict) -> Path:\\n    return get_mfdb_dir(config) / \\\"104a.mfdb.bejson\\\"\\n\\ndef get_entity_path(config: dict) -> Path:\\n    return get_mfdb_dir(config) / \\\"data\\\" / f\\\"{sanitize_name(config['project_name'])}.bejson\\\"\\n\\ndef get_templates_dir(config: dict) -> Path:\\n    return get_mfdb_dir(config) / \\\"templates\\\"\\n\\ndef safe_extract_zip(zip_file: zipfile.ZipFile, output_dir: Path) -> None:\\n    output_dir = output_dir.resolve()\\n    for member in zip_file.infolist():\\n        member_path = (output_dir / member.filename).resolve()\\n        if not str(member_path).startswith(str(output_dir)):\\n            raise ValueError(f\\\"Unsafe zip path blocked: {member.filename}\\\")\\n    zip_file.extractall(str(output_dir))\\n\\ndef collect_project_files(target_path: Path, config: dict) -> list[Path]:\\n    exts = set(config[\\\"extensions\\\"])\\n    excludes = set(config[\\\"exclude_dirs\\\"])\\n    files: list[Path] = []\\n    for root, dirs, filenames in os.walk(target_path):\\n        dirs[:] = [d for d in dirs if d not in excludes]\\n        for file_name in sorted(filenames):\\n            file_path = Path(root) / file_name\\n            if file_name == \\\"chunker_config.json\\\":\\n                continue\\n            if file_path.suffix.lower() not in exts:\\n                continue\\n            files.append(file_path)\\n    return files\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Manifest management\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef init_manifest(config: dict) -> str:\\n    manifest_path = get_manifest_path(config)\\n    if manifest_path.exists():\\n        return str(manifest_path)\\n\\n    mfdb_dir = get_mfdb_dir(config)\\n    mfdb_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n    (mfdb_dir / \\\"data\\\").mkdir(exist_ok=True)\\n\\n    manifest_doc = {\\n        \\\"Format\\\":         \\\"BEJSON\\\",\\n        \\\"Format_Version\\\": \\\"104a\\\",\\n        \\\"Format_Creator\\\": \\\"Elton Boehnen\\\",\\n        \\\"MFDB_Version\\\":   MFDB_SPEC_VERSION,\\n        \\\"DB_Name\\\":        config[\\\"project_name\\\"],\\n        \\\"DB_Description\\\": f\\\"Version archive for project: {config['project_name']}\\\",\\n        \\\"Schema_Version\\\": \\\"1.0.0\\\",\\n        \\\"Author\\\":         \\\"Elton Boehnen\\\",\\n        \\\"Created_At\\\":     now_iso(),\\n        \\\"Records_Type\\\":   [\\\"mfdb\\\"],\\n        \\\"Fields\\\":         MANIFEST_FIELDS,\\n        \\\"Values\\\":         [],\\n    }\\n    BEJSONCore.bejson_core_atomic_write(str(manifest_path), manifest_doc)\\n    return str(manifest_path)\\n\\ndef version_exists_in_manifest(manifest_path: str, entity_name: str) -> bool:\\n    try:\\n        doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(manifest_path)\\n        return any(row[M_ENTITY_NAME] == entity_name for row in doc[\\\"Values\\\"])\\n    except Exception:\\n        return False\\n\\ndef _write_manifest(manifest_path: str, doc: dict) -> None:\\n    BEJSONCore.bejson_core_atomic_write(manifest_path, doc)\\n\\ndef _write_entity(entity_path: str, doc: dict) -> None:\\n    BEJSONCore.bejson_core_atomic_write(entity_path, doc)\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Entity file management\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef _load_or_create_entity(entity_path: str, config: dict,\\n                            parent_hierarchy: str) -> dict:\\n    ep = Path(entity_path)\\n    if ep.exists():\\n        return BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(entity_path)\\n\\n    ep.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n    entity_type = sanitize_name(config[\\\"project_name\\\"])\\n    doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_create_104(\\n        record_type=entity_type,\\n        fields=ENTITY_FIELDS,\\n        values=[],\\n    )\\n    doc[\\\"Parent_Hierarchy\\\"] = parent_hierarchy\\n    return doc\\n\\ndef _get_version_rows(entity_doc: dict, entity_name: str) -> list:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Return all rows matching this entity_name (version).\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    return [r for r in entity_doc[\\\"Values\\\"] if r[E_VERSION] == entity_name]\\n\\ndef _remove_version_rows(entity_doc: dict, entity_name: str) -> int:\\n    original = len(entity_doc[\\\"Values\\\"])\\n    entity_doc[\\\"Values\\\"] = [r for r in entity_doc[\\\"Values\\\"]\\n                             if r[E_VERSION] != entity_name]\\n    return original - len(entity_doc[\\\"Values\\\"])\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Public API\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef do_validate(manifest_path_arg: str) -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Verify MFDB integrity: manifest existence, entity files, and row counts.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    manifest_path = Path(manifest_path_arg).resolve()\\n    if not manifest_path.exists():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": \\\"Manifest not found.\\\"}\\n    \\n    errors = []\\n    try:\\n        manifest_doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(manifest_path))\\n        m_fields = [f[\\\"name\\\"] for f in manifest_doc[\\\"Fields\\\"]]\\n        en_idx = m_fields.index(\\\"entity_name\\\")\\n        fp_idx = m_fields.index(\\\"file_path\\\")\\n        rc_idx = m_fields.index(\\\"record_count\\\")\\n\\n        for row in manifest_doc[\\\"Values\\\"]:\\n            if row is None or not any(row): continue\\n            if row is None or not any(row): continue\\n            v_name = row[en_idx]\\n            e_rel = row[fp_idx]\\n            e_count = row[rc_idx]\\n            e_abs = manifest_path.parent / e_rel\\n            \\n            if not e_abs.exists():\\n                errors.append(f\\\"Version {v_name}: Entity file missing: {e_rel}\\\")\\n                continue\\n            \\n            try:\\n                entity_doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(e_abs))\\n                actual_count = len(_get_version_rows(entity_doc, v_name))\\n                if actual_count != e_count:\\n                    errors.append(f\\\"Version {v_name}: Count mismatch. Manifest={e_count}, Entity={actual_count}\\\")\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                errors.append(f\\\"Version {v_name}: Error reading entity: {e}\\\")\\n        \\n        if errors:\\n            return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Validation failed with {len(errors)} errors.\\\", \\\"errors\\\": errors}\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": True, \\\"message\\\": \\\"MFDB INTEGRITY VERIFIED (OK)\\\"}\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Validation error: {e}\\\"}\\n\\ndef do_snapshot(target_dir: str) -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Create a full zip snapshot of the MFDB directory.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    target_path = Path(target_dir).resolve()\\n    config = load_or_create_config(target_path)\\n    mfdb_dir = get_mfdb_dir(config)\\n    \\n    if not mfdb_dir.exists():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": \\\"MFDB directory not found. f\\\"snapshot_{config['project_name']}_{ts}.zip\\\"\\n    zip_path = snapshots_dir / zip_name\\n    \\n    try:\\n        with zipfile.ZipFile(str(zip_path), \\\"w\\\", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zf:\\n            for root, _, filenames in os.walk(mfdb_dir):\\n                if \\\"snapshots\\\" in root: continue\\n                for filename in filenames:\\n                    abs_path = Path(root) / filename\\n                    rel_path = abs_path.relative_to(mfdb_dir)\\n                    zf.write(str(abs_path), str(rel_path))\\n        return {\\n            \\\"ok\\\": True, \\n            \\\"message\\\": f\\\"SNAPSHOT CREATED: {zip_name}\\\", \\n            \\\"zip_path\\\": str(zip_path)\\n        }\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Snapshot failed: {e}\\\"}\\n\\ndef get_version_files(manifest_path_arg: str, version: str) -> list:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Return list of file paths in a given version.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    manifest_path = Path(manifest_path_arg).resolve()\\n    entity_name = version_to_entity_name(version)\\n    try:\\n        manifest_doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(manifest_path))\\n        m_fields = [f[\\\"name\\\"] for f in manifest_doc[\\\"Fields\\\"]]\\n        fp_idx = m_fields.index(\\\"file_path\\\")\\n        en_idx = m_fields.index(\\\"entity_name\\\")\\n        row = next((r for r in manifest_doc[\\\"Values\\\"] if r and any(r) and r[en_idx] == entity_name), None)\\n        if not row: return []\\n        entity_abs = manifest_path.parent / row[fp_idx]\\n        entity_doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(entity_abs))\\n        rows = _get_version_rows(entity_doc, entity_name)\\n        return [{\\\"path\\\": r[E_FILE_PATH], \\\"name\\\": r[E_FILE_NAME], \\\"binary\\\": r[E_IS_BINARY]} for r in rows if r and any(r)]\\n    except: return []\\n\\ndef list_versions(manifest_path: str) -> list[dict]:\\n    try:\\n        doc    = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(manifest_path)\\n        fields = [f[\\\"name\\\"] for f in doc[\\\"Fields\\\"]]\\n        result = []\\n        for row in doc[\\\"Values\\\"]:\\n            rd = {}\\n            for i, fname in enumerate(fields):\\n                rd[fname] = row[i] if i < len(row) else None\\n            result.append(rd)\\n        return result\\n    except Exception:\\n        return []\\n\\ndef get_manifest_meta(manifest_path: str) -> dict:\\n    try:\\n        doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(manifest_path)\\n        return {\\n            \\\"DB_Name\\\":           \\\"Created_At\\\":     doc.get(\\\"Created_At\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n        }\\n    except Exception:\\n        return {}\\n\\ndef do_chunk(target_dir: str, changelog: str = \\\"\\\",\\n             tags: str = \\\"\\\") -> dict:\\n    try:\\n        target_path = Path(target_dir).resolve()\\n        if not target_path.is_dir():\\n            return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Not a directory: {target_dir}\\\", \\\"detail\\\": {}}\\n    \\n        config         = load_or_create_config(target_path)\\n        version        = config[\\\"version\\\"]\\n        entity_name    = version_to_entity_name(version)\\n        incl_b64       = config.get(\\\"include_binary_base64\\\", False)\\n    \\n        manifest_path = init_manifest(config)\\n    \\n        if version_exists_in_manifest(manifest_path, entity_name):\\n            return {\\n                \\\"ok\\\":      False,\\n                \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Version '{version}' already exists. Bump the version first.\\\",\\n                \\\"detail\\\":  {},\\n            }\\n    \\n        entity_path      = str(get_entity_path(config))\\n        mfdb_dir         = get_mfdb_dir(config)\\n        data_dir         = mfdb_dir / \\\"data\\\"\\n        parent_hierarchy = os.path.relpath(manifest_path, str(data_dir))\\n        entity_rel_path  = f\\\"data/{Path(entity_path).name}\\\"\\n    \\n        entity_doc = _load_or_create_entity(entity_path, config, parent_hierarchy)\\n        if changelog:\\n            entity_doc[\\\"Changelog_Latest\\\"] = f\\\"[{entity_name}] {changelog}\\\"\\n    \\n        new_rows   = []\\n        file_count = 0\\n        skipped    = []\\n    \\n        for f_path in collect_project_files(target_path, config):\\n            try:\\n                rel_path = str(f_path.relative_to(target_path))\\n                binary   = is_binary(f_path)\\n                is_b64   = False\\n                if binary and incl_b64:\\n                    raw     = f_path.read_bytes()\\n                    content = base64.b64encode(raw).decode(\\\"ascii\\\")\\n                    is_b64  = True\\n                else:\\n                    content = \\\"\\\" if binary else f_path.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n                new_rows.append([entity_name, rel_path, f_path.name, content, binary, is_b64])\\n                file_count += 1\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                skipped.append(f\\\"{f_path.name}: {e}\\\")\\n    \\n        entity_doc[\\\"Values\\\"].extend(new_rows)\\n        _write_entity(entity_path, entity_doc)\\n    \\n        # Append manifest row\\n        manifest_doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(manifest_path)\\n        manifest_doc[\\\"Values\\\"].append([\\n            entity_name,\\n            entity_rel_path,\\n            f\\\"Project version {version}\\\",\\n            file_count,\\n            version,\\n            \\\"file_path\\\",\\n            changelog or \\\"\\\",\\n            now_iso(),\\n            tags or \\\"\\\",\\n        ])\\n        _write_manifest(manifest_path, manifest_doc)\\n    \\n        return {\\n            \\\"ok\\\":      True,\\n            \\\"message\\\": f\\\"VERSION {version} CHUNKED SUCCESSFULLY\\\",\\n            \\\"detail\\\":  {\\n                \\\"version\\\":     version,\\n                \\\"entity_name\\\": entity_name,\\n                \\\"file_count\\\":  file_count,\\n                \\\"skipped\\\":     skipped,\\n                \\\"entity_path\\\": entity_path,\\n                \\\"manifest\\\":    manifest_path,\\n            },\\n        }\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"CRITICAL CHUNK ERROR: {str(e)}\\\", \\\"detail\\\": {}}\\n\\ndef do_chunk_template(target_dir: str, template_name: str = \\\"\\\") -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Create a reusable template snapshot zip in:\\n      <output>/<project>_MFDB/templates/\\n    Template can be restored later with do_unchunk_template.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    target_path = Path(target_dir).resolve()\\n    if not target_path.is_dir():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Not a directory: {target_dir}\\\", \\\"template_path\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\n    config = load_or_create_config(target_path)\\n    templates_dir = get_templates_dir(config)\\n    templates_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n\\n    files_to_pack = collect_project_files(target_path, config)\\n    if not files_to_pack:\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": \\\"No files matched chunker config filters.\\\", \\\"template_path\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\n    base_name = sanitize_name(template_name.strip()) if template_name else \\\"\\\"\\n    if not base_name:\\n        base_name = f\\\"{sanitize_name(config['project_name'])}_{version_to_entity_name(config['version'])}\\\"\\n    zip_name = f\\\"{base_name}.template.zip\\\"\\n    zip_path = templates_dir / zip_name\\n    if zip_path.exists():\\n        zip_name = f\\\"{base_name}_{get_timestamp()}.template.zip\\\"\\n        zip_path = templates_dir / zip_name\\n\\n    meta = {\\n        \\\"template_name\\\": base_name,\\n        \\\"project_name\\\": config[\\\"project_name\\\"],\\n        \\\"project_version\\\": config[\\\"version\\\"],\\n        \\\"created_at\\\": now_iso(),\\n        \\\"source_dir\\\": str(target_path),\\n        \\\"file_count\\\": len(files_to_pack),\\n        \\\"extensions\\\": list(config.get(\\\"extensions\\\", [])),\\n    }\\n\\n    try:\\n        with zipfile.ZipFile(str(zip_path), \\\"w\\\", zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zf:\\n            zf.writestr(TEMPLATE_META_FILE, json.dumps(meta, indent=2, ensure_ascii=False))\\n            for file_path in files_to_pack:\\n                rel_path = str(file_path.relative_to(target_path))\\n                zf.write(str(file_path), rel_path)\\n        return {\\n            \\\"ok\\\": True,\\n            \\\"message\\\": f\\\"TEMPLATE CHUNKED: {zip_name}\\\",\\n            \\\"template_path\\\": str(zip_path),\\n            \\\"file_count\\\": len(files_to_pack),\\n        }\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": str(e), \\\"template_path\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\ndef do_unchunk_template(target_dir: str, template_name: str = \\\"\\\", out_dir: str = \\\"\\\") -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Restore a template snapshot zip from:\\n      <output>/<project>_MFDB/templates/\\n    If template_name is empty, restores the latest template zip.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    target_path = Path(target_dir).resolve()\\n    if not target_path.is_dir():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Not a directory: {target_dir}\\\", \\\"out_dir\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\n    config = load_or_create_config(target_path)\\n    templates_dir = get_templates_dir(config)\\n    if not templates_dir.exists():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Templates directory not found: {templates_dir}\\\", \\\"out_dir\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\n    selected_zip: Path | None = None\\n    if template_name:\\n        candidate = Path(template_name)\\n        if candidate.exists() and candidate.is_file():\\n            selected_zip = candidate.resolve()\\n        else:\\n            normalized = sanitize_name(template_name.strip())\\n            if normalized.endswith(\\\".template.zip\\\"):\\n                expected = templates_dir / normalized\\n            else:\\n                expected = templates_dir / f\\\"{normalized}.template.zip\\\"\\n            if expected.exists():\\n                selected_zip = expected\\n    if selected_zip is None:\\n        all_templates = sorted(templates_dir.glob(\\\"*.template.zip\\\"), key=lambda p: p.stat().st_mtime, reverse=True)\\n        if not all_templates:\\n            return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": \\\"No template zips found.\\\", \\\"out_dir\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n        selected_zip = all_templates[0]\\n\\n    if out_dir:\\n        output_path = Path(out_dir).resolve()\\n    else:\\n        output_path = get_mfdb_dir(config).parent / \\\"unchunked_templates\\\" / f\\\"{sanitize_name(config['project_name'])}_{selected_zip.stem}_{get_timestamp()}\\\"\\n    output_path.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n\\n    try:\\n        with zipfile.ZipFile(str(selected_zip), \\\"r\\\") as zf:\\n            safe_extract_zip(zf, output_path)\\n        meta_path = output_path / TEMPLATE_META_FILE\\n        if meta_path.exists():\\n            meta_path.unlink()\\n        return {\\n            \\\"ok\\\": True,\\n            \\\"message\\\": f\\\"TEMPLATE RESTORED: {selected_zip.name}\\\",\\n            \\\"out_dir\\\": str(output_path),\\n            \\\"template_path\\\": str(selected_zip),\\n        }\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": str(e), \\\"out_dir\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\ndef do_bump(target_dir: str, part: str = \\\"patch\\\") -> dict:\\n    target_path = Path(target_dir).resolve()\\n    if not target_path.is_dir():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Not a directory: {target_dir}\\\", \\\"new_version\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n    config      = load_or_create_config(target_path)\\n    old_version = config[\\\"version\\\"]\\n    new_version = bump_version(old_version, part)\\n    config[\\\"version\\\"] = new_version\\n    save_config(target_path, config)\\n    return {\\\"ok\\\": True, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"{old_version} \\u2192 {new_version}\\\", \\\"new_version\\\": new_version}\\n\\ndef do_unchunk(manifest_path_arg: str, version: str) -> dict:\\n    manifest_path = Path(manifest_path_arg).resolve()\\n    if not manifest_path.exists():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Manifest not found: {manifest_path}\\\", \\\"out_dir\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\n    entity_name = version_to_entity_name(version)\\n    try:\\n        manifest_doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(manifest_path))\\n        m_fields     = [f[\\\"name\\\"] for f in manifest_doc[\\\"Fields\\\"]]\\n        fp_idx       = m_fields.index(\\\"file_path\\\")\\n        en_idx       = m_fields.index(\\\"entity_name\\\")\\n\\n        row = next((r for r in manifest_doc[\\\"Values\\\"]\\n                    if r[en_idx] == entity_name), None)\\n        if row is None:\\n            return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Version '{version}' not found in manifest.\\\",\\n                    \\\"out_dir\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\n        entity_abs = manifest_path.parent / row[fp_idx]\\n        if not entity_abs.exists():\\n            return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Entity file missing: {entity_abs}\\\",\\n                    \\\"out_dir\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\n        entity_doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(entity_abs))\\n        version_rows = _get_version_rows(entity_doc, entity_name)\\n\\n        if not version_rows:\\n            return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"No file records found for version '{version}'.\\\",\\n                    \\\"out_dir\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\n        db_name = manifest_doc.get(\\\"DB_Name\\\", \\\"project\\\")\\n        out_dir = manifest_path.parent.parent / \\\"unchunked\\\" / \\\\\\n                  f\\\"{db_name}_{entity_name}_{get_timestamp()}\\\"\\n        out_dir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n\\n        file_count = 0\\n        for record in version_rows:\\n            rel_path = record[E_FILE_PATH]\\n            if not rel_path:\\n                continue\\n            out_file = out_dir / rel_path\\n            out_file.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n            is_b64 = len(record) > E_IS_BASE64 and record[E_IS_BASE64]\\n            if is_b64:\\n                out_file.write_bytes(base64.b64decode(record[E_CONTENT]))\\n            elif record[E_IS_BINARY]:\\n                out_file.touch()\\n            else:\\n                out_file.write_text(record[E_CONTENT] or \\\"\\\", encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n            file_count += 1\\n\\n        return {\\n            \\\"ok\\\":        True,\\n            \\\"message\\\":   f\\\"VERSION {version} RESTORED TO DISK\\\",\\n            \\\"out_dir\\\":   str(out_dir),\\n            \\\"file_count\\\": file_count,\\n        }\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": str(e), \\\"out_dir\\\": \\\"\\\"}\\n\\ndef do_update_changelog(manifest_path_arg: str, version: str,\\n                        new_changelog: str) -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Edit changelog of an already-chunked version in the manifest only.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    manifest_path = Path(manifest_path_arg).resolve()\\n    if not manifest_path.exists():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": \\\"Manifest not found.\\\"}\\n\\n    entity_name = version_to_entity_name(version)\\n    try:\\n        doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(manifest_path))\\n        found = False\\n        for row in doc[\\\"Values\\\"]:\\n            if row[M_ENTITY_NAME] == entity_name:\\n                # Extend row if it's an older manifest without all fields\\n                while len(row) < len(MANIFEST_FIELDS):\\n                    row.append(\\\"\\\")\\n                row[M_CHANGELOG] = new_changelog\\n                found = True\\n                break\\n        if not found:\\n            return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"Version '{version}' not found.\\\"}\\n        _write_manifest(str(manifest_path), doc)\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": True, \\\"message\\\": f\\\"CHANGELOG UPDATED FOR {version}\\\"}\\n    except Exception as e:\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": str(e)}\\n\\ndef do_update_tags(manifest_path_arg: str, version: str,\\n                   tags: str) -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Set tags (comma-separated string) on a version in the manifest.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    manifest_path = Path(manifest_path_arg).resolve()\\n    if not manifest_path.exists():\\n        return {\\\"ok\\\": False, \\\"message\\\": \\\"Manifest not found.\\\"}\\n\\n    entity_name = version_to_entity_name(version)\\n    try:\\n        doc = BEJSONCore.bejson_core_load_file(str(manifest_path))\\n        found = False\\n        for row in (skip existing) | \\\"prefix\\\" (rename with _imp suffix)\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    manifest_path = Path(manifest_path_arg).resolve()\\n    zip_path      = Path(zip_path_arg).resolve()\\n\\n    if not        final_entity_name = imp_entity_name\\n                if imp_entity_name in existing_entity_names:\\n                    if on_conflict == \\\"reject\\\":\\n E_INVALID_RECORDS_TYPE)\\n    count = len(rt)\\n    if version in (\\\"104\\\", \\\"104a\\\"):\\n        if count != 1:\\n            raise BEJSONValidationError(f\\\"BEJSON {version} must have Found {count}.\\\", E_INVALID_RECORDS_TYPE)\\n    elif version == \\\"104db\\\":\\n        if count < 2:\\n            raise BEJSONValidationError(\\\"104db requires 2+ types\\\", E_INVALID_RECORDS_TYPE)\\n\\ndef bejson_validator_check_record_type_parent(doc, version):\\n    if version != \\\"104db\\\": return True\\n    fields = doc[\\\"Fields\\\"]\\n    if not fields or fields[0].get(\\\"name\\\") != \\\"Record_Type_Parent\\\":\\n        raise BEJSONValidationError(\\\"104db first field must be 'Record_Type_Parent'\\\", E_INVALID_RECORD_TYPE_PARENT)\\n    valid_types = set(doc[\\\"Records_Type\\\"])\\n    for i, record in enumerate(doc[\\\"Values\\\"]):\\n        if not record: continue\\n        rtp = record[0]\\n        if rtp not in valid_types:\\n            raise BEJSONValidationError(f\\\"Invalid Record_Type_Parent '{rtp}' at row {i}\\\", E_INVALID_RECORD_TYPE_PARENT)\\n    return True\\n\\ndef bejson_validator_check_fields_structure(doc, version):\\n    fields = doc[\\\"Fields\\\"]\\n    for i, f in enumerate(fields):\\n        fname = f.get(\\\"name\\\")\\n        ftype = f.get(\\\"type\\\")\\n        if not fname or not ftype:\\n            raise bejson_validator_check_values(doc, version, fields_count):\\n    fields = doc[\\\"Fields\\\"]\\n    for i, record in enumerate(doc[\\\"Values\\\"]):\\n        if len(record) != fields_count:\\n            raise BEJSONValidationError(f\\\"Length mismatch at row {i}\\\", E_RECORD_LENGTH_MISMATCH)\\n        for j, val in enumerate(record):\\n            ftype = fields[j].get(\\\"type\\\")\\n BEJSONValidationError(f\\\"Type mismatch at row {i}, col {j} ({fields[j]['name']}): expected string\\\", E_TYPE_MISMATCH)\\n            elif ftype == \\\"integer\\\" and (not isinstance(val, int) or isinstance(val, bool)):\\n                 raise BEJSONValidationError(f\\\"Type mismatch at row {i}, col {j} ({fields[j]['name']}): expected integer\\\", E_TYPE_MISMATCH)\\n            elif ftype == \\\"number\\\" and (not isinstance(val, (int, float)) or isinstance(val, bool)):\\n                 # bool is a subclass of int in Python, so True/False pass isinstance(int,float).\\n                 # Explicitly exclude bool \\u2014 BEJSON \\\"number\\\" means a numeric value, not a boolean.\\n                 raise BEJSONValidationError(f\\\"Type mismatch at row {i}, col {j} ({fields[j]['name']}): expected number, got bool\\\", E_TYPE_MISMATCH)\\n            elif ftype == \\\"boolean\\\" and not isinstance(val, bool):\\n                 raise BEJSONValidationError(f\\\"Type mismatch at row {i}, col {j} ({fields[j]['name']}): expected boolean\\\", E_TYPE_MISMATCH)\\n            elif ftype == \\\"array\\\" and not isinstance(val, list):\\n                 raise BEJSONValidationError(f\\\"Type mismatch at row {i}, col {j} ({fields[j]['name']}): expected array\\\", E_TYPE_MISMATCH)\\n            elif ftype == \\\"object\\\" and not      if version in (\\\"104\\\", \\\"104db\\\"):\\n            raise BEJSONValidationError(f\\\"Custom key '{key}' forbidden in {version}\\\", E_RESERVED_KEY_COLLISION)\\n        # 104a: Custom headers allowed, no strict PascalCase enforcement\\n        # Audit 2 Finding: Removed warning to avoid conflict with 104db rigidity.\\n\\ndef validate_bejson(input_data: Union[str, dict], is_file: Valid: \\\" + str(res.valid))\\n    if res.errors:\\n        lines.append(\\\"  Errors:\\\")\\n        for e in res.errors:\\n            lines.append(\\\"    - \\\" + e)\\n    if res.warnings:\\n E_INVALID_FORMAT)\\n    return True\\n\\ndef bejson_validator_validate_file(file_path):\\n    res = validate_bejson(file_path, is_file=True)\\n    if not res.valid:\\n        raise _read_file_content(path: str) -> Optional[str]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Reads file content, supporting .mfdb.zip archives.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    p = Path(path)\\n    try:\\n        if p.is_file() and not path.lower().endswith(\\\".zip\\\"):\\n            return p.read_text(encoding=\\\"utf-8\\\")\\n        \\n        # Check for zip path parts\\n        parts = p.parts\\n        for i, part in enumerate(parts):\\n            if part.lower().endswith(\\\".zip\\\"):\\n                zip_path = str(Path(*parts[:i+1]))\\n                inner_path = \\\"/\\\".join(parts[i+1:])\\n E_MFDB_CORE_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND\\n        )\\n    return doc\\n\\ndef _write_entity_doc(doc: dict, entity_path: str) -> None:\\n    if not bejson_core_atomic_write(entity_path, doc):\\n        raise bejson_core_atomic_write(manifest_path, doc):\\n        raise MFDBCoreError(f\\\"Failed to write manifest doc to {manifest_path}\\\", E_MFDB_CORE_WRITE_FAILED)\\n\\ndef _update_manifest_record_count(\\n    manifest_path: str, entity_name: str, count: int\\n) -> None:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Write a corrected record_count into the manifest for one entity.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    doc        = _load_json(manifest_path)\\n    fn_list    = [f[\\\"name\\\"] for f in doc[\\\"Fields\\\"]]\\n    if \\\"record_count\\\" not in fn_list or \\\"entity_name\\\" not in fn_list:\\n        return\\n    rc_idx = fn_list.index(\\\"record_count\\\")\\n    en_idx = fn_list.index(\\\"entity_name\\\")\\n    for row in doc[\\\"Values\\\"]:\\n        if row[en_idx] == entity_name:\\n            row[rc_idx] = count\\n            break\\n    _write_manifest_doc(doc, manifest_path)\\n\\ndef _calculate_file_hash(file_path: str) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Generate SHA-256 hash for archive integrity checks.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    sha256 = hashlib.sha256()\\n    with open(file_path, \\\"rb\\\") as f:\\n        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(4096), b\\\"\\\"):\\n            sha256.update(chunk)\\n    return sha256.hexdigest()\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# MFDBArchive (v1.2 Feature)\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nclass MFDBArchive:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Handles .mfdb.zip packaging, virtual mounting, and atomic repacking.\\n    Standardized in MFDB v1.2 for portable transport.\\n    Enhanced for CoreEvolution with sticky mounting and validation safety.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    @staticmethod\\n    def mount(archive_path: str, target_dir: str, force: bool = False, sticky: bool = True) -> str:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Extract an MFDB archive to a workspace and create a session lock.\\n        If sticky=True, it reuses existing valid extracted files.\\n        Returns the absolute path to the extracted manifest.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        arc_p = Path(archive_path)\\n        if not arc_p.exists():\\n            raise MFDBCoreError(f\\\"Archive not found: {archive_path}\\\", E_MFDB_CORE_ARCHIVE_ERROR)\\n\\n        target_p = Path(target_dir)\\n        lock_file = target_p / \\\".mfdb_lock\\\"\\n        manifest_path = target_p / \\\"104a.mfdb.bejson\\\"\\n\\n        # Sticky check: If valid files exist and hash matches, just return manifest\\n        if sticky and lock_file.exists() and manifest_path.exists():\\n            try:\\n                with open(lock_file, \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n                    lock_data = json.load(f)\\n                \\n                # Check if archive hash matches the one we mounted\\n                current_arc_hash = _calculate_file_hash(archive_path)\\n                if lock_data.get(\\\"original_hash\\\") == current_arc_hash:\\n                    # Validate the database structure before trusting the sticky mount\\n                    from lib_mfdb_validator import mfdb_validator_validate_database\\n                    if mfdb_validator_validate_database(str(manifest_path)):\\n                        return str(manifest_path.absolute())\\n            except Exception:\\n                pass # Fall through to full re-extract if sticky fails\\n\\n        if lock_file.exists() and not force:\\n            with open(lock_file, \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n                lock_data = json.load(f)\\n            if lock_data.get(\\\"pid\\\") != os.getpid():\\n                raise MFDBCoreError(\\n                    f\\\"Workspace {target_dir} is already locked by PID {lock_data.get('pid')}\\\",\\n                    E_MFDB_CORE_MOUNT_CONFLICT\\n                )\\n\\n        # Clear existing workspace if it was invalid or if we are forcing re-extract\\n        if target_p.exists():\\n            shutil.rmtree(target_dir)\\n        target_p.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)\\n\\n        with zipfile.ZipFile(archive_path, 'r') as zip_ref:\\n            # Secure extraction loop to mitigate Zip Slip.\\n            from lib_bejson_path_guard import bejson_safe_join\\n            for member in zip_ref.namelist():\\n                # Skip directories as safe_join/open will handle them\\n                if member.endswith('/'): continue\\n                \\n                # Boundary check via safe_join\\n                try:\\n                    safe_path = bejson_safe_join(target_dir, member)\\n                    # Ensure parent directory exists\\n                    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(safe_path), exist_ok=True)\\n                    with zip_ref.open(member) as source, open(safe_path, \\\"wb\\\") as target:\\n                        shutil.copyfileobj(source, target)\\n                except ValueError as e:\\n                    logging.error(f\\\"[MFDB_CORE] Security Alert: {e}\\\")\\n                    raise MFDBCoreError(f\\\"Secure extraction failed: {e}\\\", E_MFDB_CORE_ARCHIVE_ERROR)\\n\\n        if not manifest_path.exists():\\n            shutil.rmtree(target_dir)\\n            raise MFDBCoreError(\\\"Invalid MFDB Archive: 104a.mfdb.bejson missing.\\\", E_MFDB_CORE_ARCHIVE_ERROR)\\n\\n        # Create session lock with metadata\\n        lock_data = {\\n            \\\"pid\\\": os.getpid(),\\n            \\\"mounted_at\\\": datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat(),\\n            \\\"original_hash\\\": _calculate_file_hash(archive_path),\\n            \\\"archive_path\\\": str(arc_p.absolute())\\n        }\\n        with open(lock_file, \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n            json.dump(lock_data, f)\\n\\n        return str(manifest_path.absolute())\\n\\n    @staticmethod\\n    def commit(mount_dir: str, archive_path: Optional[str] = None, validate: bool = True) -> str:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Repack the workspace into a .mfdb.zip file atomically.\\n        Refuses to write if validation fails (if validate=True).\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        mount_p = Path(mount_dir)\\n        lock_file = mount_p / \\\".mfdb_lock\\\"\\n        manifest_path = mount_p / \\\"104a.mfdb.bejson\\\"\\n        \\n        if not lock_file.exists():\\n            raise MFDBCoreError(f\\\"No active mount session found in {mount_dir}\\\", E_MFDB_CORE_INVALID_OPERATION)\\n\\n        if validate:\\n            if not manifest_path.exists():\\n                raise MFDBCoreError(\\\"Commit rejected: Manifest missing in workspace.\\\", E_MFDB_CORE_WRITE_FAILED)\\n            \\n            # Run full database validation before repacking\\n            from lib_mfdb_validator import mfdb_validator_validate_database\\n            try:\\n                mfdb_validator_validate_database(str(manifest_path))\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                raise MFDBCoreError(f\\\"Commit rejected: Validation failed. {str(e)}\\\", E_MFDB_CORE_WRITE_FAILED)\\n\\n        with open(lock_file, \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            lock_data = json.load(f)\\n\\n        dest_path = archive_path or lock_data.get(\\\"archive_path\\\")\\n        if not dest_path:\\n            raise MFDBCoreError(\\\"Destination archive path unknown.\\\", E_MFDB_CORE_ARCHIVE_ERROR)\\n\\n        # Create new archive in temp location\\n        fd, temp_arc = tempfile.mkstemp(suffix=\\\".mfdb.zip\\\")\\n        os.close(fd)\\n\\n        try:\\n            with zipfile.ZipFile(temp_arc, 'w', zipfile.ZIP_DEFLATED) as zipf:\\n                for root, dirs, files in os.walk(mount_dir):\\n                    for file in files:\\n                        if file == \\\".mfdb_lock\\\": continue\\n                        file_path = Path(root) / file\\n                        arc_name = file_path.relative_to(mount_dir)\\n                        zipf.write(file_path, arc_name)\\n            \\n            # Atomic swap\\n            shutil.move(temp_arc, dest_path)\\n            \\n            # Update lock with new hash to maintain sticky state\\n            lock_data[\\\"original_hash\\\"] = _calculate_file_hash(dest_path)\\n            with open(lock_file, \\\"w\\\") as f:\\n                json.dump(lock_data, f)\\n                \\n        except Exception as e:\\n            if os.path.exists(temp_arc): os.remove(temp_arc)\\n            raise MFDBCoreError(f\\\"Commit failed: {str(e)}\\\", E_MFDB_CORE_WRITE_FAILED)\\n\\n        return dest_path\\n\\n    @staticmethod\\n    def resurrect_file(mount_dir: str, relative_path: str) -> bool:\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Surgically extract a single file from the .mfdb.zip archive into the workspace.\\n        Used for recovery when an entity file is missing or corrupted.\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        mount_p = Path(mount_dir)\\n        lock_file = mount_p / \\\".mfdb_lock\\\"\\n        if not lock_file.exists():\\n            return False\\n\\n        with open(lock_file, \\\"r\\\") as f:\\n            lock_data = json.load(f)\\n        \\n        archive_path = lock_data.get(\\\"archive_path\\\")\\n        if not archive_path or not os.path.exists(archive_path):\\n            return False\\n\\n        try:\\n            with zipfile.ZipFile(archive_path, 'r') as zip_ref:\\n                # Check if file exists in zip\\n                if relative_path in zip_ref.namelist():\\n                    zip_ref.extract(relative_path, mount_dir)\\n                    return True\\n        except Exception:\\n            pass\\n        return False\\n\\n    @staticmethod\\n    def unmount(mount_dir: str, cleanup: bool = True):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"Release the lock and optionally delete the workspace.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        mount_p = Path(mount_dir)\\n        lock_file = mount_p / \\\".mfdb_lock\\\"\\n        if lock_file.exists():\\n            os.remove(lock_file)\\n        if cleanup and mount_p.exists():\\n            shutil.rmtree(mount_dir)\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Discovery\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef mfdb_core_discover(file_path: str) -> str:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Identify the MFDB role of any file.\\n    Returns one of: 'manifest', 'entity', 'archive', 'standalone'\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    p = Path(file_path)\\n    if not p.exists():\\n        raise MFDBCoreError(f\\\"File not found: {file_path}\\\", E_MFDB_CORE_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND)\\n\\n    if p.suffix == \\\".zip\\\" and \\\".mfdb\\\" in p.name:\\n        return \\\"archive\\\"\\n\\n    try:\\n        doc = _load_json(file_path)\\n    except Exception:\\n        return \\\"standalone\\\"\\n\\n    version  = doc.get(\\\"Format_Version\\\", \\\"\\\")\\n    filename = p.name\\n    if version == \\\"104a\\\" and filename.endswith(\\\".mfdb.bejson\\\"):\\n        return \\\"manifest\\\"\\n    if version == \\\"104\\\" and doc.get(\\\"Parent_Hierarchy\\\"):\\n        return \\\"entity\\\"\\n    return \\\"standalone\\\"\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Recovery & Repair (v1.21 Feature)\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef mfdb_core_deep_verify(manifest_path: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Performs a deep audit of the entire MFDB database.\\n    Checks for:\\n      - Positional integrity (field vs value length)\\n      - Type adherence (basic primitives)\\n      - Manifest-entity consistency (record counts)\\n      - Foreign key potential breakage (optional warnings)\\n    Returns a list of finding dicts.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    findings = []\\n    manifest_doc = bejson_core_load_file(manifest_path)\\n    entries = _rows_as_dicts(manifest_doc)\\n    \\n    for entry in entries:\\n        entity_name = entry.get(\\\"entity_name\\\")\\n        file_path_rel = entry.get(\\\"file_path\\\")\\n        expected_count = entry.get(\\\"record_count\\\")\\n        \\n        entity_path = _resolve_entity_path(manifest_path, file_path_rel)\\n        if not os.path.exists(entity_path):\\n            findings.append({\\\"entity\\\": entity_name, \\\"error\\\": \\\"MISSING_FILE\\\", \\\"path\\\": file_path_rel})\\n            continue\\n            \\n        try:\\n            entity_doc = bejson_core_load_file(entity_path)\\n            # 1. Check positional integrity\\n            fields = entity_doc.get(\\\"Fields\\\", [])\\n            field_count = len(fields)\\n            values = entity_doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", [])\\n            actual_count = len(values)\\n            \\n            if expected_count is not None and expected_count != actual_count:\\n                findings.append({\\n                    \\\"entity\\\": entity_name, \\n                    \\\"warning\\\": \\\"COUNT_MISMATCH\\\", \\n                    \\\"expected\\\": expected_count, \\n                    \\\"actual\\\": actual_count\\n                })\\n            \\n            for i, row in enumerate(values):\\n                if len(row) != field_count:\\n                    findings.append({\\n                        \\\"entity\\\": entity_name, \\n                        \\\"error\\\": \\\"POSITIONAL_VIOLATION\\\", \\n                        \\\"row\\\": i, \\n                        \\\"expected\\\": field_count, \\n                        \\\"actual\\\": len(row)\\n                    })\\n                \\n                # 2. Basic Type verification\\n                for j, val in enumerate(row):\\n                    if val is None: continue\\n                    f_type = fields[j].get(\\\"type\\\")\\n                    if f_type == \\\"integer\\\" and not isinstance(val, int):\\n                         findings.append({\\\"entity\\\": entity_name, \\\"warning\\\": \\\"TYPE_MISMATCH\\\", \\\"row\\\": i, \\\"field\\\": fields[j][\\\"name\\\"], \\\"expected\\\": \\\"integer\\\", \\\"actual\\\": type(val).__name__})\\n                    elif f_type == \\\"number\\\" and not isinstance(val, (int, float)):\\n                         findings.append({\\\"entity\\\": entity_name, \\\"warning\\\": \\\"TYPE_MISMATCH\\\", \\\"row\\\": i, \\\"field\\\": fields[j][\\\"name\\\"], \\\"expected\\\": \\\"number\\\", \\\"actual\\\": type(val).__name__})\\n                    elif f_type == \\\"boolean\\\" and not isinstance(val, bool):\\n                         findings.append({\\\"entity\\\": entity_name, \\\"warning\\\": \\\"TYPE_MISMATCH\\\", \\\"row\\\": i, \\\"field\\\": fields[j][\\\"name\\\"], \\\"expected\\\": \\\"boolean\\\", \\\"actual\\\": type(val).__name__})\\n\\n        except Exception as e:\\n            findings.append({\\\"entity\\\": entity_name, \\\"error\\\": \\\"CORRUPT_JSON\\\", \\\"message\\\": str(e)})\\n            \\n    return findings\\n\\ndef mfdb_core_self_heal(manifest_path: str) -> Dict[str, Any]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Attempts to fix common issues identified by deep_verify.\\n    Actions:\\n      - Resyncs record_count in manifest.\\n      - Padds short records with nulls (Positional Repair).\\n      - Removes invalid records if necessary (Extreme measure).\\n    Returns a report of actions taken.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    report = {\\\"actions\\\": [], \\\"remaining_errors\\\": []}\\n    findings = mfdb_core_deep_verify(manifest_path)\\n    \\n    needs_manifest_sync = False\\n    \\n    for f in findings:\\n        entity = f.get(\\\"entity\\\")\\n        if f.get(\\\"warning\\\") == \\\"COUNT_MISMATCH\\\":\\n            _update_manifest_record_count(manifest_path, entity, f[\\\"actual\\\"])\\n            report[\\\"actions\\\"].append(f\\\"Resynced record_count for {entity} to {f['actual']}\\\")\\n        \\n        elif f.get(\\\"error\\\") == \\\"POSITIONAL_VIOLATION\\\":\\n            # Attempt repair\\n            entity_path = _get_entity_path(manifest_path, entity)\\n            try:\\n                doc = bejson_core_load_file(entity_path)\\n                field_count = len(doc[\\\"Fields\\\"])\\n                repaired = 0\\n                for i, row in enumerate(doc[\\\"Values\\\"]):\\n                    if len(row) < field_count:\\n                        doc[\\\"Values\\\"][i] = row + [None] * (field_count - len(row))\\n                        repaired += 1\\n                    elif len(row) > field_count:\\n                        doc[\\\"Values\\\"][i] = row[:field_count]\\n                        repaired += 1\\n                if repaired > 0:\\n                    bejson_core_atomic_write(entity_path, doc)\\n                    report[\\\"actions\\\"].append(f\\\"Repaired {repaired} positional violations in {entity}\\\")\\n            except Exception as e:\\n                report[\\\"remaining_errors\\\"].append(f\\\"Failed to repair {entity}: {str(e)}\\\")\\n        \\n        elif f.get(\\\"error\\\") == \\\"MISSING_FILE\\\":\\n            # Attempt resurrection\\n            mount_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(manifest_path))\\n            if MFDBArchive.resurrect_file(mount_dir, f[\\\"path\\\"]):\\n                report[\\\"actions\\\"].append(f\\\"Resurrected missing entity file: {f['path']}\\\")\\n            else:\\n                report[\\\"remaining_errors\\\"].append(f\\\"Could not resurrect {entity}\\\")\\n        \\n        elif f.get(\\\"error\\\"):\\n            report[\\\"remaining_errors\\\"].append(f\\\"{entity}: {f['error']} - {f.get('message', '')}\\\")\\n\\n    return report\\n\\ndef _mfdb_core_repair_hierarchy(entity_path: str, new_hierarchy: str) -> bool:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Surgically update the Parent_Hierarchy header in a BEJSON 104 file.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    try:\\n        doc = bejson_core_load_file(entity_path)\\n        doc[\\\"Parent_Hierarchy\\\"] = new_hierarchy\\n        bejson_core_atomic_write(entity_path, doc)\\n        return True\\n    except Exception:\\n        return False\\n\\ndef mfdb_core_smart_repair(manifest_path: str, error: MFDBValidationError) -> bool:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Attempt to automatically repair the MFDB workspace based on a validation error.\\n    Supported:\\n      - E_MFDB_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND (33): Resurrects from archive.\\n      - E_MFDB_BIDIRECTIONAL_FAIL (38) / E_MFDB_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND (37): Patches Parent_Hierarchy.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    mount_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(manifest_path))\\n    ctx = error.context\\n\\n    if error.code == E_MFDB_ENTITY_NOT_FOUND or error.code == 33:\\n        rel_path = ctx.get(\\\"file_path_rel\\\")\\n        if rel_path:\\n            return MFDBArchive.resurrect_file(mount_dir, rel_path)\\n\\n    if error.code == E_MFDB_BIDIRECTIONAL_FAIL or error.code == E_MFDB_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND:\\n        entity_path = ctx.get(\\\"actual_path\\\")\\n        new_hierarchy = ctx.get(\\\"suggested_hierarchy\\\")\\n        # If suggested_hierarchy is missing but we are in a mount_dir, \\n        # assume standard v1.21 structure\\n        if not new_hierarchy and entity_path:\\n             new_hierarchy = \\\"../104a.mfdb.bejson\\\"\\n\\n        if entity_path and new_hierarchy:\\n            return _mfdb_core_repair_hierarchy(entity_path, new_hierarchy)\\n\\n    return False\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Read operations\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef mfdb_core_load_manifest(manifest_path: str) -> list[dict]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Validate and load the manifest.\\n    Returns all manifest records as a list of field-name-keyed dicts.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    mfdb_validator_validate_manifest(manifest_path)\\n    doc = _load_json(manifest_path)\\n    if not isinstance(doc, dict):\\n        raise MFDBCoreError(f\\\"Failed to load manifest: {manifest_path} (not a dict)\\\", E_MFDB_CORE_MANIFEST_NOT_FOUND)\\n    return _rows_as_dicts(doc)\\n\\ndef mfdb_core_load_entity(manifest_path: str, entity_name: str) -> list[dict]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Load all records for a named entity.\\n    Returns a list of field-name-keyed dicts (dense - no null-padding).\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    doc = _load_entity_doc(manifest_path, _rows_as_dicts(doc)\\n\\ndef mfdb_core_get_entity_doc(manifest_path: str, entity_name: str) -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Return the raw BEJSON 104 document dict for a named entity.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    return _load_entity_doc(manifest_path, entity_name)\\n\\ndef mfdb_core_get_stats(manifest_path: str) -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Return a summary statistics dict for the entire MFDB.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    doc     = _load_json(manifest_path)\\n    entries = _rows_as_dicts(doc)\\n\\n    entity_stats = []\\n    for entry in entries:\\n        resolved = _resolve_entity_path(manifest_path, entry[\\\"file_path\\\"])\\n        if os.path.exists(resolved):\\n            edoc        = _load_json(resolved)\\n            rec_count   = len(edoc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []))\\n            field_count = len(edoc.get(\\\"Fields\\\", []))\\n        else:\\n            rec_count   = -1\\n            field_count = -1\\n\\n        entity_stats.append({\\n            \\\"entity_name\\\":  entry[\\\"entity_name\\\"],\\n            \\\"file_path\\\":    entry[\\\"file_path\\\"],\\n            \\\"record_count\\\": rec_count,\\n            \\\"field_count\\\":  field_count,\\n            \\\"primary_key\\\":  entry.get(\\\"primary_key\\\"),\\n        })\\n\\n    return {\\n        \\\"db_name\\\":        doc.get(\\\"DB_Name\\\", \\\"\\\"),\\n        \\\"schema_version\\\": ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Query operations\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\ndef mfdb_core_query_entity(\\n    manifest_path: str,\\n    entity_name: str,\\n    predicate: Callable[[dict], bool],\\n) -> list[dict]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Return all records from an entity for which predicate(record) is True.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    records = mfdb_core_load_entity(manifest_path, entity_name)\\n    return [r for r in records if predicate(r)]\\n\\ndef mfdb_core_build_index(\\n    manifest_path: str,\\n    entity_name: str,\\n    field_name: str,\\n) -> dict:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Build an in-memory hash index on a field for fast lookups.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    records = mfdb_core_load_entity(manifest_path, entity_name)\\n    return {r[field_name]: r for r in records if r.get(field_name) is not None}\\n\\ndef mfdb_core_join(\\n    manifest_path: str,\\n    from_entity:   str,\\n    to_entity:     str,\\n    from_fk:       str,\\n    to_pk:         str,\\n) -> list[dict]:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"Cross-entity equi-join.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    from_records = mfdb_core_load_entity(manifest_path, from_entity)\\n    to_index     = mfdb_core_build_index(manifest_path, to_entity, to_pk)\\n\\n    results = []\\n    for record in from_records:\\n        fk_val = record.get(from_fk)\\n        target = to_index.get(fk_val, {})\\n        merged = dict(record)\\n        for k, v in target.items():\\n            merged[f\\\"{to_entity}__{k}\\\"] = v\\n        results.append(merged)\\n\\n    return results\\n\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Write operations\\n# \\\"\\\"\\\"Raised when a BEJSON core operation fails.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    def __init__(self, message: str, code: int = None):\\n        super().__init__(message)\\n        self.code = code\\n\\nclass ResilientPIDLock:\\n    def __init__(self, target_path: Union[str, Path], timeout_seconds: int = 10):\\n        self.target    = Path(target_path)\\n        self.lock_dir  = Path(f\\\"{target_path}.lockdir\\\")\\n        self.meta_file = self.lock_dir / \\\"lock_meta.json\\\"\\n        self.timeout   = timeout_seconds\\n\\n    def acquire(self) -> bool:\\n        start_time = time.time()\\n        while time.time() - start_time < self.timeout:\\n            try:\\n                self.lock_dir.mkdir(exist_ok=False)\\n                self.meta_file.write_text(json.dumps({\\n                    \\\"pid\\\":       os.getpid(),\\n                    \\\"timestamp\\\": int(time.time())\\n                }))\\n                return True\\n            except FileExistsError:\\n                if self.meta_file.exists():\\n                    try:\\n                        meta      = json.loads(self.meta_file.read_text())\\n                        owner_pid = meta.get(\\\"pid\\\")\\n                        if owner_pid:\\n                            os.kill(owner_pid, 0)  # Signal 0: check if alive\\n                    except (ProcessLookupError, OSError):\\n                        # Owner is dead \\u2014 safely reclaim\\n                        self.release()\\n                        continue\\n                    except Exception:\\n                        pass\\n                time.sleep(0.1)\\n        return False\\n\\n    def release(self):\\n        if self.meta_file.exists():\\n            try:\\n                self.meta_file.unlink()\\n            except OSError:\\n                pass\\n        try:\\n            self.lock_dir.rmdir()\\n        except OSError:\\n            pass\\n\\n    def \\\"\\\"\\\"Writes a BEJSON file atomically using a temp file and sync.\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    target_dir = os.path.dirname(os.path.abspath(path))\\n    os.makedirs(target_dir, exist_ok=True)\\n\\n    # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n# Core class\\n# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\nclass HTML3_List_Renderer:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Multi-mode BEJSON 104 list renderer.\\n    Modes: TREE (default), SIDEBAR.\\n\\n    Expected BEJSON 104 field names (all resolved via FK-convention aware lookup):\\n      - id           : unique row identifier\\n      - title        : display label\\n      - description  : optional subtitle / detail text\\n      - parent_id    : parent row id (or \\\"RUN\\\")\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\n    def __init__(self):\\n        pass\\n\\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\\n    # Field-map aware hierarchy builder\\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\\n\\n    def _build_hierarchy(self, doc: dict):\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        Converts a flat BEJSON 104 Values array into a tree structure.\\n\\n        Returns: (roots, item_map, raw_items)\\n          roots     \\u2014 list of top-level item dicts\\n          item_map  \\u2014 {id: item_dict}\\n          raw_items \\u2014 flat list of all item dicts (for JSON serialization)\\n        \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n        f_map = _get_field_map(doc)\\n\\n        # Resolve each semantic field using FK-convention aware lookup\\n        id_idx         = _resolve_field(f_map, \\\"id\\\")\\n        title_idx      = _resolve_field(f_map, \\\"title\\\", \\\"name\\\", \\\"label\\\")\\n        desc_idx       = _resolve_field(f_map, \\\"description\\\", \\\"desc\\\")\\n        pid_idx        = _resolve_field(f_map, \\\"parent_id\\\", \\\"parent_id_fk\\\", \\\"parent\\\")\\n        url_idx        = _resolve_field(f_map, \\\"url\\\", \\\"href\\\", \\\"link\\\")\\n        act_url_idx    = _resolve_field(f_map, \\\"action_url\\\")\\n        act_label_idx  = _resolve_field(f_map, \\\"action_label\\\")\\n\\n        item_map = {}\\n        raw_items = []\\n        roots = []\\n\\n        for row in doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", []):\\n            if not row:\\n                continue\\n            item = {\\n                \\\"id\\\":           _get(row, id_idx),\\n                \\\"title\\\":        _get(row, title_idx) or \\\"(untitled)\\\",\\n                \\\"description\\\":  _get(row, desc_idx),\\n                \\\"parent_id\\\":    _get(row, pid_idx),\\n                \\\"url\\\":          _get(row, url_idx),\\n                \\\"action_url\\\":   _get(row, act_url_idx),\\n                \\\"action_label\\\": _get(row, act_label_idx) or \\\"RUN\\\",\\n                \\\"children\\\":     [],\\n            }\\n raw_items:\\n            pid = item[\\\"parent_id\\\"]\\n            if pid and pid in item_map:\\n                item_map[pid][\\\"children\\\"].append(item)\\n            else:\\n                roots.append(item)\\n\\n        return roots, item_map, raw_items\\n\\n    # -----------------------------------------------------------------------\\n    # Public render entry point\\n    # _render_tree(self, roots, raw_items, cid, title, on_click):\\n        def build_node(item):\\n            has_children = bool(item[\\\"children\\\"])\\n            toggle = \\\"[-]\\\" if has_children else \\\"[o]\\\"\\n\\n            if item[\\\"action_url\\\"]:\\n                action_html = (\\n                    '<div class=\\\"c-html3-list__action\\\">'\\n                    '<a href=\\\"' + Resolve Hint (Priority: param > header > heuristic)\\n    hint = hint or doc.get(\\\"Rendering_Hint\\\")\\n    rt = doc.get(\\\"Records_Type\\\", [])\\n    entity = rt[0] if rt else \\\"Data\\\"\\n    title = title or doc.get(\\\"DB_Name\\\") or entity\\n\\n    # 2. Heuristics if no hint provided\\n    if not hint:\\n        # 104a with few records -> Description List or Stats Bar\\n        if doc.get(\\\"Format_Version\\\") == \\\"104a\\\":\\n            if len(doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", [])) < 8:\\n                hint = \\\"stats\\\"\\n            else:\\n                hint = \\\"table\\\"\\n        # 104 with numeric fields -> Chart candidate\\n        elif doc.get(\\\"Format_Version\\\") == \\\"104\\\":\\n            has_numeric = any(f.get(\\\"type\\\") in (\\\"number\\\", \\\"integer\\\") for f in doc.get(\\\"Fields\\\", []))\\n            if has_numeric and len(doc.get(\\\"Values\\\", [])) > 2:\\n                hint = \\\"chart\\\"\\n            else:\\n                hint = \\\"table\\\"\\n        # 104db -> Multi-table Viewer\\n        elif doc.get(\\\"Format_Version\\\") == \\\"104db\\\":\\n            hint = \\\"viewer\\\"\\n        else:\\n            hint = \\\"table\\\"\\n\\n    # 3. Component Mapping\\n    try:\\n        if hint == \\\"stats\\\":\\n            # Convert values to stats_list format\\n            stats = []\\n            # Optimized Field Mapping (Phase 2: Standardized)\\n            fi = bejson_core_get_field_map(doc)\\n\\n            # Priority 1: standard key/value\\n            # Priority 2: label/value (Bento compat)\\n            label_idx = fi.get(\\\"key\\\", fi.get(\\\"label\\\", -1))\\n            val_idx   = fi.get(\\\"value\\\", -1)\\n\\n            # Fail-safe: exactly 2 fields\\n            if label_idx == -1 or val_idx == -1:\\n                if len(doc.get(\\\"Fields\\\", [])) == 2:\\n                    label_idx, val_idx = 0, 1\\n                else:\\n                    logging.warning(f\\\"[Renderer] Missing standard fields for 'stats' hint in {title}\\\")\\n                    return html_table(doc)\\n\\n            for row in doc[\\\"Values\\\"]:\\n                label = row[label_idx] if label_idx < len(row) else \\\"Unknown\\\"\\n                value = row[val_idx] if val_idx < len(row) else \\\"\\\"\\n                stats.append({\\\"label\\\": label, \\\"value\\\": value})\\n            return html_stats_bar(stats)\\n\\n        elif hint == \\\"chart\\\":\\n            fields = doc[\\\"Fields\\\"]\\n            labels_idx = -1\\n            data_idx = -1\\n            for i, f in enumerate(fields):\\n                if f[\\\"type\\\"] == \\\"string\\\" and labels_idx == -1: labels_idx = i\\n                if f[\\\"type\\\"] in (\\\"number\\\", \\\"integer\\\") and data_idx == -1: data_idx = i\\n            \\n            if labels_idx != -1 and data_idx != -1:\\n                labels = [row[labels_idx] for row in doc[\\\"Values\\\"]]\\n                data = [row[data_idx] for row in doc[\\\"Values\\\"]]\\n                return html_chart(title=title, labels=labels, data=data)\\n            return html_table(doc)\\n\\n        elif hint == \\\"bento\\\":\\n            return html_bento_grid(doc)\\n\\n        elif hint == \\\"viewer\\\":\\n            return bejson_to_html_viewer(doc)\\n\\n        else: # Collect all unique keys from all dictionaries in a single pass\\n    # Using a list for stable order (first seen first kept) but a set for O(1) membership check\\n    all_keys = []\\n    keys_set = set()\\n    for d in data_list:\\n        for k in d.keys():\\n            if k not in keys_set:\\n                all_keys.append(k)\\n                keys_set.add(k)\\n    \\n    # 2. Construct Fields array (Standard 104a)\\n    fields = [{\\\"name\\\": k, \\\"type\\\": \\\"string\\\"} for k in all_keys]\\n    \\n    # 3. Construct Values array ensuring positional integrity\\n    values = [[d.get(k) for k in all_keys] for d in data_list]\\n        \\n    return {\\n        \\\"Format\\\": \\\"BEJSON\\\",\\n        \\\"Format_Version\\\": \\\"104a\\\",\\n        \\\"Format_Creator\\\": \\\"Elton Boehnen\\\",\\n        \\\"Records_Type\\\": [entity_name],\\n        \\\"Fields\\\": fields,\\n        \\\"Values\\\": values\\n    }\\n\\nclass FlaskDashboard:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Streamlines Core-Command dashboard creation in Flask.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    def __init__(self, app=None, d684df8b-cbb9-44dd-860b-120aed5e9e13\\n\\\"\\\"\\\"\\n\\nimport math\\n\\nclass BEJSONPhysics:\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    Mirror of lib_bejson_physics.js\\n    Python/Flask-compatible 2D physics engine using BEJSON 104.\\n    \\\"\\\"\\\"\\n    def __init__(self, world_name=\\\"PhysicsWorld\\\"):\\n        self.bejson = {\\n            \\\"Format\\\": \\\"BEJSON\\\",\\n            \\\"Format_Creator\\\": \\\"Elton Boehnen\\\",\\n            \\\"Format_Version\\\": \\\"104\\\",\\n            \\\"Records_Type\\\": [\\\"Body\\\"],\\n            \\\"Fields\\\": [\\n                { \\\"name\\\": \\\"id\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"string\\\" },\\n                { \\\"name\\\": \\\"x\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"number\\\" },\\n                { \\\"name\\\": \\\"y\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"number\\\" },\\n                { \\\"name\\\": \\\"width\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"number\\\" },\\n                { \\\"name\\\": \\\"height\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"number\\\" },\\n                { \\\"name\\\": \\\"vx\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"number\\\" },\\n                { \\\"name\\\": \\\"vy\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"number\\\" },\\n                { \\\"name\\\": \\\"mass\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"number\\\" },\\n                { \\\"name\\\": \\\"isStatic\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"boolean\\\" },\\n                { \\\"name\\\": \\\"groups\\\", \\\"type\\\": \\\"array\\\" }\\n            ],\\n            \\\"Values\\\": []\\n        }\\n        self.gravity = {\\\"x\\\": 0, \\\"y\\\": 9.8}\\n        self.friction = 0.9  # Parity with bejson_physics.ts\\n        self.restitution = 0.8 # Energy loss on collision\\n\\n    def add_body(self, body_id, x, y, width, height, **options):\\n        vx = options.get(\\\"vx\\\", 0)\\n        vy = options.get(\\\"vy\\\", 0)\\n        mass = options.get(\\\"mass\\\", 1)\\n        is_static = options.get(\\\"isStatic\\\", False)\\n        groups = options.get(\\\"groups\\\", [\\\"default\\\"])\\n        \\n        self.bejson[\\\"Values\\\"].append([\\n            body_id, x, y, width, height, vx, vy, mass, is_static, groups\\n        ])\\n\\n    def step(self, dt):\\n        values = self.bejson[\\\"Values\\\"]\\n        # 1. Integration\\n        for row in values:\\n            if row[8]: # isStatic\\n                continue\\n            \\n            # Apply friction to current velocity\\n            row[5] *= self.friction\\n            row[6] *= self.friction\\n            \\n            # Apply gravity\\n            row[5] += self.gravity[\\\"x\\\"] * dt # vx\\n            row[6] += self.gravity[\\\"y\\\"] * dt # vy\\n            \\n            # Apply velocity to position\\n            row[1] += row[5] * dt # x\\n            row[2] += row[6] * dt # y\\n\\n        # 2. Collision Resolution\\n        for i in range(len(values)):\\n            body_a = values[i]\\n            for j in range(i + 1, len(values)):\\n                body_b = values[j]\\n                if self._check_aabb(body_a, body_b):\\n                    self._resolve_collision(body_a, body_b)\\n\\n    def _check_aabb(self, a, b):\\n        return (a[1] < b[1] + b[3] and \\n                a[1] + a[3] > b[1] and \\n                a[2] < b[2] + b[4] and \\n                a[2] + a[4] > b[2])\\n\\n    def _resolve_collision(self, a, b):\\n        if a[8] and b[8]: return\\n        \\n        # Advanced Resolution: Elastic collision with restitution\\n        # For dynamic-static or dynamic-dynamic\\n        if a[8]: # a is static\\n            b[5] *= -self.restitution\\n            b[6] *= -self.restitution\\n        elif b[8]: # b is static\\n            a[5] *= -self.restitution\\n            a[6] *= -self.restitution\\n        else:\\n            # Simple momentum exchange for dynamic-dynamic\\n            m_a, m_b = a[7], b[7]\\n            temp_vx_a, temp_vy_a = a[5], a[6]\\n            \\n            # v' = (v1(m1-m2) + 2*m2*v2) / (m1+m2)\\n            a[5] = (temp_vx_a * (m_a - m_b) + 2 * m_b * b[5]) / (m_a + m_b) * self.restitution\\n            a[6] = (temp_vy_a * (m_a - m_b) + 2 * m_b * b[6]) / (m_a + m_b) * self.restitution\\n            \\n            b[5] = (b[5] * (m_b - m_a) + 2 * m_a * temp_vx_a) / (m_a + m_b) * self.restitution\\n            b[6] = (b[6] * (m_b - m_a) + 2 * m_a * temp_vy_a) / (m_a + m_b) * self.restitution\\n\\n    def export_bejson(self):\\n        import json\\n        return json.dumps(self.bejson, indent=2)\\n\",\n      false\n    ]\n  ]\n}",
